Table mine.blast_pattern {
  blast_pattern_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for blast_pattern']
  adjacent_blast_pattern_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on blast_pattern (adjacent_blast_pattern_id)']
  bench_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.bench. Business justification: A blast pattern is designed for a specific bench, defining the drill hole geometry, spacing, and explosive parameters appropriate for that bench\'s rock characteristics and height. This FK links the bl']
  geological_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geological rock type or lithology for which this blast pattern is optimized (e.g., iron ore, overburden, coal, hard rock).']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where this blast pattern is designed for use.']
  pit_or_level_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific open-cut pit or mining area where this blast pattern is applied.']
  primary_explosive_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: Blast patterns specify the primary explosive product type required. Linking to material_master ensures the correct explosive product code is used when raising purchase orders and goods issues, support']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Blast pattern designs require formal risk assessments covering flyrock, airblast overpressure, ground vibration, and proximity to infrastructure. Drill and blast engineers reference the governing risk']
  application_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing operational guidance, constraints, or special instructions for applying this blast pattern in the field.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the blast pattern was formally approved for operational use.']
  bench_height_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Vertical height of the bench face in meters for which this blast pattern is designed. Critical parameter for calculating explosive load and fragmentation outcomes.']
  blast_pattern_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the blast pattern. Active patterns are approved for operational use; draft patterns are under design; retired patterns are no longer used but retained for historical reference.']
  burden_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Perpendicular distance in meters from the blast hole to the free face. Primary design parameter controlling rock movement and fragmentation.']
  charge_length_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total length of the explosive column in meters within the blast hole.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this blast pattern record was first created in the system.']
  delay_timing_ms varchar [note: 'Time delay in milliseconds between detonation of adjacent blast holes, used to control vibration, fragmentation, and muck pile throw.']
  design_date date [note: 'Date when the blast pattern was originally designed.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this blast pattern becomes valid for operational use.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this blast pattern remains valid. Nullable for patterns with indefinite validity.']
  explosive_mass_per_hole_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total mass of explosive loaded into each blast hole in kilograms.']
  fragmentation_target_p80_mm decimal(6,1) [note: 'Target fragmentation size in millimeters where 80% of the blasted material passes through (P80 metric). Drives crusher feed requirements and downstream processing efficiency.']
  hole_depth_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total depth of the blast hole in meters, including sub-drill below the bench floor to ensure complete breakage.']
  hole_diameter_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Diameter of the blast holes in millimeters. Determines explosive charge capacity and influences fragmentation and powder factor.']
  initiation_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of detonation initiation system used in the blast pattern. Electronic systems offer precise timing; non-electric and shock tube systems are cost-effective alternatives.']
  is_standard_pattern boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a standard, repeatable blast pattern (true) or a custom, one-off design (false).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this blast pattern record was last updated in the system.']
  number_of_holes varchar [note: 'Total count of blast holes in the pattern design.']
  number_of_rows varchar [note: 'Number of rows of blast holes in the pattern, measured perpendicular to the free face.']
  pattern_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code identifying the blast pattern design, used in operational communications and drill-and-blast planning systems.']
  pattern_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the blast pattern, typically describing the geometry or application context.']
  pattern_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the blast pattern geometry. Square and rectangular patterns are common in open-cut operations; staggered patterns optimize energy distribution; echelon patterns suit angled benches; radial patterns are used in underground or specialized applications.']
  powder_factor_kg_per_m3 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Mass of explosive in kilograms required to break one cubic meter of rock. Key performance indicator for blast efficiency and cost control.']
  rock_strength_mpa decimal(7,2) [note: 'Unconfined compressive strength of the target rock in megapascals. Influences explosive selection and powder factor requirements.']
  safety_exclusion_zone_m decimal(6,1) [note: 'Minimum safe distance in meters from the blast area within which all personnel and equipment must be evacuated prior to detonation.']
  spacing_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Distance in meters between adjacent blast holes in the same row. Typically 1.0 to 1.5 times the burden distance.']
  stemming_length_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Length of inert material (typically crushed rock or drill cuttings) placed at the top of the blast hole in meters to confine explosive gases and prevent premature venting.']
  sub_drill_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional drilling depth below the bench floor in meters to ensure complete rock breakage at the toe of the blast.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the blast pattern design, incremented when modifications are made to an existing pattern.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for blast_pattern. Referenced by blast_pattern_id.'
}

Table mine.pit_or_level {
  pit_or_level_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pit_or_level']
  area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mine_area. Business justification: A pit or underground level is located within a mine area. This FK establishes the spatial hierarchy: mine_site → mine_area → pit_or_level → bench, which is the standard operational hierarchy in mining']
  capex_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_budget. Business justification: Pit and level development is capital expenditure (pre-strip, decline development). Each pit or level has an approved CAPEX budget; finance tracks development spend vs budget per pit/level — a standard']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Pit/level-level production reporting, reserve classification, and scheduling require commodity linkage. \'primary_commodity\' plain attribute is a denormalized reference to product.commodity; replaced b']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Pits and underground levels are managed as cost centres for operational cost tracking. Mine controllers track mining costs (drilling, blasting, haulage) by pit or level via cost centres — a fundamenta']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Each pit and underground level requires a specific emergency response plan (evacuation routes, refuge chambers, assembly points). Mining safety regulations mandate level-specific ERPs. Mine rescue coo']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Pits and underground levels are physical locations in the mine hierarchy that correspond to functional location nodes in the maintenance system. PM schedules for pit infrastructure (pumps, ventilation']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.lom_plan. Business justification: A pit or level is planned and scheduled within a Life of Mine plan. This FK links the operational pit/level entity to the governing LOM plan, enabling LOM plan-level aggregation of pit/level performan']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent mine site where this pit or level is located.']
  parent_pit_or_level_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pit_or_level (parent_pit_or_level_id)']
  rehabilitation_provision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved environmental rehabilitation plan for this pit or level upon closure.']
  surface_right_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.surface_right. Business justification: Operational pits require surface rights over the disturbed land. The pit/level operational record must reference the surface right to support land access compliance, compensation payment tracking, and']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Pits and underground levels are physical structures that sit within a tenement. Statutory reporting, tenement expenditure compliance, and regulatory authority submissions require attributing productio']
  access_ramp_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary access ramp or decline providing haulage access to this pit or level.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when mining operations were completed at this pit or level. Used for historical analysis and future planning accuracy.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when mining operations commenced at this pit or level. Used for schedule variance analysis and performance tracking.']
  average_ore_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average grade of ore at this pit or level, expressed as percentage of target mineral content. Business-sensitive data used for mine sequencing and blending strategies.']
  bench_height_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Vertical height of the mining bench at this level, measured in meters. Standard bench heights are critical for blast design and equipment selection.']
  blast_pattern_code varchar [note: 'Standard blast design pattern code used at this pit or level. References drill spacing, burden, and explosive configuration for this geological domain.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pit or level record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  cutoff_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Minimum ore grade threshold for economic extraction at this pit or level, expressed as percentage. Material below this grade is classified as waste.']
  depth_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vertical depth of the pit or level from surface or reference datum, measured in meters. Used for geotechnical analysis and ventilation planning.']
  design_capacity_tonnes_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned daily production capacity for this pit or level, measured in tonnes per day. Used for production scheduling and resource allocation.']
  dilution_factor_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Expected percentage of waste material mixed with ore during extraction at this pit or level. Used for grade reconciliation and mill feed planning.']
  elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the pit floor or level datum above sea level, measured in meters. Critical for mine planning, haulage optimization, and surveying.']
  haulage_distance_km decimal(8,3) [note: 'Average haulage distance from this pit or level to the primary destination (ROM pad, crusher, or waste dump), measured in kilometers. Used for cycle time and cost modeling.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this pit or level record is currently active and valid for operational use. True indicates active, False indicates inactive or archived.']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'The extraction method employed at this pit or level: open-cut/open-pit for surface mining, underground methods including block cave, sublevel stoping, longwall, or room and pillar.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, or additional context about this pit or level.']
  ore_reserve_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated economically recoverable ore reserves at this pit or level, measured in tonnes. Business-sensitive reserve data used for Life of Mine (LOM) planning.']
  pit_or_level_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the pit or level, used in operational systems and reporting. Externally-known unique code.']
  pit_or_level_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the pit or level for identification and communication purposes.']
  pit_or_level_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pit or level: planned (future), design (engineering phase), development (construction), active (in production), depleted (ore exhausted), suspended (temporarily halted), closed (operations ceased), or rehabilitated (environmental restoration complete).']
  pit_or_level_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the mining excavation structure: pit for open-cut operations, level for underground operations, bench for stepped excavation, stage for phased development, cutback for pit expansion, or pushback for sequential mining phases.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for completion of mining operations at this pit or level. Used for Life of Mine (LOM) planning and closure planning.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for commencement of mining operations at this pit or level. Used for production scheduling and resource planning.']
  recovery_factor_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Expected percentage of in-situ ore that will be successfully extracted and recovered from this pit or level. Accounts for mining losses and operational constraints.']
  slope_angle_degrees decimal(5,2) [note: 'Design slope angle for pit walls or level access at this location, measured in degrees from horizontal. Critical for geotechnical stability and safety.']
  strip_ratio decimal(8,3) [note: 'Ratio of waste to ore for this pit or level, calculated as waste volume divided by ore volume. Key economic indicator for open-cut mining operations.']
  surveyed_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent survey measurement for this pit or level. Used for as-built reconciliation and spatial accuracy tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pit or level record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  ventilation_requirement_m3_per_sec decimal(10,2) [note: 'Required airflow rate for underground levels, measured in cubic meters per second. Critical for occupational health and safety compliance.']
  waste_volume_bcm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated volume of waste material to be removed from this pit or level, measured in bank cubic meters. Critical for strip ratio calculation and waste dump planning.']
  water_inflow_rate_liters_per_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average groundwater inflow rate at this pit or level, measured in liters per minute. Used for dewatering system design and water management planning.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pit_or_level. Referenced by pit_or_level_id.'
}

Table mine.bench {
  bench_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bench']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Benches are physical mine locations where drilling, blasting, and loading occur. Maintenance activities including berm maintenance, road grading, and drainage are scheduled against benches as function']
  heritage_clearance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.heritage_clearance. Business justification: Individual benches require heritage clearance before drilling and blasting can proceed. The bench record must reference the heritage clearance covering that bench area to support pre-mining compliance']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.lom_plan. Business justification: A bench is planned and scheduled within a Life of Mine plan. This FK links the bench entity to the governing LOM plan, enabling LOM plan-level aggregation of bench-level production targets and actuals']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site where this bench is located.']
  parent_bench_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bench (parent_bench_id)']
  pit_or_level_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pit or open-cut mine where this bench is located.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Benches are physical mining units within a tenement boundary. Annual statutory mining returns and regulatory compliance reporting require knowing which tenement each bench sits within. A mine surveyor']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when mining operations were completed on this bench.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when mining operations commenced on this bench.']
  bench_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the bench used in mine planning and operational systems.']
  bench_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the bench.']
  bench_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the bench in its lifecycle: planned for future extraction, actively being mined, depleted of material, temporarily suspended, or permanently closed.']
  bench_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bench based on material type: ore-bearing, waste rock, mixed material, overburden, or interburden.']
  berm_width_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width of the safety berm or catch bench in meters, designed to catch falling material and provide access.']
  blast_pattern_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standard drill-and-blast pattern design used for this bench.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bench record was first created in the system.']
  design_capacity_bcm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned total volume of material to be extracted from the bench in bank cubic meters (in-situ volume).']
  drill_hole_burden_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard distance from the drill hole to the free face (burden) in the blast pattern, measured in meters.']
  drill_hole_diameter_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard diameter of blast holes drilled in the bench, measured in millimeters.']
  drill_hole_spacing_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard horizontal spacing between drill holes in the blast pattern, measured in meters.']
  elevation_rl_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'The reduced level elevation of the bench floor in meters above mean sea level, used for vertical positioning in mine design.']
  face_angle_degrees decimal(5,2) [note: 'Angle of the bench face in degrees from horizontal, critical for geotechnical stability and safety.']
  geotechnical_domain varchar [note: 'Geotechnical classification or domain code for the bench, indicating rock mass characteristics and stability parameters.']
  height_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Vertical height of the bench face in meters, typically standardized within a pit design.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the bench is currently active in production operations.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Primary geological material type present in the bench (e.g., iron ore, copper ore, coal, limestone, sandstone).']
  mining_method varchar [note: 'Primary extraction method used for the bench: drill-and-blast, rip-and-load (dozer ripping), direct loading, or selective mining.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, comments, or special instructions related to the bench.']
  ore_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average ore grade percentage for the bench, representing the concentration of valuable mineral content.']
  ore_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total tonnage of ore material within the bench.']
  phase_code varchar [note: 'Mine planning phase code indicating which development phase or pushback this bench belongs to.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when mining operations are planned to be completed on this bench, per the Life of Mine (LOM) plan.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when mining operations are planned to commence on this bench, per the Life of Mine (LOM) plan.']
  remaining_volume_bcm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current remaining volume of material available for extraction from the bench in bank cubic meters.']
  rock_mass_rating varchar [note: 'Rock Mass Rating score for the bench, a geotechnical classification system indicating rock quality and stability.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Mining sequence order number for the bench within the pit development plan, indicating the planned extraction order.']
  strip_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of waste tonnes to ore tonnes for the bench, a key economic indicator for mining operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bench record was last modified in the system.']
  waste_tonnes decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total tonnage of waste material within the bench.']
  width_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Horizontal width of the bench working area in meters, measured from crest to toe.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for bench. Referenced by bench_id.'
}

Table processing.plant {
  plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the mineral processing plant facility. Primary key for the processing_plant master register. SSOT for all processing facility master data.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Processing plants are designed and optimized for specific commodities (iron ore, copper, coal). Essential for production planning, capacity allocation, JORC resource reporting, and LOM planning. Domai']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Every processing plant operates under a cost centre for opex tracking, AISC reporting, and variance analysis. processing_plant.opex_cost_centre (plain attr) is denormalized cost_centre_code; replace w']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Processing plants must maintain site-specific emergency response plans (fire, chemical spill, structural failure) under mining safety legislation. A mining HSE manager expects plant.emergency_response']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: Processing plants operate under environmental permits governing air emissions, water discharge, and tailings management. Regulatory compliance tracking requires direct linkage between plant operations']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Plants send routine process control samples (shift composites, circuit optimization samples) to designated laboratories for analysis. Tracks primary lab relationship for turnaround management and meta']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mine site or operational location where this processing plant is physically situated. Links the plant to its geographic and operational context.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Plants are profit-generating entities assigned to profit centres in SAP/ERP for segment P&L reporting, AISC disclosure, and investor reporting. A mining finance expert would expect every plant to have']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Each processing plant is designed and operated to produce a specific saleable product (e.g., copper cathode, iron ore fines). Plant design parameters and production reporting are anchored to the salea']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Each plant is designed and operated to produce output meeting a specific product specification. Plant design parameters (nameplate_recovery_rate_pct, feed_grade_design_pct) are set against a specifica']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Processing plants operate under granted mining licences on specific tenements. Required for regulatory compliance reporting, expenditure commitment tracking, and operational boundary verification. Eve']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Plant capital expenditure is tracked via WBS elements for project accounting and asset capitalization. processing_plant.capex_wbs_element (plain attr) is denormalized WBS code; replace with FK for cap']
  aisc_benchmark_usd_per_t decimal(10,2) [note: 'The budgeted or benchmark All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) for processing at this plant expressed in USD per dry metric tonne of ore processed. Used as the financial performance baseline for C1 cash cost reporting and LOM plan economics.']
  annual_capacity_mt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Nameplate annual throughput capacity expressed in million dry metric tonnes per annum (Mtpa). Derived from design_capacity_tph and planned operating hours but stored as a master data attribute for LOM plan alignment and regulatory resource reporting.']
  care_maintenance_date date [note: 'The date on which the plant was placed into care-and-maintenance status. Null if the plant has never been placed on care-and-maintenance. Used for OPEX tracking, insurance, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  comminution_technology varchar [note: 'Primary size-reduction (comminution) technology installed in the plant. SAG (Semi-Autogenous Grinding), HPGR (High Pressure Grinding Rolls), ball mill, rod mill, crusher, AG (Autogenous Grinding), or combined circuit. Drives energy consumption benchmarking and maintenance planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAG|HPGR|ball_mill|rod_mill|crusher|AG|combined — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commissioned_date date [note: 'The date on which the processing plant was formally commissioned and accepted into commercial production. Used for asset depreciation calculations (IFRS 16), LOM plan baseline, and regulatory reporting of production commencement.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the processing plant is located (e.g., \'AUS\', \'CHL\', \'ZAF\', \'CAN\'). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (JORC, NI 43-101, SAMREC, MSHA) and environmental reporting obligations.']
  decommissioned_date date [note: 'The date on which the plant was permanently decommissioned and removed from the active asset register. Null if the plant is still operational or on care-and-maintenance. Triggers mine rehabilitation and closure obligations.']
  design_capacity_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate design throughput capacity of the plant expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph) of Run of Mine (ROM) feed. Represents the engineering-rated maximum throughput under optimal conditions. Used as the denominator for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculations.']
  elevation_masl decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation of the processing plant site in metres above sea level (mASL). Relevant for water balance modelling, slurry pipeline hydraulics design, and environmental impact assessments.']
  feed_grade_design_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Design ROM feed head grade (percentage of valuable mineral in the feed ore) for which the plant was engineered. Used as the baseline for metallurgical balance calculations, cut-off grade assessments, and LOM plan modelling.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the processing plant centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS (Geographic Information System) mapping, spatial analysis, and environmental impact zone delineation.']
  lims_plant_code varchar [note: 'The plant identifier used in the LabWare LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) for sample registration, assay result linkage, and metallurgical accounting. Enables traceability from plant feed to product assay.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the processing plant centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS mapping, spatial analysis, and environmental impact zone delineation.']
  nameplate_recovery_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Design-rated metallurgical recovery rate expressed as a percentage (0–100). Represents the percentage of the target valuable mineral in the feed that is expected to report to the concentrate or product stream under nameplate conditions. Key input to LOM plan financial modelling and JORC/NI 43-101 resource estimation.']
  number_of_processing_lines varchar [note: 'Total count of parallel processing lines (circuits) within the plant. Each line represents an independent processing train capable of treating ore from feed to product. Drives capacity planning, maintenance scheduling, and redundancy analysis.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the processing plant. \'active\' = fully operational; \'care-and-maintenance\' = temporarily idle but preserved; \'decommissioned\' = permanently closed; \'commissioning\' = in start-up phase; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted due to regulatory or operational reasons.']
  ore_type_compatibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ore/commodity types this plant is designed and certified to process (e.g., \'iron ore, copper, gold, lithium, nickel, coal, diamonds\'). Drives feed stream routing and LOM plan alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron ore|copper|gold|lithium|nickel|coal|diamonds|zinc|lead|manganese — promote to reference product]']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the processing plant, used across ERP, SCADA, and FMS systems (e.g., \'PP-PILBARA-01\', \'PROC-CU-02\'). Unique across the enterprise and referenced in operational reporting.']
  plant_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the processing plant including key design features, processing circuit configuration, historical context, and any notable operational characteristics. Supports regulatory submissions, investor reports, and technical documentation.']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the mineral processing plant facility (e.g., \'Pilbara Iron Ore Processing Plant 1\', \'Olympic Dam Copper Concentrator\'). Used in reports, dashboards, and regulatory submissions.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the processing plant by metallurgical process family. \'concentrator\' = physical separation to produce mineral concentrate; \'hydrometallurgical\' = chemical leaching/SX-EW; \'pyrometallurgical\' = smelting/roasting; \'beneficiation\' = upgrading without full extraction; \'refinery\' = final product refining; \'pelletising\' = iron ore pellet production.']
  power_consumption_design_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate design electrical power consumption in kilowatts (kW) at full throughput. Used for energy cost modelling (OPEX), carbon footprint reporting (GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement), and grid capacity planning.']
  primary_processing_method varchar [note: 'The principal mineral processing and beneficiation method employed by this plant. Comminution includes SAG/HPGR milling; flotation covers froth flotation separation; leaching includes heap and tank leaching; SX-EW (Solvent Extraction Electrowinning) for copper cathode production; DMS (Dense Media Separation) for coal/diamonds; gravity and magnetic separation for iron ore. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comminution|flotation|leaching|SX-EW|DMS|gravity|magnetic — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this processing plant master record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and regulatory record-keeping obligations.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this processing plant master record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  sap_functional_location varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) functional location code assigned to this processing plant. Enables linkage to maintenance work orders, preventive maintenance plans, and CAPEX/OPEX cost centre reporting in SAP.']
  scada_system_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system instance or node that monitors and controls this processing plant. Used to link real-time process historian data (OSIsoft PI System) to the plant master record.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or territory in which the processing plant is located (e.g., \'Western Australia\', \'British Columbia\', \'Antofagasta\'). Used for jurisdictional regulatory compliance, environmental permitting, and geographic reporting.']
  tailings_generation_design_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate design rate of tailings (gangue and process residue) generated by the plant in dry metric tonnes per hour. Drives TSF (Tailings Storage Facility) capacity planning, environmental licence conditions, and ICMM tailings governance compliance.']
  water_consumption_design_m3ph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nameplate design water consumption rate in cubic metres per hour (m³/h) for the plant under full throughput conditions. Critical for water balance management, environmental licence compliance, and EIS (Environmental Impact Statement) reporting.']

  Note: 'Master register of all mineral processing plant facilities. Captures plant identity, site location, design capacity (tph), commissioned date, primary processing method (comminution, flotation, leaching, SX-EW, DMS, gravity, magnetic), ore type compatibility (iron ore, copper, gold, lithium, nickel, coal, diamonds), operational status (active, care-and-maintenance, decommissioned), number of processing lines, and nameplate recovery rate. SSOT for all processing facility master data. Parent entity for circuits, feed streams, and all operational records.'
}

Table processing.circuit {
  circuit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a processing circuit record within the mineral processing plant. Primary key for the processing_circuit data product.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Circuits are engineered for specific commodities with tailored flowsheets and reagent schemes. Critical for metallurgical accounting, recovery optimization, and circuit performance reporting. Replaces']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Individual processing circuits (rougher, cleaner, leach) are discrete cost objects in mining operations. Circuit-level cost centre assignment enables granular AISC/C1 cost reporting and variance analy']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Specific circuits (cyanide leach, acid circuits) require dedicated emergency response plans under ICMC and national chemical safety regulations, distinct from the plant-level plan. Mining HSE managers']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Circuits are maintainable functional locations within plant hierarchy. Maintenance strategies, PM schedules, and work orders are assigned to circuit-level functional locations for granular maintenance']
  geological_domain_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Processing circuits are designed and configured for specific geological domains — oxide circuits differ fundamentally from sulphide circuits in reagent_scheme_code, design_ph_target, and target_recove']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Each processing circuit is assigned a primary laboratory for routine process control assays (head grade, concentrate grade, tailings grade). Metallurgical accounting and circuit performance reporting ']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan under which this circuit\'s design parameters and recovery targets were established. Enables traceability of circuit design basis to the governing mine plan.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant in which this circuit operates. Links circuit-level data to plant-level operational context.']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Each processing circuit is configured to produce a specific saleable product. Circuit performance reporting, reagent schemes, and design parameters are set based on the saleable product requirements. ']
  sample_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.sample_program. Business justification: Each processing circuit operates under a defined sample program specifying sampling points, frequency, and analytical suite for metallurgical accounting and process control. This is a fundamental oper']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Each circuit is designed to produce output meeting a specific product specification. Circuit design parameters (target_product_grade_pct, target_recovery_rate_pct) are derived from the specification. ']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Processing circuit design specifies which TSF receives its tailings output — a fundamental environmental permit and water balance requirement. Metallurgical engineers and environmental teams must know']
  upstream_circuit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on circuit (upstream_circuit_id)']
  circuit_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the processing circuit within the plant flowsheet (e.g., PC-SAG-01, FL-RGH-02). Used in P&ID documentation, SCADA tags, and operational reporting.']
  circuit_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the processing circuit including its role in the plant flowsheet, key equipment, and any notable design features or operational constraints. Supports engineering documentation and onboarding.']
  circuit_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the processing circuit (e.g., Primary SAG Mill Circuit, Rougher Flotation Circuit, Leach Circuit 1). Used in operational dashboards and reporting.']
  circuit_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the processing circuit. Drives scheduling, maintenance planning, and production reporting inclusion/exclusion.']
  circuit_type varchar [note: 'Category of mineral processing technology employed in this circuit. Drives applicable KPIs, reagent profiles, and recovery benchmarks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comminution|flotation|leaching|gravity_separation|dense_media_separation|sx_ew|thickening|filtration|classification — promote to reference product]']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the processing circuit was formally commissioned and accepted into operational service. Used for asset age calculations, warranty tracking, and depreciation under IFRS.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the processing circuit was or is planned to be permanently decommissioned. Nullable for active circuits. Used for mine closure planning, asset write-off scheduling, and rehabilitation cost provisioning.']
  design_availability_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Design basis mechanical availability of the circuit expressed as a percentage of scheduled operating hours. Used as the denominator in OEE calculations and production planning. Derived from MTBF and MTTR estimates at design stage.']
  design_feed_size_p80_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Design basis P80 particle size of the feed material entering this circuit in millimetres. P80 represents the 80th percentile passing size. Critical for comminution circuit design and mill sizing.']
  design_ph_target decimal(4,2) [note: 'Design basis target pH value for the circuit process stream. Applicable to flotation (pH control for selectivity), leaching (acid/alkaline conditions), and SX-EW circuits. Drives lime/acid reagent consumption.']
  design_product_size_p80_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'Design basis P80 particle size of the product leaving this circuit in millimetres. Defines the target grind size for comminution circuits and classification efficiency for downstream separation circuits.']
  design_residence_time_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Design basis residence time of material within the circuit expressed in minutes. Determines tank/vessel sizing and is a key parameter for leach kinetics, flotation recovery, and thickener design.']
  design_slurry_density_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Design basis slurry density expressed as percentage solids by weight for circuits handling slurry streams. Critical for pump sizing, pipe design, and process control setpoints.']
  design_throughput_mtpa decimal(12,3) [note: 'Annualised nameplate design throughput capacity expressed in million dry metric tonnes per annum (Mtpa). Used for LOM plan alignment, capital project justification, and annual production reporting.']
  design_throughput_tph decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate design throughput capacity of the circuit expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph). Represents the engineering design basis used for LOM plan benchmarking and OEE calculations.']
  design_utilisation_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Design basis utilisation rate of the circuit expressed as a percentage of available hours actually used for production. Combined with availability to derive OEE. Accounts for planned feed interruptions and scheduled downtime.']
  design_water_consumption_m3pt decimal(8,4) [note: 'Design basis fresh water consumption rate for this circuit expressed in cubic metres per dry metric tonne of feed processed. Used for water balance modelling, environmental licence compliance, and OPEX planning.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the current circuit configuration, design parameters, and targets are effective. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) management and historical performance benchmarking against the correct design basis.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which the current circuit configuration and design parameters are effective. Null for the current active version. Enables historical tracking of circuit design changes and target revisions over the LOM.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the environmental operating permit or licence governing this circuit\'s operations (e.g., water use licence, air emission permit). Required for regulatory compliance reporting under ISO 14001 and EIS obligations.']
  feed_material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary feed material entering this circuit (e.g., ROM Ore for primary comminution, slurry for flotation). Determines applicable feed quality parameters and circuit design basis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rom_ore|crushed_ore|ground_ore|concentrate|slurry|leach_solution|tailings|recycled_stream — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  flowsheet_sequence_order varchar [note: 'Integer position of this circuit within the overall plant processing flowsheet. Defines the order of material flow from feed preparation through to final product, enabling flowsheet visualisation and mass balance calculations.']
  installed_power_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total installed electrical power capacity of the circuit in kilowatts (kW). Used for energy consumption benchmarking, OPEX modelling, and specific energy intensity calculations (kWh/t).']
  is_primary_circuit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this circuit is the primary (first-pass) processing circuit in the plant flowsheet as opposed to a secondary, scavenger, or retreatment circuit. Used for production reporting hierarchy and mass balance prioritisation.']
  number_of_units varchar [note: 'Count of parallel processing units (e.g., mills, flotation cells, leach tanks) operating within this circuit. Used for availability calculations, maintenance scheduling, and throughput modelling.']
  pi_af_element_path varchar [note: 'Full Asset Framework (AF) element path in the OSIsoft PI System for this processing circuit (e.g., \\Server\Plant\MillCircuit\SAG01). Enables direct linkage between the data product and real-time process historian data.']
  reagent_scheme_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standard reagent addition scheme applied in this circuit (e.g., collector, frother, pH modifier for flotation; acid type for leaching). Links to reagent consumption planning and OPEX budgeting.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this processing circuit record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this processing circuit record was last modified. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the process engineer accountable for the design, optimisation, and performance of this circuit. Used for accountability tracking and escalation routing.']
  scada_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'Root tag prefix used in the SCADA/DCS system (OSIsoft PI) to identify all process data points associated with this circuit (e.g., PLANT1.SAG01). Enables automated data ingestion from the PI historian into the lakehouse.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Specific processing sub-type within the circuit type category (e.g., SAG Mill under comminution, Rougher under flotation, CIL under leaching). Enables granular performance benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sag_mill|ball_mill|hpgr|rod_mill|rougher|scavenger|cleaner|recleaner|heap_leach|vat_leach|cil|cip|dms|jig|spiral|electrowinning|solvent_extraction — promote to reference product]']
  target_feed_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'LOM plan target head grade of the feed material entering this circuit expressed as a percentage of the primary commodity. Used for cut-off grade optimisation and circuit performance benchmarking.']
  target_recovery_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'LOM plan target metallurgical recovery rate for the primary commodity expressed as a percentage (e.g., 87.500 for 87.5%). Represents the expected proportion of valuable mineral extracted relative to feed grade. Basis for reserve reporting under JORC/CIM.']

  Note: 'Defines individual processing circuits within a plant (e.g., primary comminution, secondary grinding, rougher flotation, cleaner flotation, leach circuit, SX-EW circuit). Captures circuit type, feed material, design throughput, target recovery rate, and sequence order within the processing flowsheet. Enables circuit-level performance tracking.'
}

Table processing.feed_stream {
  feed_stream_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each ore feed stream record entering the processing plant. Primary key for the feed_stream data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the primary conveyor or feeder equipment delivering material to this feed stream. Links to the equipment asset register for maintenance scheduling and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) tracking.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG/Ball Mill circuit, DMS circuit, flotation circuit) that this feed stream feeds into. Enables circuit-level mass balance and throughput analysis.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Feed streams carry specific commodities from mine to plant. Fundamental for grade control, blending decisions, and feed scheduling. Replaces denormalized commodity text field with proper FK for refere']
  geological_domain_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Feed blend optimisation and metallurgical prediction require knowing which geological domain material is being fed. Metallurgists set head_grade_pct, processing_route, and ore_type targets per geologi']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the LOM (Life of Mine) plan version that defines the target feed parameters for this stream. Enables variance analysis between planned and actual feed characteristics for production reconciliation.']
  ore_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.ore_reserve. Business justification: Feed stream design parameters (head grade, cut-off grade, ore type, throughput) are directly derived from the ore reserve classification. LOM feed scheduling and mill feed blend optimisation require t']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Feed streams originate from specific orebodies. Mine-to-mill optimization and metallurgical reconciliation require tracking which orebody supplies each feed stream to correlate geological characterist']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the mineral processing plant or facility that this feed stream supplies. Supports multi-plant operations and mass balance reporting at plant level.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Feed streams must reference production schedules to track which mine production periods feed which processing campaigns. Essential for ore type scheduling, blend optimization, and integrated mine-mill']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Feed streams originate from specific prospects/orebodies. Critical for mine-to-mill optimization, ore tracking, metallurgical reconciliation, and LOM planning. Real business need: tracking ore source ']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the specific physical source location (ROM pad, stockpile, crusher station) from which this feed stream originates. Links to the plant location or stockpile master.']
  sample_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.sample_program. Business justification: Feed streams are sampled under a defined sample program governing sampling protocol, frequency, and analytical suite for metallurgical accounting. Knowing which sample program applies to each feed str']
  source_tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Feed streams originate from ore extracted from specific tenements. Essential for royalty calculations, grade reconciliation against tenement reserves, and compliance with tenement-specific production ']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Feed streams are configured to meet a target product specification (grade, particle size, moisture). Process engineers reference the specification when setting feed stream design parameters. Metallurg']
  abrasion_index decimal(6,3) [note: 'Bond Abrasion Index (Ai) of the ore in the feed stream, a dimensionless measure of ore abrasiveness. Drives liner and media wear rate predictions for SAG mills, ball mills, and HPGR (High Pressure Grinding Rolls), informing maintenance scheduling and OPEX forecasting.']
  bond_work_index_kwh_t decimal(7,3) [note: 'Bond Ball Mill Work Index (BWi) of the ore expressed in kilowatt-hours per tonne (kWh/t). Measures ore hardness and grindability; used to predict mill power requirements, throughput, and energy consumption in comminution circuit design and optimisation.']
  bulk_density_t_m3 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Bulk density of the ore material in the feed stream expressed in tonnes per cubic metre (t/m³). Used to convert volumetric flow measurements to mass flow for mass balance calculations and stockpile volume reconciliation.']
  commissioned_date date [note: 'The date on which this feed stream was formally commissioned and accepted into operational service. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, warranty management, and CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) project closure reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this feed stream record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data lineage and record provenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cut_off_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The minimum economic grade threshold (cut-off grade) applied to material entering this feed stream. Material below cut-off grade is classified as waste and diverted to the waste dump. Defined per LOM plan and commodity economics.']
  design_capacity_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum rated throughput capacity of the feed stream infrastructure (conveyor, feeder, chute) in dry metric tonnes per hour. Used for bottleneck analysis and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculations.']
  dilution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of waste material mixed with ore in the feed stream due to mining dilution. Dilution reduces the effective head grade and is tracked for reconciliation between geological resource model grades and actual plant feed grades.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this feed stream definition and its associated parameters (nominal rate, target grade, processing route) became operationally effective. Supports temporal versioning of feed stream configurations aligned to LOM plan revisions.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this feed stream definition ceases to be operationally effective. Null indicates the stream is currently active with no planned end date. Supports LOM plan stage transitions and plant reconfiguration tracking.']
  feed_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature of the ore or slurry in the feed stream measured in degrees Celsius. Relevant for leaching circuits (SX-EW), autoclave operations, and cold-climate operations where freezing affects conveyor and crusher performance.']
  gangue_content_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage of waste rock (gangue) minerals in the feed stream material. High gangue content increases dilution, reduces effective head grade, and impacts reagent consumption in flotation and leaching circuits.']
  head_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The assay-determined grade of the primary valuable mineral in the feed stream expressed as a percentage (e.g., % Fe for iron ore, % Cu for copper). Head grade is the key input for recovery rate calculations and production reconciliation against the LOM plan.']
  moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the ore in the feed stream expressed as a percentage of total wet mass. Moisture affects conveyor performance, crusher throughput, mill power draw, and dry tonne calculations for mass balance and invoicing.']
  nominal_feed_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'The design or nameplate feed rate for this stream expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph). Represents the target throughput capacity used in LOM (Life of Mine) plan scheduling and plant design. Distinct from actual measured feed rate.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Geological ore type classification of the material in this feed stream (e.g., oxide, sulphide, transitional, fresh, weathered). Ore type drives processing route selection, reagent dosing, and expected recovery rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oxide|sulphide|transitional|fresh|weathered|mixed|high_grade|low_grade — promote to reference product]']
  p50_particle_size_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'The 50th percentile (median) passing particle size of the feed stream material in millimetres. Complements P80 to characterise the full particle size distribution (PSD) shape, supporting comminution circuit modelling and screen efficiency analysis.']
  p80_particle_size_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'The 80th percentile passing particle size of the feed stream material expressed in millimetres (mm). P80 is the industry-standard metric for characterising particle size distribution (PSD) in comminution circuits. Critical input for SAG mill and HPGR (High Pressure Grinding Rolls) performance modelling.']
  pi_tag_feed_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System historian tag name for the real-time feed rate measurement instrument (belt scale or flow meter) associated with this feed stream. Enables direct linkage between the data product and the SCADA/PI process historian for real-time and historical data retrieval.']
  pi_tag_particle_size varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System historian tag name for the online particle size analyser (PSA) instrument monitoring P80 for this feed stream. Supports real-time comminution circuit control and automated variance alerting.']
  processing_route varchar [note: 'The designated mineral processing route for material in this feed stream (e.g., SAG/Ball Mill comminution, DMS (Dense Media Separation), flotation, leaching, SX-EW (Solvent Extraction Electrowinning)). Determines downstream circuit routing and reagent requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sag_ball_mill|hpgr|dms|flotation|leaching|sx_ew|gravity|magnetic_separation|cil|cip — promote to reference product]']
  slurry_density_t_m3 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Density of the ore-water slurry mixture in the feed stream expressed in tonnes per cubic metre. Applicable to wet processing circuits (SAG mill discharge, flotation feed). Monitored via OSIsoft PI System for real-time process control.']
  slurry_solids_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of solids by mass in the slurry feed stream (percent solids). A key process control parameter for wet grinding and flotation circuits; directly affects mill power draw, pump performance, and flotation cell residence time.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical origin point from which ore material enters this feed stream. ROM (Run of Mine) pad indicates direct truck tip; stockpile indicates reclaimed material; crusher indicates post-comminution feed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rom_pad|primary_crusher|secondary_crusher|tertiary_crusher|stockpile|reclaim_conveyor|direct_tip|sag_discharge — promote to reference product]']
  stream_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the feed stream within the processing plant configuration (e.g., FS-ROM-001, FS-CRU-002). Used in mass balance reconciliation and plant P&ID references.']
  stream_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name of the feed stream (e.g., \'Primary ROM Feed\', \'SAG Mill Discharge\', \'Crusher Product Recirculating Load\'). Used in operational dashboards and reporting.']
  stream_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feed stream definition within the processing plant. Active streams are currently receiving material; suspended streams are temporarily offline; decommissioned streams are permanently retired from the circuit.']
  stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the feed stream by its position and role in the comminution circuit. ROM (Run of Mine) feed is direct from the ROM pad; crushed feed is post-primary crusher; recirculating load is material returned for regrinding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rom_feed|crushed_feed|recirculating_load|classifier_overflow|classifier_underflow|screen_oversize|sag_discharge|ball_mill_discharge — promote to reference product]']
  target_feed_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned or scheduled feed rate for this stream in dry metric tonnes per hour as defined in the current production schedule or LOM plan. Distinct from nominal (design) feed rate; used for shift-level production planning and variance reporting.']
  target_head_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The planned head grade of the primary valuable mineral for this feed stream as per the current production schedule or LOM plan. Used for production reconciliation and variance analysis against actual assayed head grade.']
  target_p80_mm decimal(8,3) [note: 'The planned P80 particle size target for this feed stream in millimetres as specified in the processing plant design or LOM plan. Used to assess crusher and mill performance against design specifications.']
  top_size_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum lump size of material in the feed stream expressed in millimetres. Defines the upper bound of the particle size distribution and is critical for crusher gap settings, conveyor design, and mill liner protection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this feed stream record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Defines ore feed streams entering the processing plant including ROM feed, crushed feed, and recirculating loads. Captures stream identity, source (ROM pad, crusher, stockpile), nominal feed rate (tph), particle size distribution (P80), moisture content, and ore type classification. Supports mass balance and throughput tracking.'
}

Table processing.shift_production_run {
  shift_production_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shift production run record in the processing plant. Primary key for this transactional entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete operational shift event with a clear lifecycle in the mineral processing domain.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.circuit. Business justification: A shift production run is executed on a specific processing circuit within a plant. The circuit_id FK directly links the shift run to its circuit, enabling circuit-level performance aggregation. The e']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Shift-level production costs (labour, consumables, utilities) are allocated to cost centres for unit cost calculation, AISC reporting, and budget variance analysis. Core mining cost accounting process']
  geological_domain_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Shift reporting and metallurgical reconciliation require tracking which geological domain material was processed each shift.   laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Shift samples are dispatched to specific laboratories for analysis. Essential for tracking sample turnaround times, metallurgical accounting, and reconciling lab results with production data for perfo']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which this shift production run is being reconciled. Enables tracking of actual production performance versus the approved LOM plan targets for throughput, grade, and recovery.']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Production runs frequently trigger corrective maintenance work orders when equipment issues or process deviations are detected during shift operations. Links shift performance problems to maintenance ']
  metallurgical_balance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.metallurgical_balance. Business justification: Each shift production run contributes to a metallurgical accounting period. Linking shift_production_run to its metallurgical_balance period enables direct aggregation of shift-level production data i']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Shift production runs process ore from specific orebodies. Operational reporting, grade reconciliation, and metallurgical accounting require linking production shifts to source orebody to compare actu']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mineral processing plant where this shift production run occurred. Links to the plant master reference. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category (resource reference to the processing facility). Used for plant-level production aggregation and benchmarking.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Processing shift runs must align with mine production schedules for integrated mine-to-mill optimization, bottleneck analysis, and shift-level production reconciliation. Essential for real-time produc']
  rom_stockpile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.rom_stockpile. Business justification: Processing shift runs track feed source from ROM stockpiles for inventory management, feed scheduling, and stockpile depletion tracking. Essential for real-time feed management and stockpile inventory']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Shifts produce specific saleable products (concentrate grades, cathodes). Essential for production reconciliation, inventory management, and linking shift output to sales contracts. Natural business r']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.shift_report. Business justification: Daily mine-plant shift reconciliation requires aligning the mine shift report (ore mined, tonnes moved) with the processing shift production run (ore processed, recovery) for the same shift period. Mi']
  shutdown_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.shutdown_plan. Business justification: Shift production runs that fall within a planned shutdown window must be attributed to the shutdown plan for production-loss accounting and schedule compliance reporting. Mining operations teams expli']
  source_tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Production runs process ore from specific tenements. Required for royalty payment calculations, tenement-specific statutory production reporting, and reconciliation of tenement reserves against actual']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Each shift production run targets a specific product specification. Shift supervisors and metallurgists assess whether the run met grade, moisture, and impurity limits. This drives shift quality repor']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Every processing shift generates tailings sent to specific TSF. Required for material flow tracking, mass balance reconciliation, TSF capacity planning, and water balance closure. Fundamental operatio']
  actual_throughput_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual average processing rate achieved during the shift, expressed in dry tonnes per hour (tph). Calculated as tonnes processed divided by operating hours. Compared against design throughput rate for performance gap analysis.']
  calendar_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of hours in the shift period as defined by the site calendar (typically 12 hours for a standard shift). Used as the denominator for plant utilisation and OEE calculations. Expressed in decimal hours.']
  circuit_availability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of calendar hours during which the processing circuit was available for production, calculated as (calendar hours minus planned and unplanned downtime) divided by calendar hours, expressed as a percentage. Key KPI for processing plant performance against LOM plan targets.']
  concentrate_produced_tonnes decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry tonnes of mineral concentrate or final processed product produced during the shift. Used for production accounting, inventory management, and sales dispatch planning. Sourced from product weighbridge or belt scale measurements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift production run record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of data ingestion from OSIsoft PI System and MES. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  design_throughput_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nameplate or design throughput rate of the processing plant circuit, expressed in dry tonnes per hour (tph), as specified in the LOM plan and plant design documentation. Used as the benchmark for actual throughput performance assessment.']
  feed_blend_oxide_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of oxide ore type in the ROM feed blend during the shift. Part of the feed blend composition tracking used to predict and explain metallurgical recovery variations and circuit performance. Sourced from grade control block data and ROM stockpile management.']
  feed_blend_sulphide_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sulphide ore type in the ROM feed blend during the shift. Used alongside oxide percentage to characterise the feed blend composition for metallurgical performance analysis and reagent dosage optimisation.']
  feed_grade_element varchar [note: 'Chemical element symbol for which the feed head grade is reported (e.g., Fe for iron ore, Cu for copper, Li for lithium). Enables multi-commodity processing plants to correctly attribute grade measurements to the target mineral. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Fe|Cu|Au|Ag|Li|Ni|Co|Zn|Pb|Mo — promote to reference product]']
  feed_head_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Assayed head grade of the primary valuable element (e.g., Fe%, Cu%, Li%) in the ROM feed during the shift, expressed as a percentage. Sourced from LabWare LIMS composite sample assay results. Critical input for metallurgical recovery calculations and LOM plan reconciliation.']
  feed_moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the ROM feed expressed as a percentage of wet weight, measured by on-belt moisture analyser or laboratory sample. Used to convert wet tonnes to dry tonnes for metallurgical accounting and mass balance calculations.']
  metallurgical_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the valuable element in the ROM feed that is successfully recovered into the final concentrate or product during the shift. Calculated from feed grade, product grade, and mass balance. Core KPI for processing efficiency and LOM plan reconciliation.']
  oee_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall Equipment Effectiveness expressed as a percentage, combining availability, performance, and quality rate for the processing plant during the shift. Industry-standard KPI for processing plant productivity benchmarking against LOM plan targets. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System.']
  operating_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual number of hours the processing plant was in productive operation during the shift, excluding planned and unplanned downtime. Used in OEE calculation and throughput rate derivation. Expressed in decimal hours.']
  planned_downtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours of scheduled downtime during the shift for planned maintenance, inspections, or scheduled shutdowns. Used in circuit availability and OEE calculations. Sourced from Infor EAM work order schedules.']
  plant_utilisation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available hours during which the plant was actively processing ore, calculated as operating hours divided by available hours. Reflects how effectively available plant time is being used for production. Reported in daily production meetings.']
  primary_ore_type varchar [note: 'Dominant ore type or lithological classification of the ROM feed processed during the shift (e.g., oxide, sulphide, mixed, transitional, fresh). Determines the applicable processing circuit configuration and expected metallurgical recovery. Sourced from Geovia GEMS geological model and grade control data.']
  product_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Assayed grade of the primary valuable element in the final concentrate or product produced during the shift, expressed as a percentage. Sourced from LabWare LIMS product sample assay. Used for product quality certification, sales contract compliance, and metallurgical accounting.']
  rom_feed_tonnes_dry decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed to the processing plant during the shift, calculated by applying the measured moisture factor to wet tonnes. Dry tonnes are the standard basis for metallurgical accounting, resource reporting, and LOM plan reconciliation.']
  rom_feed_tonnes_wet decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total wet tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed to the processing plant during the shift, as measured at the feed conveyor belt scale. Wet weight includes moisture content. Used for mass balance and throughput reporting.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shift production run record. \'open\' indicates the shift is in progress; \'completed\' indicates shift has ended and data is captured; \'under_review\' indicates supervisor review pending; \'approved\' indicates shift supervisor sign-off complete; \'cancelled\' indicates the shift was voided. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the production shift commenced. Used as the primary date dimension for daily production reporting, reconciliation against LOM plan targets, and regulatory reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (date-level precision).']
  shift_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the production shift concluded, sourced from the MES or OSIsoft PI System historian. Used for shift duration calculation, handover documentation, and OEE reporting. ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  shift_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by the shift supervisor covering significant events, process upsets, equipment issues, safety observations, or other contextual information relevant to the shift production run. Supports shift handover communication and exception reporting.']
  shift_number varchar [note: 'Designates the shift rotation within the calendar day: \'day\' (typically 06:00–18:00), \'night\' (18:00–06:00), or \'afternoon\' (14:00–22:00) depending on site roster configuration. Used for shift-based production reporting and crew scheduling.']
  shift_run_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the shift production run, used in daily production reports, shift handover documentation, and reconciliation against LOM plan targets. Format: SPR-YYYY-MM-DD-PLANTCODE-SEQ. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  shift_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the production shift commenced, sourced from the MES or OSIsoft PI System historian. Used for accurate shift duration calculation, OEE computation, and integration with SCADA event logs. ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  supervisor_signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift supervisor formally approved and signed off the shift production run record. Marks the transition from \'under_review\' to \'approved\' status. Required for daily production reporting and audit compliance.']
  tonnes_processed decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry tonnes of ore that completed the primary processing circuit during the shift. May differ from ROM feed tonnes due to in-circuit inventory changes, spillage, or sampling losses. Used as the primary throughput metric for production reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  unplanned_downtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours of unscheduled downtime during the shift due to equipment breakdowns, process upsets, or emergency stoppages. Key input for MTBF and MTTR analysis and OEE degradation reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift production run record was last modified, including supervisor sign-off, data corrections, or status transitions. Supports audit trail and change tracking in the Silver layer. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the mine site during the shift that may have affected processing plant operations (e.g., extreme heat impacting reagent performance, rain increasing feed moisture, dust storms affecting conveyor operations). Used for operational exception reporting and HSE compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|storm|fog|high_wind|dust_storm|snow|extreme_heat — promote to reference product]']
  weather_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether adverse weather conditions materially impacted processing plant operations or throughput during the shift. Used to qualify production shortfalls in reporting and distinguish weather-related downtime from equipment or process failures.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each processing plant production shift — the operational heartbeat of the processing domain. Captures shift date, shift number (day/night/afternoon), plant ID, crew ID, tonnes fed (ROM dry and wet), tonnes processed, circuit availability (%), plant utilisation (%), OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), feed grade (head grade by element), operating hours, calendar hours, actual throughput rate (tph), design throughput rate (tph), feed blend composition (ore type percentages), primary ore type, weather conditions affecting operations, and shift supervisor sign-off. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian and MES (Manufacturing Execution System). Serves as the single source of truth for shift and daily production reporting against LOM plan targets. All circuit-level process events, reagent consumption, utility consumption, and operational exception records link back to this shift record via FK. Supports daily production meetings, weekly/monthly reporting, and annual reconciliation.'
}

Table processing.unit_operation_event {
  unit_operation_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each unit operation event record in the mineral processing domain. Primary key for the silver-layer lakehouse table.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary processing equipment asset (e.g., SAG mill, ball mill, flotation cell, leach tank) involved in this unit operation event.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit) where this unit operation event occurred. Links to the circuit master.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Unit operations (crushing, grinding, separation) process specific commodities. Required for metallurgical accounting by commodity type, recovery tracking, and operational performance analysis. Core to']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Individual unit operations (mills, flotation cells, leach tanks) are cost-tracked for activity-based costing, energy consumption analysis, and process optimization ROI. Standard process costing practi']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Unit operations process specific feed streams. One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the operation event to the feed stream being processed.']
