  grade_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: Unit operation events record feed_grade_pct and product_grade_pct for a specific element. Linking to grade_parameter ensures correct parameter definition is referenced for metallurgical accounting, pr']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Unit operation events (comminution, separation) often generate maintenance work orders when equipment performance degrades or failures occur. Links operational events to maintenance response for equip']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.plant. Business justification: unit_operation_event is the unified transactional record for all mineral processing operations. While plant can be derived via circuit.plant_id or shift_production_run.plant_id, a direct plant_id FK i']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Comminution and processing unit operations track throughput and energy consumption against scheduled mine production for bottleneck analysis, cost allocation, and integrated mine-mill performance opti']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Unit operations process ore from specific prospects. Required for metallurgical domain modeling, recovery prediction by ore type, and process optimization. Real business need: ore-specific comminution']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Unit operations (crushing, leaching, SXEW) require task-level risk assessments (HAZOP/JSA) under mining process safety regulations. A mining HSE manager expects each unit operation event to reference ']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Unit operation events contribute to production of a specific saleable product. Production accounting and cost allocation per saleable product requires this link. commodity_id alone is insufficient — c']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this unit operation event was recorded. Enables shift-level metallurgical accounting and performance reporting.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Comminution/grinding events produce tailings streams directed to specific TSF. Essential for metallurgical accounting, TSF deposition planning, and environmental compliance reporting. Tracks material ']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for process operators or metallurgical accountants to record observations, abnormal conditions, equipment issues, or data quality notes associated with this unit operation event. Sourced from MES shift log entries.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this unit operation event record was first captured and written to the silver-layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  current_density_a_per_m2 decimal(8,3) [note: 'Electrical current density applied in electrowinning (EW) cells during SX-EW unit operations, expressed in A/m². Type-specific parameter for electrowinning operations. Controls cathode deposition rate, product purity, and specific energy consumption.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this unit operation event data was sourced. Supports data lineage, quality assessment, and reconciliation between SCADA historian (OSIsoft PI System), MES, and LabWare LIMS.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp when this unit operation event concluded, as recorded by the SCADA historian. Null if the event is still active. Used to calculate operating duration and throughput rates.']
  event_reference_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable externally-known business identifier for this unit operation event, used in metallurgical reports, process control logs, and MES reconciliation. Format: UOE-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp when this unit operation event commenced, as recorded by the SCADA historian (OSIsoft PI System). Used as the principal business event time for metallurgical accounting period alignment.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the unit operation event record. \'active\' indicates the shift event is in progress; \'completed\' indicates normal shift closure; \'aborted\' indicates unplanned stoppage; \'suspended\' indicates planned hold; \'pending_review\' indicates metallurgical accountant review required.']
  feed_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Head grade of the valuable mineral in the feed material expressed as a percentage (e.g., % Cu, % Fe, % Li2O). Sourced from online analysers or composite assay samples. Critical for recovery calculation and LOM plan grade reconciliation.']
  feed_tonnage_dry_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonnes of ore or material fed to the unit operation during the event period. Core throughput metric for metallurgical accounting and LOM plan variance analysis. Sourced from belt scale or flow meter readings in OSIsoft PI System.']
  froth_depth_mm decimal(7,2) [note: 'Depth of the froth layer in flotation cells, expressed in millimetres. Type-specific parameter for flotation unit operations (rougher, scavenger, cleaner). Controlled variable for concentrate grade and recovery optimisation. Sourced from ultrasonic level sensors via OSIsoft PI System.']
  is_lom_plan_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this unit operation event falls within an active LOM (Life of Mine) plan period with defined targets, enabling filtering of events for plan vs actual variance reporting.']
  leach_solution_grade_g_per_l decimal(8,4) [note: 'Concentration of the target metal in the pregnant leach solution (PLS), expressed in grams per litre. Type-specific parameter for heap, tank, vat, CIL, and CIP leaching operations. Used in leach circuit mass balance and SX-EW feed quality assessment.']
  lom_plan_feed_tonnage_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'LOM (Life of Mine) plan target for feed tonnage to this unit operation for the corresponding shift/period. Used for variance analysis between actual and planned throughput. Sourced from Deswik or Hexagon MinePlan scheduling outputs.']
  lom_plan_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'LOM (Life of Mine) plan target metallurgical recovery percentage for this unit operation and period. Used for variance analysis between actual and planned recovery. Sourced from process design basis and LOM plan assumptions.']
  medium_density_kg_per_m3 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Density of the dense medium (ferrosilicon or magnetite suspension) used in Dense Media Separation (DMS) unit operations, expressed in kg/m³. Type-specific parameter for DMS operations. Controls separation cut-point and product quality.']
  metallurgical_account_period varchar [note: 'The calendar month period (YYYY-MM) to which this unit operation event is assigned for metallurgical accounting and financial reporting purposes. Used to aggregate shift-level events into monthly mass balance reconciliation reports.']
  operating_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total hours the unit operation was in active production during the event period. Excludes planned and unplanned downtime. Used in OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation and availability reporting.']
  operation_subtype varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification of the unit operation within its type (e.g., SAG mill, ball mill, HPGR, crusher for comminution; rougher, scavenger, cleaner for flotation; heap, tank, vat, CIL, CIP for leaching; solvent extraction, electrowinning for SX-EW; spirals, shaking tables, jigs for gravity). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sag_mill|ball_mill|hpgr|crusher|rougher|scavenger|cleaner|heap_leach|tank_leach|vat_leach|cil|cip|solvent_extraction|electrowinning|spirals|shaking_tables|jigs|dense_media_separation|magnetic_separation|thickener|filter — promote to reference product]']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Type discriminator identifying the class of mineral processing unit operation for this event. Drives which type-specific attribute groups are populated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comminution|flotation|leaching|sx_ew|dms|gravity_separation|magnetic_separation|thickening_dewatering — promote to reference product]']
  p80_feed_microns decimal(10,2) [note: '80th percentile passing size of the feed material in microns (µm). Primary comminution circuit feed size parameter used to characterise ore hardness, set mill operating conditions, and benchmark against design P80. Applicable to comminution operation types (SAG, ball mill, HPGR, crusher).']
  p80_product_microns decimal(10,2) [note: '80th percentile passing size of the product material in microns (µm) exiting the comminution unit operation. Compared against target grind size to assess liberation efficiency and downstream flotation or leach performance.']
  plant_area_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the physical plant area or section within the processing facility where this unit operation is located (e.g., COMM-01 for comminution area 1, FLOT-02 for flotation area 2). Aligns with P&ID area designations.']
  power_consumption_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total electrical energy consumed by the unit operation during the event period, expressed in kWh. Used in energy cost allocation, OPEX reporting, and carbon footprint calculations. Sourced from power meters via OSIsoft PI System.']
  product_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Grade of the valuable mineral in the product or concentrate expressed as a percentage. For flotation concentrates this is the concentrate grade; for leach circuits this is the solution grade. Used in metallurgical accounting and concentrate quality reporting.']
  product_tonnage_dry_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonnes of product (concentrate, leach solution, or processed material) produced by the unit operation during the event period. Used in metallurgical accounting mass balance calculations.']
  reagent_dosage_g_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Total reagent addition rate for the primary reagent in the unit operation, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed. Covers collectors, frothers, depressants (flotation), or lixiviants (leaching). Used in reagent cost tracking and process optimisation.']
  recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the valuable mineral in the feed that is recovered into the product stream during this unit operation event. Calculated as (product mass × product grade) / (feed mass × feed grade) × 100. Core KPI for process performance and LOM plan variance.']
  slurry_density_kg_per_m3 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Density of the slurry (water and ore mixture) in the unit operation, expressed in kg/m³. Critical process control parameter for flotation, leaching, and thickening circuits. Sourced from nuclear density gauges or Coriolis meters via OSIsoft PI System.']
  slurry_ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'pH value of the process slurry measured during the unit operation event. Critical for flotation reagent effectiveness, leach chemistry control, and environmental compliance. Sourced from inline pH probes via OSIsoft PI System.']
  specific_energy_kwh_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Energy consumed per dry metric tonne of feed processed by the unit operation, expressed in kWh/t. Key efficiency metric for comminution circuits (SAG, ball mill, HPGR) and electrowinning. Sourced from power meters via OSIsoft PI System. Used in OPEX analysis and LOM energy cost modelling.']
  tailings_tonnage_dry_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonnes of tailings (gangue and waste material) generated by the unit operation during the event period. Used in mass balance reconciliation, TSF (Tailings Storage Facility) capacity planning, and environmental reporting.']
  throughput_rate_tph decimal(10,3) [note: 'Instantaneous or average throughput rate of the unit operation during the event period, expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (t/h). Compared against design capacity and LOM plan targets for process control and production reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this unit operation event record was last modified, including metallurgical accountant adjustments, data quality corrections, or status transitions.']
  water_consumption_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total volume of process water consumed by the unit operation during the event period, expressed in cubic metres. Used in water balance reporting, environmental compliance (EIS), and OPEX cost allocation. Sourced from flow meters via OSIsoft PI System.']

  Note: 'Unified transactional record of all mineral processing operations per shift per circuit, using a type discriminator to capture comminution (SAG, ball mill, HPGR, crusher), flotation (rougher, scavenger, cleaner), leaching (heap, tank, vat, CIL/CIP), SX-EW (solvent extraction, electrowinning), DMS (dense media separation), gravity separation (spirals, shaking tables, jigs), magnetic separation, and thickening/dewatering events. Common attributes: circuit ID, shift reference, feed tonnage, product tonnage, recovery (%), specific energy (kWh/t). Type-specific attribute groups capture process-specific parameters (e.g., P80 for comminution, froth depth for flotation, medium density for DMS, current density for SX-EW). Sourced from OSIsoft PI System SCADA historian and MES. Supports metallurgical accounting, process control, and LOM plan variance analysis.'
}

Table processing.flotation_event {
  flotation_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each flotation circuit performance record per shift. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Flotation events occur on specific flotation cell assets. Metallurgical engineers and maintenance teams track cell-level performance (recovery, froth depth, air flow) against asset OEE and maintenance']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flotation circuit (e.g., rougher, scavenger, cleaner bank) where this event was recorded. Links to the processing circuit master resource. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE / resource reference category.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Flotation circuits target specific minerals/commodities (copper, nickel, zinc). Core to recovery tracking, circuit optimization, and metallurgical reporting. Domain experts expect commodity-specific f']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Flotation events process specific feed streams. One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the flotation event to the feed stream being processed.']
  grade_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: Flotation events record feed_grade_pct, concentrate_grade_pct, and tailings_grade_pct for a specific element. Linking to grade_parameter ensures correct parameter definition for metallurgical reportin']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Flotation involves chemical reagents (collectors, frothers, depressants) and mechanical hazards (impellers, agitators). Incidents during flotation operations (chemical spills, equipment failures, expo']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Flotation circuit samples (feed, concentrate, tailings) are sent to designated labs for grade and recovery verification. Critical for process control, circuit optimization, and validating online analy']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Flotation cell mechanical issues (impeller wear, air system failures) during events trigger maintenance work orders. Links flotation performance problems to maintenance interventions for recovery opti']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or concentrator facility where the flotation circuit operates. Enables plant-level aggregation and benchmarking across sites.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Flotation events track recovery performance against scheduled mine production for metallurgical accounting, ore type performance analysis, and integrated mine-mill optimization. Essential for recovery']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Flotation events occur during a production shift run. One flotation event belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many flotation events. This links the event to the processing domain\'s shift entit']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this flotation event was recorded. Enables shift-level metallurgical accounting and performance comparison across day, afternoon, and night shifts.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Flotation circuit operators target a specific concentrate specification (grade, recovery, impurity limits). Linking enables automated conformance checking of concentrate_grade_target_pct and recovery_']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Flotation circuits generate tailings streams deposited to specific TSF. Critical for metallurgical balance closure, TSF capacity management, and geochemical characterization. Tracks tailings_mass_t de']
  unit_operation_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: unit_operation_event is described as the \'unified transactional record of all mineral processing operations per shift per circuit, using a type pattern\' — it is the supertype record. flotation_event i']
  air_flow_rate_m3_per_min decimal(8,3) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of air injected into the flotation cells, measured in cubic metres per minute (m³/min). Air flow is a primary control variable affecting bubble generation, froth stability, and mineral recovery. Monitored via OSIsoft PI SCADA integration.']
  cell_count_operating varchar [note: 'Number of flotation cells actively operating in the circuit during this event period. Cells may be taken offline for maintenance or cleaning. Operating cell count affects residence time and overall circuit capacity. Sourced from Infor EAM work order data.']
  cell_volume_m3 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total active volume of the flotation cells in operation during this event, measured in cubic metres (m³). Combined with feed rate, determines slurry residence time which is a key flotation kinetics parameter.']
  circuit_availability_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage of the event period during which the flotation circuit was available and operating (not in planned or unplanned downtime). Calculated as operating hours divided by scheduled hours × 100. Contributes to Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) reporting.']
  circuit_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the valuable mineral in the feed that is recovered into the concentrate product for this flotation circuit stage. Calculated as: (concentrate mass × concentrate grade) / (feed mass × feed grade) × 100. Core metallurgical KPI for LOM plan tracking and reagent optimisation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  circuit_stage varchar [note: 'The specific stage of the flotation circuit captured in this event record. Rougher is the primary separation stage; scavenger recovers minerals from rougher tailings; cleaner upgrades rougher concentrate grade; recleaner further upgrades cleaner concentrate. Critical for stage-by-stage metallurgical accounting.']
  collector_dosage_g_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Dosage rate of the collector reagent (e.g., xanthate, dithiophosphate) added to the flotation circuit, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed (g/t). Collector promotes hydrophobicity of target mineral surfaces to enable bubble attachment. Key reagent optimisation variable.']
  concentrate_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Grade of the valuable mineral in the flotation concentrate product, expressed as a percentage (%). Measures the quality of the separation achieved. Higher concentrate grade reduces downstream smelting costs and improves payable metal. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  concentrate_grade_target_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The planned or contractually required concentrate grade for the valuable mineral, expressed as a percentage (%). Used to assess whether the flotation circuit is meeting product quality specifications for offtake agreements and smelter contracts.']
  concentrate_mass_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry mass of concentrate produced by the flotation circuit during the event period, measured in dry metric tonnes (t). Used in metallurgical accounting mass balance and payable metal calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flotation event record was first captured in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  depressant_dosage_g_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Dosage rate of the depressant reagent (e.g., sodium cyanide, zinc sulphate, CMC) added to the flotation circuit, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed (g/t). Depressants selectively prevent flotation of gangue minerals or penalty elements, improving concentrate grade and selectivity.']
  downtime_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason for any unplanned or planned downtime experienced by the flotation circuit during this event period (e.g., pump failure, cell maintenance, reagent outage, power interruption). Null if no downtime occurred. Used for reliability analysis and maintenance planning in Infor EAM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pump_failure|cell_maintenance|reagent_outage|power_interruption|planned_shutdown|instrumentation_fault — promote to reference product]']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp marking the end of the flotation measurement period (typically shift end). Combined with start timestamp to define the measurement window for throughput and reagent consumption calculations.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this flotation event record, formatted as FLT-YYYY-MM-DD-NNNN. Used for cross-system reference, metallurgical accounting reconciliation, and audit trail. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp marking the start of the flotation measurement period (typically shift start). Used for time-series alignment with OSIsoft PI historian data and SCADA records. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the flotation event record. Draft indicates data entry in progress; confirmed indicates shift data locked; reviewed indicates metallurgist sign-off; approved indicates inclusion in metallurgical balance; voided indicates record invalidated. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  feed_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Head grade of the valuable mineral (e.g., copper, lithium, nickel) in the flotation feed, expressed as a percentage (%). Derived from assay results and used as the denominator in circuit recovery calculations. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay data.']
  feed_particle_size_p80_um decimal(8,2) [note: 'The 80th percentile passing particle size (P80) of the flotation feed, measured in micrometres (µm). Particle size is a critical determinant of flotation performance — coarse particles are difficult to float while ultra-fine particles suffer from poor bubble attachment. Sourced from LabWare LIMS particle size analysis.']
  feed_rate_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mass flow rate of ore slurry fed into the flotation circuit during the event period, measured in dry metric tonnes per hour (t/h). Key throughput metric for metallurgical accounting and LOM plan performance tracking.']
  froth_depth_mm decimal(7,2) [note: 'Depth of the froth layer maintained in the flotation cell during the event period, measured in millimetres (mm). Froth depth is a critical control parameter influencing concentrate grade and recovery trade-off. Deeper froth generally improves grade at the expense of recovery.']
  frother_dosage_g_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Dosage rate of the frother reagent (e.g., MIBC, pine oil, polyglycol) added to the flotation circuit, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed (g/t). Frother stabilises the froth layer and controls bubble size distribution. Critical for froth stability and concentrate transport.']
  impeller_speed_rpm decimal(7,2) [note: 'Average rotational speed of the flotation cell impellers during the event period, measured in revolutions per minute (RPM). Impeller speed controls slurry agitation, air dispersion, and bubble-particle contact. Monitored via OSIsoft PI SCADA.']
  lime_dosage_kg_per_t decimal(8,3) [note: 'Dosage rate of lime (calcium hydroxide or calcium oxide) added to the flotation circuit for pH control, expressed in kilograms per dry metric tonne of feed (kg/t). Lime is typically the highest-volume reagent in sulphide flotation circuits and a significant OPEX driver.']
  mass_pull_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'The ratio of concentrate mass to feed mass expressed as a percentage (%). Indicates the proportion of total feed mass reporting to the concentrate. High mass pull may indicate over-flotation and gangue entrainment; low mass pull may indicate under-recovery. Key flotation control parameter.']
  mineral_type varchar [note: 'The primary valuable mineral being recovered in this flotation circuit event (e.g., copper, nickel, lithium). Determines the applicable reagent suite, recovery benchmarks, and product specifications. Essential for multi-commodity processing plants. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: copper|nickel|lithium|zinc|lead|molybdenum|gold|silver — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ore type being processed in the flotation circuit during this event (e.g., sulphide, oxide, mixed, transitional). Ore type significantly influences reagent selection, dosage rates, and achievable recovery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sulphide|oxide|mixed|transitional|primary|secondary — promote to reference product]']
  pulp_density_pct_solids decimal(6,2) [note: 'Solids content of the flotation feed slurry expressed as a percentage by mass (% solids). Pulp density affects reagent effectiveness, bubble-particle collision rates, and overall flotation kinetics. Monitored continuously via OSIsoft PI.']
  pulp_ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'pH of the flotation pulp during the event period. pH is a critical chemical environment parameter that governs mineral surface chemistry, collector adsorption, and selectivity between valuable minerals and gangue. Typically controlled with lime addition.']
  recovery_target_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'The planned or budgeted circuit recovery percentage for this flotation stage as defined in the LOM plan or metallurgical design criteria. Enables variance analysis between actual and target recovery for performance management and LOM plan tracking.']
  sample_reference varchar [note: 'The LabWare LIMS sample identifier for the assay samples collected during this flotation event (feed, concentrate, and tailings). Enables traceability between the flotation performance record and the underlying laboratory assay results supporting grade data.']
  slurry_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the flotation feed slurry during the event period, measured in degrees Celsius (°C). Slurry temperature affects reagent solubility, reaction kinetics, and froth stability. Particularly relevant in cold-climate operations and heated leach-flotation circuits.']
  tailings_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Grade of the valuable mineral remaining in the flotation tailings stream, expressed as a percentage (%). Indicates mineral losses to the Tailings Storage Facility (TSF). Used to calculate circuit recovery and identify optimisation opportunities. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  tailings_mass_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry mass of tailings discharged from the flotation circuit during the event period, measured in dry metric tonnes (t). Used in mass balance reconciliation and TSF volumetric planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flotation event record was last modified. Tracks data corrections, metallurgist adjustments, and re-assay updates. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  water_addition_m3_per_h decimal(8,3) [note: 'Rate of process water added to the flotation circuit to maintain target pulp density, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/h). Water consumption is a key environmental and OPEX metric, particularly in water-stressed mining regions subject to ISO 14001 reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of flotation circuit performance per shift including rougher, scavenger, and cleaner stages. Captures feed grade, concentrate grade, tailings grade, mass pull (%), air flow rate (m³/min), froth depth (mm), reagent dosage rates, and circuit recovery (%). Supports metallurgical accounting and reagent optimisation.'
}

Table processing.leach_event {
  leach_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each leaching operation event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the leach_event data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Leach events occur in specific leach tanks or heap leach pad assets. Hydromet operations track extraction efficiency and reagent consumption per tank asset to schedule maintenance, manage asset life, ']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the leach circuit or processing unit (heap leach pad, CIL/CIP tank, vat leach vessel) where this event occurred. Links to the processing circuit master.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Leaching extracts specific commodities (copper, gold, nickel, uranium). Fundamental process-commodity link for hydrometallurgical operations. Replaces denormalized commodity text field. Essential for ']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Leach events process specific feed streams. One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the leach event to the feed stream being processed.']
  grade_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: Leach events record feed_grade_pct and residue_grade_pct for a specific element. Linking to grade_parameter ensures correct parameter definition for extraction efficiency reporting, regulatory complia']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Leaching uses hazardous chemicals (cyanide for gold, sulfuric acid for copper). Incidents during leach operations (solution spills, gas releases, exposure events) require direct traceability to leach ']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Leach solution samples (PLS, raffinate, residue) analyzed at specific labs for extraction efficiency monitoring and reagent optimization. Standard hydrometallurgical process control requirement for co']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Leach tank/pad equipment failures (agitators, pumps, solution distribution) during leach events generate maintenance work orders. Links hydrometallurgical process issues to maintenance response for ex']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the leach event occurred. Supports multi-site reporting and plant-level performance benchmarking.']
  process_sample_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the representative leach solution or residue sample submitted to the laboratory for assay analysis during this event. Links to LabWare LIMS sample management records for quality control and mass balance verification.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Leach events must reference production schedules for heap leach ore tracking, metal recovery forecasting, and ore type extraction performance analysis. Critical for heap leach operations and long-term']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Leach performance is ore-source dependent. Essential for heap leach/tank leach optimization, resource model metallurgical assumptions validation. Real business need: extraction efficiency tracking by ']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Leach events occur during a production shift run. One leach event belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many leach events. This links the event to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this leach event was recorded. Enables shift-based production reporting and reconciliation.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Leach residues/tailings deposited to specific TSF. Required for geochemical risk management (ARD assessment), TSF design compliance, and environmental monitoring. Tracks ore_feed_mass_t minus recovere']
  unit_operation_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: leach_event is a specialized subtype of unit_operation_event for heap/tank/vat leaching operations. Linking leach_event to its parent unit_operation_event enables the supertype/subtype pattern for uni']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Operational batch or lot number assigned to the ore charge or solution batch processed in this leach event. Used for traceability from ROM feed through to final concentrate or pregnant leach solution (PLS). Sourced from LabWare LIMS or SCADA batch management.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text operational comments or notes recorded by the process operator or metallurgist for this leach event. May include observations on solution colour, foam, equipment behaviour, or other qualitative process indicators not captured by structured fields.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this leach event record was first captured and persisted in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system from which this leach event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, quality assessment, and reconciliation between automated sensor data (OSIsoft PI, SCADA) and manually entered or LIMS-derived records.']
  dissolved_metal_concentration_ppm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Concentration of the target dissolved metal in the pregnant leach solution (PLS) or leachate, measured in parts per million (ppm). Primary indicator of leaching effectiveness and solution grade. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results or inline OSIsoft PI analyser tags.']
  dissolved_oxygen_mg_l decimal(8,3) [note: 'Concentration of dissolved oxygen in the leach solution, measured in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Dissolved oxygen is a critical parameter for cyanide leaching of gold and silver, directly influencing metal dissolution rates. Monitored via OSIsoft PI DO analysers.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the leaching operation concluded for this event or batch. Used in conjunction with event_start_timestamp to derive leach cycle duration.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the leach event, typically sourced from the OSIsoft PI System or SCADA batch/event log. Used for cross-system traceability and operational reporting (e.g., LE-2024-00123).']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the leaching operation commenced for this event or batch. Principal real-world event time used for duration calculations, shift allocation, and production scheduling alignment.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the leach event. Indicates whether the leaching operation is actively running, has completed normally, was aborted due to process upset, is on hold, or is pending quality review before finalisation.']
  extraction_efficiency_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the target metal extracted from the ore feed into solution during this leach event, calculated as (metal dissolved / metal in feed) × 100. Key performance indicator for process optimisation, LOM plan reconciliation, and recovery rate reporting. Sourced from LabWare LIMS mass balance.']
  feed_grade_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'Head grade of the ore feed entering the leach circuit, expressed as a percentage (%) for base metals or g/t equivalent converted to percentage for precious metals. Sourced from assay results in LabWare LIMS. Critical input for extraction efficiency and recovery rate calculations.']
  leach_duration_hrs decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the leaching operation for this event, measured in hours. Represents the actual leach time from solution application/charge to drain/discharge. Used for cycle time optimisation, throughput modelling, and LOM plan variance analysis.']
  leach_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the leaching method applied in this event. Distinguishes between heap leach (copper, gold), tank leach (CIL/CIP for gold), vat leach, and agitation leach configurations. Drives process parameter benchmarking and recovery rate analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: heap_leach|tank_leach|vat_leach|cil|cip|agitation_leach — promote to reference product]']
  ore_feed_mass_t decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total dry mass of ore charged to the leach circuit for this event, measured in metric tonnes. Represents the principal quantitative input for recovery rate calculation and throughput reporting against Life of Mine (LOM) plan targets.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Mineralogical classification of the ore processed in this leach event. Ore type significantly influences leaching chemistry, reagent selection, and achievable recovery rates. Sourced from Geovia GEMS geological model and grade control assay data.']
  oxidation_reduction_potential_mv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Oxidation-reduction potential (ORP) of the leach solution measured in millivolts (mV). Critical electrochemical parameter for monitoring leaching conditions, particularly in cyanide leach (gold) and SX-EW (copper) circuits. Sourced from OSIsoft PI inline analysers.']
  pi_tag_reference varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System tag name or event frame reference associated with this leach event. Enables direct traceability back to the process historian for raw time-series data retrieval, audit, and re-processing. Applicable when data_source is pi_system or scada.']
  process_upset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a process upset or abnormal operating condition occurred during this leach event (True = upset occurred, False = normal operation). Process upsets may include pH excursions, reagent dosing failures, pump trips, or temperature deviations. Triggers investigation and corrective action workflows in IsoMetrix.']
  reagent_concentration_g_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of the primary leaching reagent (e.g., cyanide for gold CIL/CIP, sulphuric acid for copper heap leach, caustic soda for lithium leach) in the leach solution, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Key process control parameter for optimising extraction efficiency and reagent cost (OPEX).']
  reagent_consumption_kg decimal(14,3) [note: 'Total mass of primary leaching reagent consumed during this leach event, measured in kilograms. Used for OPEX tracking, reagent cost allocation, and environmental reporting of chemical usage. Sourced from Pronto Xi inventory consumption records and SCADA flow meters.']
  reagent_type varchar [note: 'Type of primary leaching reagent used in this event. Determines applicable safety, environmental, and regulatory requirements. Examples include sodium cyanide (gold CIL/CIP), sulphuric acid (copper heap leach), and caustic soda (lithium leach circuits). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cyanide|sulphuric_acid|caustic_soda|ammonia|chlorine|hydrochloric_acid — promote to reference product]']
  residue_grade_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'Grade of the target metal remaining in the leach residue (tailings) after the leaching event, expressed as a percentage. Used to calculate actual metal recovery and identify process losses. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results on residue samples.']
  slurry_density_g_cm3 decimal(8,4) [note: 'Density of the ore-solution slurry (water and ore mixture) in the leach circuit, measured in grams per cubic centimetre (g/cm³). Applicable to tank leach and CIL/CIP circuits. Monitored via OSIsoft PI density instruments. Affects mass transfer and reagent efficiency.']
  solution_flow_rate_m3_hr decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of leach solution through the circuit during this event, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/hr). Monitored via OSIsoft PI flow instrumentation. Influences reagent distribution, contact time, and overall leaching kinetics.']
  solution_ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured pH of the leach solution at the time of this event or at the representative sampling point within the event window. Critical process control parameter for reagent efficiency, metal dissolution kinetics, and environmental compliance (acid mine drainage prevention).']
  solution_temperature_c decimal(7,2) [note: 'Temperature of the leach solution in degrees Celsius (°C) measured during this event. Temperature directly influences leaching kinetics, reagent consumption rates, and metal dissolution efficiency. Monitored via OSIsoft PI System instrumentation.']
  solution_volume_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Total volume of leach solution applied or circulated during this event, measured in cubic metres (m³). Used for water balance reporting, reagent dosing calculations, and environmental water management compliance.']
  tank_pad_reference varchar [note: 'Operational identifier of the specific leach tank, leach pad cell, or vat vessel used in this event (e.g., TANK-03, PAD-CELL-7B). Sourced from the OSIsoft PI asset hierarchy or SCADA tag configuration. Enables equipment-level performance tracking.']
  target_extraction_efficiency_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned or budgeted extraction efficiency percentage for this leach event type and ore type, as defined in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan or metallurgical test work. Enables variance analysis between actual and planned recovery performance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this leach event record was last modified or updated in the data platform. Supports change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance.']
  upset_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the process upset or abnormal condition that occurred during this leach event, if applicable. Populated when process_upset_flag is True. Captures the nature of the deviation for root cause analysis and corrective action tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of leaching operations (heap leach, tank leach, vat leach) per shift or batch. Captures leach tank/pad ID, solution pH, temperature (°C), reagent concentration (g/L), leach time (hrs), dissolved metal concentration (ppm), and extraction efficiency (%). Applicable to gold CIL/CIP, copper heap leach, and lithium leach circuits.'
}

Table processing.sxew_event {
  sxew_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SX-EW operational event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: SX/EW events occur on specific electrowinning cell or mixer-settler assets. Copper refineries track cathode production, current efficiency, and cell voltage per asset for OEE reporting, maintenance sc']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SX-EW processing circuit (tankhouse or extraction circuit) where this event occurred. Links to the processing circuit master resource.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: SX-EW produces specific commodities (copper cathode). Direct production link essential for cathode quality tracking, production reporting, and linking hydrometallurgical output to saleable products. S']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: SX-EW events process specific feed streams (PLS feed). One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the SX-EW event to the feed stream being processed.']
  grade_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: SX-EW events record cathode_grade_pct and aqueous_feed_grade_gl for specific elements. Linking to grade_parameter ensures correct parameter definition for cathode quality reporting, LME grade complian']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: SXEW involves high-voltage electrical systems (electrowinning cells) and corrosive solutions (sulfuric acid electrolyte). Incidents during SXEW operations (electrical faults, acid spills, cathode hand']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Electrolyte and cathode samples from SX-EW circuits sent to designated labs for copper grade, impurity analysis, and cathode quality verification. Essential for product quality control and sales contr']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: SX-EW circuit equipment issues (rectifier failures, cell short circuits, organic pumps) during production events trigger maintenance work orders. Links cathode production problems to maintenance inter']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or hydrometallurgical facility where the SX-EW circuit operates. Enables multi-site reporting and plant-level performance aggregation.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: SX-EW events track cathode production against scheduled mine production for integrated copper production planning, metal accounting, and sales contract fulfillment. Essential for copper operations wit']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this SX-EW event was recorded. Each SX-EW event is captured per shift as the primary reporting cadence.']
  unit_operation_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: sxew_event is a specialized subtype of unit_operation_event for Solvent Extraction Electrowinning operations. Linking sxew_event to its parent unit_operation_event enables unified hydrometallurgical p']
  acid_concentration_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Sulphuric acid concentration in the electrowinning electrolyte, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Acid balance is critical for cathode quality, current efficiency, and prevention of anode corrosion in the EW tankhouse.']
  aqueous_feed_grade_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Copper concentration in the aqueous feed (pregnant leach solution, PLS) entering the solvent extraction circuit, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Determines the extraction circuit loading and directly influences cathode production output.']
  cathode_grade_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Purity of the copper cathode produced, expressed as a percentage of copper content. LME Grade A copper cathode requires ≥99.99% Cu. Used for product quality certification, sales contract compliance, and LME registration eligibility.']
  cathode_production_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total mass of copper cathode produced during the shift, measured in kilograms. The primary production output metric for the SX-EW circuit, used for daily production reporting, LOM plan reconciliation, and sales volume tracking.']
  cathode_quality_grade varchar [note: 'Quality classification of copper cathode produced during the shift. \'LME_Grade_A\' indicates product meets London Metal Exchange Grade A specification (≥99.99% Cu); \'off_spec\' indicates product below LME standard but saleable; \'rejected\' indicates product not suitable for sale.']
  cathodes_harvested varchar [note: 'Number of individual copper cathode sheets harvested (stripped) from the electrowinning cells during the shift. Used for tankhouse productivity tracking, cathode cycle time management, and inventory reconciliation.']
  cell_voltage_v decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average operating voltage across the electrowinning cells during the shift, measured in volts (V). Used to monitor energy efficiency, detect anode/cathode fouling, and calculate specific energy consumption per tonne of copper produced.']
  cells_operating varchar [note: 'Number of electrowinning cells in active operation during the shift. Used to calculate effective circuit capacity, monitor cell availability, and assess impact of cell outages on production.']
  cells_total varchar [note: 'Total number of electrowinning cells installed in the circuit (nameplate capacity). Used as the denominator for cell availability calculations and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) reporting.']
  circuit_downtime_min varchar [note: 'Total unplanned and planned downtime for the SX-EW circuit during the shift, measured in minutes. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculation, maintenance planning via Infor EAM, and production loss reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SX-EW event record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking. Populated automatically at record insertion.']
  current_density_am2 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Electrical current density applied across the electrowinning cathode surface, measured in amperes per square metre (A/m²). A primary electrowinning process control parameter governing copper deposition rate, cathode quality, and energy consumption.']
  current_efficiency_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Ratio of actual copper deposited to the theoretical copper that should have been deposited based on Faraday\'s Law, expressed as a percentage. A key electrowinning performance indicator; values below target indicate short-circuiting, impurity effects, or electrolyte imbalance.']
  downtime_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason code for circuit downtime recorded during the shift. Used for root cause analysis, maintenance prioritisation, and production loss categorisation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned_maintenance|unplanned_breakdown|process_upset|reagent_shortage|power_outage|other — promote to reference product if additional codes required]']
  electrolyte_temperature_c decimal(7,2) [note: 'Operating temperature of the electrowinning electrolyte during the shift, measured in degrees Celsius (°C). Temperature affects copper ion mobility, current efficiency, and cathode surface quality. Monitored via OSIsoft PI System.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the SX-EW shift event, formatted as SXEW-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used for cross-referencing with OSIsoft PI System historian records, lab assay reports, and production reconciliation.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the SX-EW event record. \'draft\' indicates data entry in progress; \'confirmed\' indicates shift data locked; \'reviewed\' indicates metallurgist review complete; \'approved\' indicates management sign-off; \'voided\' indicates record invalidated.']
  extraction_efficiency_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of copper extracted from the aqueous feed (PLS) into the organic phase during the solvent extraction stage, calculated as (PLS grade – raffinate grade) / PLS grade × 100. A key metallurgical recovery indicator for the SX circuit.']
  impurity_fe_mgl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of iron (Fe) impurity in the electrowinning electrolyte, measured in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Elevated iron levels reduce current efficiency and degrade cathode surface quality. Monitored via LabWare LIMS shift samples.']
  impurity_mn_mgl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of manganese (Mn) impurity in the electrowinning electrolyte, measured in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Manganese causes anode passivation and cathode surface defects. Tracked via LabWare LIMS for electrolyte bleed management.']
  lom_plan_cathode_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Planned cathode copper production target for the shift as defined in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan or short-term production schedule. Used for variance analysis between actual and planned production, and for LOM plan tracking.']
  organic_cu_concentration_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of copper in the organic (solvent) phase of the solvent extraction circuit, measured in grams per litre (g/L). A key process control parameter indicating the loading efficiency of the organic extractant (e.g., LIX reagent). Sourced from OSIsoft PI System online analysers or LabWare LIMS shift samples.']
  organic_flow_rate_m3h decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of the organic solvent phase circulating through the solvent extraction mixer-settlers, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/h). Used to control the aqueous-to-organic (A:O) ratio and optimise copper extraction efficiency.']
  pls_flow_rate_m3h decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of the pregnant leach solution (PLS) entering the solvent extraction circuit, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/h). Controls the aqueous-to-organic (A:O) ratio and extraction circuit residence time.']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the SX-EW production event occurred. Used for daily production reporting, reconciliation against Life of Mine (LOM) plan targets, and regulatory disclosure.']
  raffinate_grade_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Copper concentration in the raffinate (barren aqueous solution) exiting the solvent extraction circuit after copper has been extracted, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Used to calculate extraction efficiency and monitor copper losses to raffinate.']
  reagent_consumption_lm3 decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volume of organic extractant reagent (e.g., LIX 984N or equivalent) consumed per cubic metre of pregnant leach solution processed, measured in litres per cubic metre (L/m³). Used for reagent cost tracking, OPEX reporting, and supply chain planning via Pronto Xi.']
  shift_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the operational shift ended for this SX-EW event. Used to calculate shift duration and align with production reporting periods.']
  shift_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the operational shift commenced for this SX-EW event. Principal business event timestamp used for time-series analysis, production scheduling alignment, and OSIsoft PI historian correlation.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift period: day, afternoon, or night. Used for shift-pattern analysis, fatigue risk management, and production performance benchmarking across shift rotations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system from which this SX-EW event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation between systems, and data quality root cause analysis in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  specific_energy_kwh_t decimal(12,4) [note: 'Electrical energy consumed per tonne of copper cathode produced, measured in kilowatt-hours per tonne (kWh/t Cu). A key OPEX and sustainability metric for the electrowinning circuit, used for energy cost allocation and carbon footprint reporting.']
  spent_electrolyte_grade_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Copper concentration in the spent electrolyte returning from the electrowinning cells to the stripping circuit, measured in grams per litre (g/L). The difference between strip solution grade and spent electrolyte grade represents the copper deposited per pass.']
  strip_solution_grade_gl decimal(10,4) [note: 'Copper concentration in the electrolyte strip solution (advance electrolyte) fed to the electrowinning tankhouse, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Directly controls cathode deposition rate and current efficiency in the EW circuit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SX-EW event record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks silver layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of Solvent Extraction Electrowinning (SX-EW) operations per shift. Captures organic phase copper concentration (g/L), aqueous feed grade, strip solution grade, current density (A/m²), cell voltage (V), cathode production (kg/shift), and current efficiency (%). Specific to copper SX-EW processing circuits.'
}

Table processing.reagent_consumption {
  reagent_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each reagent consumption record in the mineral processing circuit. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  chemical_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.chemical_register. Business justification: Reagents consumed in processing must be registered chemicals with SDS, GHS classification, and handling procedures. Links consumption records to chemical hazard data for regulatory compliance (thresho']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing circuit (e.g., flotation, leaching, SX-EW, DMS) where the reagent was applied. Enables circuit-level OPEX analysis.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Reagent costs are material opex components (often 10-30% of processing costs) allocated to cost centres for AISC calculation and budget control. reagent_consumption.cost_centre_code (plain attr) is de']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Reagent costs (cyanide, lime, collectors) are posted to specific GL accounts for AISC C1 cost reporting. reagent_consumption already has cost_centre but lacks GL account reference needed for automated']
  goods_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_issue. Business justification: In mining ERP, reagent consumption triggers a goods issue (SAP movement type 201/261) from warehouse stock. Linking reagent_consumption to goods_issue enables material document reconciliation, invento']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Reagent quality samples (collectors, frothers, flocculants) analyzed at specific labs for specification compliance and batch acceptance testing. Supply chain quality control requirement to ensure reag']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the reagent was consumed. Supports multi-site OPEX reporting and benchmarking.']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Reagent handling activities (cyanide dosing, acid addition, xanthate use) require specific task-level risk assessments beyond the chemical register entry. Mining HSE systems link reagent consumption r']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Reagent consumption is recorded per shift run. One consumption record belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many consumption records. This links the consumption to the processing domain\'s shift ']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which the reagent was consumed. Links to the shift master for date, crew, and time-of-day context.']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reagent master record identifying the specific chemical reagent (collector, frother, flocculant, lime, cyanide, acid, solvent, etc.) consumed.']
  unit_operation_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: Reagent consumption occurs during specific unit operations (flotation, leaching, etc.). Linking reagent_consumption to unit_operation_event provides granular traceability of reagent use to the specifi']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Reagent consumption during maintenance activities (pump repairs, system flushes, reagent line maintenance) is costed against the work order for AISC and opex allocation. Mining cost accountants requir']
  batch_lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier batch or lot number of the reagent consumed. Enables traceability for quality incidents, reagent performance investigations, and environmental compliance audits.']
  consumption_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this consumption record, formatted as RC-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN. Used for cross-referencing with SAP material documents, LIMS records, and environmental compliance reports.']
  consumption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reagent consumption record. Draft records are pending operator confirmation; confirmed records are locked for OPEX reporting; adjusted records have been corrected post-shift; cancelled records are voided.']
  consumption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world date and time at which the reagent consumption event occurred or was recorded by the process control system (OSIsoft PI / SCADA). Distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reagent consumption record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost and total cost fields (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Required for multi-currency OPEX reporting and financial consolidation.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the reagent consumption data: SCADA/OSIsoft PI automated reading, manual operator entry, LabWare LIMS import, SAP ERP goods issue, or estimated value. Critical for data quality assessment and audit trail.']
  dosage_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate at which the reagent was dosed into the circuit, expressed in grams per tonne (g/t) of ore processed or kilograms per hour (kg/hr) depending on the reagent type and circuit. Core metric for reagent optimisation and benchmarking against LOM plan targets.']
  dosage_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the reagent dosage rate field. Common units include grams per tonne (g/t) for solid reagents dosed relative to ore throughput, and kilograms per hour (kg/hr) for liquid reagents dosed by flow rate.']
  environmental_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reagent consumption event requires mandatory environmental compliance reporting (e.g., cyanide usage under ICMC, acid consumption under EIS conditions). Drives automated compliance workflow in IsoMetrix.']
  feed_grade_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Head grade (percentage) of the valuable mineral in the ore feed to the circuit during the shift. Contextualises reagent dosage decisions — higher feed grades may require adjusted reagent rates for optimal recovery.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reagent is classified as hazardous under applicable environmental and safety regulations (e.g., cyanide, sulphuric acid). Triggers mandatory environmental compliance reporting and PPE requirements per ISO 14001 and ISO 45001.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or supervisor notes regarding the reagent consumption event, such as reasons for deviation from planned dosage, equipment issues affecting dosing, or reagent quality observations.']
  ore_throughput_t decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of ore processed through the circuit during the shift. Used as the denominator for calculating specific reagent consumption (g/t) and benchmarking against LOM plan targets.']
  plan_dosage_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Life of Mine (LOM) plan target dosage rate for this reagent in this circuit, in the same unit as dosage_rate. Enables variance analysis between actual and planned reagent consumption for process optimisation.']
  plan_quantity_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Life of Mine (LOM) plan target quantity of reagent for this shift and circuit in kilograms. Used for budget variance reporting and OPEX performance management against the approved mine plan.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP or Pronto Xi purchase order number under which the reagent batch was procured. Enables three-way matching (PO, goods receipt, invoice) and links consumption to procurement contracts for OPEX tracking.']
  quantity_consumed_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total mass of reagent consumed during the shift in kilograms. Primary quantity field for OPEX cost calculation, reagent inventory reconciliation, and environmental compliance reporting of chemical usage volumes.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity consumed field. Defaults to kilograms (kg) for solid reagents; litres (L) for liquid reagents. Enables multi-reagent reporting with consistent unit handling.']
  recovery_rate_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the valuable mineral recovered from the feed ore during the shift in this circuit. Key process performance indicator correlated with reagent dosage to assess reagent effectiveness and optimise consumption.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the shift during which the reagent was consumed. Used for daily and monthly OPEX roll-up reporting and LOM plan variance analysis.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operational shift (day, night, or afternoon) during which the reagent was consumed. Supports shift-level performance analysis and reagent usage pattern identification.']
  slurry_density_g_per_cm3 decimal(6,4) [note: 'Density of the slurry in grams per cubic centimetre at the time of reagent consumption. Influences reagent dispersion and effectiveness; monitored via OSIsoft PI System for process control and optimisation.']
  slurry_ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'pH level of the slurry (water and ore mixture) in the circuit at the time of reagent consumption. Critical process parameter for reagent effectiveness — lime and acid dosage directly controls pH for flotation and leaching circuits.']
  specific_consumption_g_per_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Reagent consumption normalised per dry metric tonne of ore processed (grams per tonne). Calculated as quantity_consumed_kg × 1000 / ore_throughput_t. Key OPEX optimisation metric and LOM plan variance indicator.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supplier who provided the reagent batch consumed. Used for supplier performance management, procurement analysis, and reagent quality investigations.']
  total_cost decimal(16,4) [note: 'Total Operating Expenditure (OPEX) cost of reagent consumed during the shift, calculated as quantity_consumed_kg multiplied by unit_cost. Primary financial metric for reagent OPEX tracking, budget variance, and All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) reporting.']
  unit_cost decimal(14,4) [note: 'Cost per kilogram (or applicable unit) of the reagent at the time of consumption, sourced from the SAP purchase order or standard cost. Used for shift-level OPEX calculation and reagent cost variance analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reagent consumption record was last modified (e.g., post-shift adjustment, supervisor confirmation). Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of reagent consumption per shift per circuit. Captures reagent type (collector, frother, flocculant, lime, cyanide, acid, solvent), dosage rate (g/t or kg/hr), total quantity consumed (kg), unit cost, batch lot number, and circuit applied. Supports reagent cost tracking (OPEX), optimisation, and environmental compliance reporting.'
}

Table processing.slurry_measurement {
  slurry_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each slurry measurement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this time-series observation table. Entity role: EVENT_LOG — append-only stream of immutable slurry parameter observations captured from OSIsoft PI System via OPC-UA at key mineral processing points.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical instrument or sensor asset record in the asset management system (Infor EAM) that generated this measurement. Enables traceability from measurement data to instrument maintenance history, calibration records, and asset lifecycle management. Supports instrument drift detection and predictive maintenance analytics.']
  measurement_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the process measurement point (instrument tag location) where the slurry parameters were captured. Corresponds to the PI tag source location in the OSIsoft PI System, identifying the physical sensor or instrument node in the mineral processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill discharge, flotation feed, leach feed).']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the slurry measurement was recorded. Enables multi-site reporting and benchmarking across processing facilities within the mining operation.']
  process_circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., comminution, flotation, leaching, SX-EW) within the plant where the measurement was taken. Supports circuit-level mass balance and metallurgical accounting.']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Slurry measurements are taken during a production shift run. One measurement belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many measurements. This links the measurement to the processing domain\'s shift ']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this slurry measurement was recorded. Enables shift-level aggregation and reporting of process performance, throughput, and data quality statistics. Links to the shift schedule maintained in SAP S/4HANA HR or the mine operations shift management system.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Slurry measurements at the plant tailings outlet (density, flow rate, pH, solids content) are used for TSF deposition planning and tailings characterisation. Linking measurement points to the receivin']
  alarm_high_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The configured upper alarm threshold for this measurement point in the SCADA/DCS system. When the measured value exceeds this limit, an automated high alarm is generated for operator response. Expressed in the same engineering unit as the measurement. Used for automated alarm generation and process safety management.']
  alarm_low_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The configured lower alarm threshold for this measurement point in the SCADA/DCS system. When the measured value falls below this limit, an automated low alarm is generated for operator response. Expressed in the same engineering unit as the measurement. Used for automated alarm generation and process safety management.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent calibration performed on the instrument or sensor that generated this measurement, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used to assess measurement reliability, identify instruments due for recalibration, and support data quality investigations. Instruments with expired calibration may have their measurements flagged as \'suspect\' in the data quality flag.']
  conductivity_ms_cm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Electrical conductivity of the slurry at the measurement point, expressed in millisiemens per centimetre (mS/cm). Indicator of dissolved ion concentration, relevant for process water quality management, reagent dosing control in flotation, and environmental monitoring of process water circuits.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI System data quality status flag for this measurement record, indicating the reliability of the captured value. \'good\' = valid measurement within instrument range; \'suspect\' = value outside expected range or instrument drift detected; \'bad\' = sensor fault or communication failure; \'substituted\' = value replaced by manual entry or interpolation. Critical for downstream metallurgical mass balance calculations and automated alarm generation. Aligns with OPC-UA quality codes.']
  deviation_from_setpoint decimal(18,6) [note: 'The arithmetic difference between the actual measured value and the configured process setpoint value (measured_value minus setpoint_value), expressed in the same engineering unit. A non-derived operational field captured directly from the PI System control deviation tag. Positive values indicate measurement above setpoint; negative values indicate below setpoint. Used for process control performance monitoring and alarm root cause analysis.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measurement associated with the primary measurement value for this PI tag (e.g., t/m³, %w/w, m³/hr, pH, °C, cP, mS/cm, µm). Sourced from the PI tag engineering unit configuration. Supports dynamic unit-aware analytics and cross-tag comparisons in the metallurgical historian.']
  flow_rate_m3_hr decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of the slurry stream at the measurement point, expressed in cubic metres per hour (m³/hr). Measured by electromagnetic or Coriolis flow meter. Used in metallurgical mass balance, throughput calculations, and pump performance monitoring. Fundamental input for recovery rate and concentrate production calculations.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (UTC) at which this measurement record was ingested into the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer from the OSIsoft PI System source. Distinct from the measurement_timestamp (when the physical measurement occurred). Used for data pipeline latency monitoring, SLA tracking, and identifying late-arriving data in streaming ingestion pipelines.']
  is_alarm_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether an active process alarm condition existed at this measurement point at the time of measurement (True = alarm active; False = no alarm). Sourced from the SCADA/DCS alarm management system via OSIsoft PI System event frames. Enables alarm frequency analysis, process stability assessment, and operator response time reporting.']
  is_in_control boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the measurement value was within the defined process control limits (alarm_low_limit to alarm_high_limit) at the time of measurement (True = in control; False = out of control). Supports Statistical Process Control (SPC) analysis, process stability KPIs, and automated metallurgical mass balance quality gates.']
  mass_flow_rate_t_hr decimal(12,4) [note: 'Mass flow rate of dry solids in the slurry stream, expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (t/hr). Calculated from volumetric flow rate and slurry density adjusted for percent solids. Primary throughput metric for metallurgical accounting, LOM (Life of Mine) plan tracking, and production reporting against targets.']
  measurement_point_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the physical location and purpose of the measurement point within the processing circuit (e.g., \'SAG Mill 01 Discharge Slurry\', \'Flotation Feed Box\', \'CIL Tank 3 Overflow\'). Supports operator interpretation and alarm investigation.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (UTC) at which the slurry parameter observation was recorded by the OSIsoft PI System historian. This is the principal event time for the measurement, distinct from record ingestion timestamps. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Critical for time-series trending, process control, and metallurgical mass balance calculations.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical parameter being measured at this process point. Identifies which slurry property this record represents, enabling filtering and aggregation by parameter type across the processing circuit. Aligns with PI tag attribute classification in the OSIsoft PI System. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: density|percent_solids|flow_rate|ph|temperature|viscosity|conductivity|particle_size|mass_flow — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_calibration_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next calibration of the instrument or sensor, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used to proactively manage instrument maintenance schedules via Infor EAM and to flag measurements from instruments approaching or past their calibration due date. Supports data quality governance and regulatory compliance for measurement traceability.']
  opc_ua_node_reference varchar [note: 'The OPC-UA (OPC Unified Architecture) node identifier for the data source that feeds this measurement into the OSIsoft PI System. Provides the source system address for data lineage, integration troubleshooting, and SCADA connectivity management. Format follows OPC-UA NodeId specification (e.g., ns=2;s=SITE.AREA.UNIT.TAG).']
  particle_size_p80_um decimal(10,3) [note: 'The 80th percentile passing particle size of the solid fraction in the slurry, expressed in micrometres (µm). Key comminution performance indicator for SAG mill and HPGR circuits. P80 is the industry-standard metric for grind size control, directly impacting downstream flotation and leaching recovery rates.']
  percent_solids decimal(6,3) [note: 'Weight percentage of solid material in the slurry, expressed as percent weight-by-weight (%w/w). Derived from slurry density and known ore specific gravity. A key process control parameter for comminution (SAG, HPGR), flotation, and leaching circuits. Typical operating range: 20–75 %w/w depending on circuit type.']
  ph_value decimal(5,3) [note: 'Measured pH of the slurry at the measurement point on the standard pH scale (0–14). Critical process control parameter for flotation (reagent effectiveness), leaching (acid/alkaline conditions), and cyanide destruction circuits. Monitored continuously to ensure optimal mineral recovery and environmental compliance. Typical operating range varies by circuit: flotation 8–11, acid leach 1.5–2.5.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System tag identifier (instrument tag name) associated with this measurement point. Serves as the source system natural key enabling traceability back to the PI historian tag configuration and SCADA/OPC-UA data source.
