Format follows site-specific PI tag naming conventions (e.g., SITE.AREA.UNIT.PARAMETER).']
  process_area_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the functional process area within the mineral processing plant where the measurement was taken (e.g., COMM for comminution, FLOT for flotation, LEACH for leaching, SXEW for SX-EW, THCK for thickening). Supports area-level operational reporting and alarm management.']
  raw_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The unprocessed, unvalidated measurement value as received directly from the OSIsoft PI System historian before any quality filtering, engineering unit conversion, or substitution is applied. Preserved for audit, instrument calibration verification, and data quality investigation. Enables comparison between raw and processed values to detect instrument drift.']
  sample_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'The configured sampling or scan interval for this PI tag in seconds, indicating how frequently the OSIsoft PI System captures a new measurement value (e.g., 1, 5, 10, 30, 60 seconds). Used to assess data density, identify gaps in time-series data, and configure alarm thresholds appropriately.']
  sensor_status_code varchar [note: 'Instrument or sensor status code as reported by the SCADA/OPC-UA system at the time of measurement. Provides granular diagnostic information beyond the data quality flag, including specific fault codes (e.g., COMM_FAIL, RANGE_HI, RANGE_LO, CAL_DUE, OFFLINE, NORMAL). Used for instrument maintenance scheduling via Infor EAM and sensor fault detection analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NORMAL|COMM_FAIL|RANGE_HI|RANGE_LO|CAL_DUE|OFFLINE|FROZEN|DRIFT — promote to reference product]']
  setpoint_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The target or desired setpoint value for this measurement parameter as configured in the DCS/SCADA process control system. Expressed in the same engineering unit as the measurement. Enables deviation analysis between actual measured values and process control targets, supporting process optimisation and LOM plan performance tracking.']
  slurry_density_t_m3 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured density of the slurry (water and ore mixture) at the measurement point, expressed in tonnes per cubic metre (t/m³). Captured from nuclear density gauge or Coriolis flow meter via OPC-UA. Critical parameter for metallurgical mass balance calculations, pump sizing, and process control. Typical range for mineral processing slurries: 1.0–2.2 t/m³.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The natural key or record identifier from the OSIsoft PI System historian for this specific measurement event (e.g., PI event frame ID or compressed archive record reference). Enables exact traceability back to the source historian record for data lineage, audit, and reconciliation purposes in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  substitution_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a measurement value was substituted when data_quality_flag = \'substituted\'. Values: \'manual_entry\' = operator manually entered value; \'interpolated\' = value interpolated from adjacent timestamps; \'last_known_good\' = previous valid value carried forward; \'calculated\' = derived from related measurements; \'not_applicable\' = no substitution occurred. Required for metallurgical mass balance audit trail and data governance compliance.']
  temperature_c decimal(7,3) [note: 'Measured temperature of the slurry at the measurement point, expressed in degrees Celsius (°C). Relevant for leaching kinetics (heap leach, pressure oxidation), reagent reaction rates, and viscosity calculations. Monitored for process optimisation and equipment protection (e.g., pump seal integrity).']
  viscosity_cp decimal(10,4) [note: 'Dynamic viscosity of the slurry at the measurement point, expressed in centipoise (cP). Affects pump performance, pipeline transport efficiency, and flotation cell hydrodynamics. Elevated viscosity can indicate high clay content or incorrect percent solids, impacting comminution and separation efficiency.']

  Note: 'Time-series measurement record of slurry parameters at key process points captured from OSIsoft PI System via OPC-UA. Captures measurement point ID, timestamp, slurry density (t/m³), percent solids (%w/w), flow rate (m³/hr), pH, temperature (°C), viscosity (cP), data quality flag (good/suspect/bad/substituted), and sensor status code. Data quality flags enable detection of sensor faults, instrument drift, and substituted values for downstream metallurgical mass balance calculations. Enables real-time process control, historian trending, and automated alarm generation.'
}

  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Concentrate batches are produced during a production shift run. One batch belongs to one shift run; one shift run produces many batches. This links the batch to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']

Business justification: Concentrate batches must be traced to source tenements for royalty payment calculations based on production value, regulatory reporting by tenement, and sales contract compliance requiring origin cert']

Business justification: Concentrate batches must meet contractual specifications for grade, moisture, impurities. Critical for quality control, sales contract compliance, and penalty/bonus calculations. Complements existing ']
  waste_rock_dump_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.waste_rock_dump. Business justification: Some concentrate production processes generate waste rock co-products (e.g., gravity separation rejects) sent to waste rock dumps. Material tracking requirement for inventory reconciliation and waste ']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the concentrate batch, used across sales, logistics, and laboratory systems. Format: commodity prefix, year, and sequential number (e.g., CU-2024-000123). Sourced from LabWare LIMS batch numbering.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the concentrate batch through the production-to-dispatch workflow: in_progress (being produced), sampled (sample taken for assay), assayed (lab results received), approved (quality approved for sale), dispatched (shipped to customer), rejected (failed quality control). Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'The primary commodity contained in this concentrate batch (e.g., copper, iron ore, lithium, nickel, coal). Drives downstream sales, logistics, and regulatory reporting classification.']
  concentrate_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The grade of the primary commodity in the final concentrate product for this batch, expressed as a percentage (% Cu, % Fe, % Li, % Ni). The key product quality metric used in sales contracts and customer specifications. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this concentrate batch record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and governance compliance.']
  dispatch_mode varchar [note: 'The primary transport mode used to dispatch this concentrate batch from the processing plant to the port or customer (e.g., rail, road, ship, pipeline, conveyor). Determines logistics cost allocation and supply chain planning.']
  dry_weight_dmt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Dry weight of the concentrate batch in dry metric tonnes (DMT), calculated after moisture correction. The primary commercial weight measure used for sales contracts, invoicing, and production reporting. Key SSOT field for concentrate production output.']
  energy_consumption_kwh_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Electrical energy consumed per tonne of ore processed for this concentrate batch, expressed in kilowatt-hours per tonne (kWh/t). Sourced from OSIsoft PI System. Key OPEX and sustainability metric tracked against LOM plan targets.']
  feed_tonnes decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed into the processing circuit to produce this concentrate batch. Used as the denominator in recovery rate and yield calculations.']
  head_grade_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The grade of the primary commodity in the feed ore (Run of Mine (ROM)) entering the processing circuit for this batch, expressed as a percentage (%). Represents the input quality before beneficiation. Used for recovery rate calculation and reconciliation.']
  impurity_silica_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Silica (SiO2) content in the concentrate batch expressed as a percentage (%). A key gangue impurity that affects product quality, smelter penalties, and customer acceptance. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results.']
  impurity_sulfur_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Sulfur (S) content in the concentrate batch expressed as a percentage (%). A critical impurity for smelter operations and environmental compliance. High sulfur levels may attract penalties or rejection. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  is_quality_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this concentrate batch has passed quality control review and been formally approved for sale or dispatch. True = approved; False = pending or rejected. Set by the metallurgist or quality manager in LabWare LIMS.']
  moisture_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Moisture content of the concentrate batch expressed as a percentage (%) of wet weight. Determined by laboratory analysis per ISO 3087. Critical for DMT calculation and product quality compliance.']
  particle_size_p80_um decimal(10,4) [note: 'The P80 particle size of the concentrate batch in micrometres (µm), representing the sieve size through which 80% of the material passes. Critical comminution (size reduction) quality parameter affecting downstream processing efficiency and product specification compliance.']
  process_route varchar [note: 'The mineral processing route used to produce this concentrate batch (e.g., flotation, leaching, Dense Media Separation (DMS), Solvent Extraction Electrowinning (SX-EW), High Pressure Grinding Rolls (HPGR), Semi-Autogenous Grinding (SAG)). Determines applicable quality standards and reagent profiles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flotation|leaching|DMS|SX-EW|HPGR|SAG|gravity|magnetic_separation — promote to reference product]']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this concentrate batch was produced at the processing plant. Used for daily production reporting and reconciliation against the production schedule.']
  production_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the concentrate batch production was completed and the batch was sealed/finalised at the processing plant. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian.']
  provisional_price_usd_dmt decimal(18,4) [note: 'The provisional sale price per dry metric tonne (DMT) in US dollars (USD) agreed at the time of dispatch, subject to final price adjustment based on quotation period commodity prices. Used for initial revenue recognition under IFRS 15.']
  quality_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the concentrate batch was formally approved for sale following quality control review. Represents a key lifecycle milestone in the batch workflow. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.']
  quality_grade varchar [note: 'The quality classification assigned to this concentrate batch based on assay results and product specification compliance. Grade A = premium specification met; Grade B = standard specification met; Grade C = marginal specification; off_spec = outside specification but saleable; rejected = failed quality control and cannot be sold.']
  reagent_consumption_kg_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total reagent consumption per tonne of ore processed for this concentrate batch, expressed in kilograms per tonne (kg/t). Aggregates all flotation, leaching, or other process reagents. Used for operating expenditure (OPEX) tracking and process efficiency analysis.']
  recovery_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The percentage of the valuable mineral in the feed ore that is successfully recovered into the concentrate product for this batch. Calculated as (concentrate grade × DMT) / (head grade × feed tonnes) × 100. Key processing efficiency KPI tracked against LOM plan targets.']
  sample_date date [note: 'The date on which the representative sample was taken from this concentrate batch for laboratory assay analysis. Used for sample chain-of-custody tracking and quality control compliance.']
  slurry_density_gcm3 decimal(8,4) [note: 'The density of the concentrate slurry (water and ore mixture) in grams per cubic centimetre (g/cm³) at the point of batch finalisation. Monitored via OSIsoft PI System SCADA. Used for process control and dewatering circuit optimisation.']
  throughput_tph decimal(10,4) [note: 'The average ore processing throughput rate in tonnes per hour (tph) achieved during the production of this concentrate batch. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System SCADA historian. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) and capacity utilisation analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this concentrate batch record was last modified. Audit trail field used for change tracking, data lineage, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  water_consumption_m3_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Process water consumed per tonne of ore processed for this concentrate batch, expressed in cubic metres per tonne (m³/t). Critical environmental compliance metric reported under ISO 14001 and GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement.']
  wet_weight_t decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total wet weight of the concentrate batch in metric tonnes (t) as measured at the processing plant prior to moisture correction. Used as the basis for moisture content calculation.']
  yield_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The mass yield of concentrate produced as a percentage of the total ore feed for this batch (DMT concentrate / feed tonnes × 100). Indicates the ratio of product output to ore input and is used for process efficiency benchmarking.']

  Note: 'Master record of each concentrate production batch produced at the processing plant. Captures batch number, commodity type (copper concentrate, iron ore fines, lithium hydroxide), production date, dry weight (dmt), moisture content (%), head grade, concentrate grade (% Cu, % Fe, % Li), and recovery rate (%). SSOT for concentrate production output linked to sales and product domains.'
}

Table processing.metallurgical_balance {
  metallurgical_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each metallurgical balance record in the processing domain. Primary key for the metallurgical_balance data product.']

  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Metallurgical balances are commodity-specific for mass/metal accounting. Essential for JORC compliance, resource reconciliation, and ore reserve reporting. Replaces denormalized commodity text field. ']

Core mining finance process linking technical metallurgica']
  geological_domain_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain.   mining_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mining_block. Direct link to royalty_obligati']

Business justification: Metallurgical balance periods are reconciled per saleable product for financial reporting and production accounting. commodity_id alone is insufficient — a plant producing both copper cathode and copp']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Metallurgical balances reconcile production against tenement reserves and are required for statutory reporting by tenement to regulatory authorities. Essential for reserve depletion tracking and compl']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Metallurgical balance periods must reconcile tailings_tonnes_dry sent to specific TSF. Fundamental accounting control for mass balance closure, regulatory reporting, and TSF capacity verification. Lin']
  accounting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the metallurgical accounting period covered by this balance record. Together with accounting_period_start defines the reconciliation window.']
  accounting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the metallurgical accounting period covered by this balance record (daily, weekly, or monthly). Aligns with the financial reporting calendar.']
  adjustment_authoriser varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the competent person or authorised metallurgist who approved the adjustment or restatement of this balance record. Required for JORC-compliant audit trail and regulatory sign-off.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for any adjustment or restatement applied to this version of the metallurgical balance (e.g., assay rerun, sampling error correction, instrument calibration update, stockpile reweigh). Required for audit trail and competent person sign-off.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this metallurgical balance record was formally approved and signed off by the competent person. Marks the transition to approved status in the balance workflow.']
  balance_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the metallurgical balance record, used in reports, sign-off documentation, and regulatory submissions. Format: METBAL-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the metallurgical balance record: draft (in preparation), submitted (awaiting review), under_review (being assessed by competent person), approved (signed off), or rejected (returned for correction).']
  concentrate_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Assayed grade of the concentrate product expressed in the same unit as feed_grade_unit. Determines the commercial value and smelter payability of the concentrate.']
  concentrate_tonnes_dry decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of concentrate product produced during the accounting period. Represents the valuable mineral product output from the processing circuit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this metallurgical balance record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  dilution_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Estimated percentage of waste rock (gangue) dilution in the feed material, representing the proportion of non-ore material included during mining and haulage. Impacts feed grade and recovery.']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total electrical energy consumed by the processing circuit during the accounting period in kilowatt hours. Key OPEX component and sustainability metric reported under GRI and ICMM frameworks.']
  feed_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Assayed head grade of the feed material expressed as percentage (%) for base metals or grams per tonne (g/t) for precious metals. Determined by LabWare LIMS assay results on feed samples.']
  feed_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the feed head grade value: pct (percentage), g_per_t (grams per tonne), kg_per_t (kilograms per tonne), or ppm (parts per million). Varies by commodity.']
  feed_moisture_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Moisture content of the feed material as a percentage of wet weight. Used to convert wet tonnes to dry tonnes for metallurgical accounting purposes.']
  feed_tonnes_dry decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed to the processing circuit during the accounting period. Dry weight basis is the standard for metallurgical accounting.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record is the current (latest approved) version of the metallurgical balance for the given period and circuit. True = current version; False = superseded version retained for audit trail.']
  mass_balance_closure_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Mass balance closure expressed as a percentage: (concentrate_tonnes_dry + tailings_tonnes_dry) / feed_tonnes_dry × 100. Values outside 98–102% typically trigger investigation and adjustment.']
  measured_recovery_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Actual metallurgical recovery percentage calculated from assayed feed, concentrate, and tailings grades and tonnes using the standard two-product formula. Represents the proportion of valuable mineral recovered into concentrate.']
  metal_units_feed decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total contained metal units in the feed stream for the period, calculated as feed_tonnes_dry × feed_grade. Expressed in tonnes of metal or troy ounces depending on commodity. Key input to JORC resource-to-reserve reconciliation.']
  metal_units_recovered decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total contained metal units recovered into concentrate for the period, calculated as concentrate_tonnes_dry × concentrate_grade. Used in LOM plan tracking and sales reconciliation.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Geological ore type classification of the feed material processed during the period (e.g., oxide, sulphide, transitional, fresh, weathered). Ore type significantly influences metallurgical recovery and reagent consumption.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the metallurgical accounting period: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annual. Determines the reporting frequency and aggregation level for LOM plan tracking.']
  plant_availability_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the accounting period during which the processing plant was available for production (scheduled hours minus planned and unplanned downtime). Component of Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE).']
  reagent_consumption_kg_per_t decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total reagent consumption per dry metric tonne of feed processed during the period, aggregated across all reagents (collectors, frothers, pH modifiers, leach reagents). Key OPEX driver for processing cost reporting.']
  recovery_variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Difference between measured_recovery_pct and theoretical_recovery_pct (measured minus theoretical). Positive value indicates outperformance; negative indicates underperformance against LOM plan.']
  tailings_grade decimal(10,4) [note: 'Assayed grade of the tailings stream expressed in the same unit as feed_grade_unit. Indicates the residual valuable mineral content lost to tailings; key input to recovery calculation and environmental reporting.']
  tailings_tonnes_dry decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total dry metric tonnes of tailings (gangue and process waste) discharged to the Tailings Storage Facility (TSF) during the accounting period. Used in mass balance closure and TSF capacity planning.']
  theoretical_recovery_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Theoretical or planned recovery percentage for the period based on LOM plan targets, ore type characterisation, and process design parameters. Used as the benchmark for variance analysis.']
  throughput_rate_tph decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average processing throughput rate in dry metric tonnes per hour during the accounting period. Key operational KPI for plant performance against design capacity and LOM plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this metallurgical balance record was last modified. Updated on every version increment, adjustment, or status change. Supports audit trail requirements.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this metallurgical balance record for the given period and circuit. Increments with each adjustment or restatement, supporting full audit trail and competent person sign-off requirements.']
  water_consumption_m3 decimal(14,3) [note: 'Total fresh water consumed by the processing circuit during the accounting period in cubic metres. Reported for environmental compliance under ISO 14001 and GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement.']

  Note: 'Periodic (daily/weekly/monthly) metallurgical accounting reconciliation record for a processing circuit or plant. Captures accounting period, feed tonnes, feed grade, concentrate tonnes, concentrate grade, tailings tonnes, tailings grade, calculated recovery (%), theoretical recovery, reconciliation variance, adjustment reason, adjustment authoriser, and version number for audit trail. Supports JORC-compliant resource-to-reserve reconciliation, LOM plan tracking, and regulatory reporting. Versioned records with adjustment metadata provide the audit trail required for metallurgical balance sign-off by competent persons.'
}

Table processing.recovery_target {
  recovery_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each recovery target record in the LOM plan-derived processing circuit performance baseline.']
  block_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.block_model. Business justification: Recovery targets are derived from block model grade distributions and metallurgical domain assignments during LOM planning. Mine planners use block model data to set target_recovery_pct and target_hea']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, SX-EW circuit, DMS circuit) for which this recovery target applies.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Recovery targets are set per commodity in LOM plans and feasibility studies. Core to mine planning, economic modeling, and performance tracking. Replaces denormalized commodity text field with proper ']
  geological_domain_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Recovery targets are set per geological domain because oxide, sulphide, and transitional domains have fundamentally different metallurgical recovery characteristics. Mine planners set target_recovery_']
  grade_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: Recovery targets are set per grade parameter — Cu recovery target differs from Au recovery target even in the same circuit. LOM planning requires recovery targets to reference the specific grade param']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version from which this recovery target is derived. Enables traceability of targets back to the authorised planning document.']
  metallurgical_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.metallurgical_test. Business justification: Recovery targets are derived from and must be traceable to specific metallurgical test results (flotation kinetics, leach extraction curves, grind-recovery relationships). The feasibility_study_ref pl']
  mining_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mining_block. Business justification: Recovery targets vary by mining block ore type, grade, and metallurgical domain for domain-based planning, ore type scheduling, and metallurgical performance forecasting. Critical for LOM planning wit']
  ore_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.ore_reserve. Business justification: Recovery targets are modifying factors applied to ore reserves under JORC/NI43-101 reporting standards. Metallurgists set recovery targets directly from ore reserve grade and mineralogy assumptions. C']
  orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Recovery targets are set per orebody based on geological characteristics, mineralogy, and metallurgical testwork. Life-of-mine planning requires orebody-specific recovery assumptions to accurately for']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the circuit operates. Supports multi-site recovery target management.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Processing recovery targets are set during budget and LOM planning at profit centre level. Linking enables variance reporting between budgeted recovery targets and actual metallurgical balance results']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Recovery targets are set per ore source/prospect because different prospects have distinct metallurgical characteristics (mineralogy, hardness, grade variability). Plants processing ore from multiple ']
  regulatory_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Mining licences frequently impose minimum metallurgical recovery conditions on tenement holders. Processing recovery targets are set to comply with these regulatory conditions. Compliance reporting re']
  resource_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.resource_estimate. Business justification: Recovery targets are derived from resource estimate metallurgical assumptions. Critical for LOM planning, economic modeling, and feasibility study validation. Real business need: linking processing ta']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Recovery targets are set per saleable product — copper cathode recovery targets differ from copper concentrate targets even from the same ore. LOM planning and feasibility studies set recovery targets']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Recovery targets are set in the context of a specific product specification — the target grade and impurity limits define the required recovery percentage. LOM planning and feasibility studies explici']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Recovery targets are set per tenement because ore characteristics and LOM economics are tenement-specific. Feasibility studies and LOM planning require recovery targets attributed to the tenement from']
  volume_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.volume_plan. Business justification: Production-to-sales volume planning alignment is a core mining commercial process: the sales volume plan is built directly from processing recovery targets (planned throughput, recovery rate, concentr']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Competent Person or authorised technical authority who approved this recovery target for use in LOM planning and variance reporting. Required for JORC/NI 43-101 traceability.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recovery target was formally approved by the authorised Competent Person or planning authority. Provides audit trail for regulatory and governance compliance.']
  basis_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the technical study or planning document type that forms the basis for this recovery target (e.g., LOM plan, definitive feasibility study, pre-feasibility study, scoping study, annual budget). Indicates the confidence level and authority of the target.']
  concentrate_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target concentrate grade value (e.g., pct for percentage, g_per_t for grams per tonne). Ensures correct interpretation of the concentrate quality specification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recovery target record was first created in the data platform. Provides audit trail for data lineage and governance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this recovery target version becomes the active baseline for variance analysis and performance reporting. Supports Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) Type 2 versioning of LOM plan targets.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this recovery target version ceases to be the active baseline, typically when superseded by a revised LOM plan. Null indicates the record is currently active. Supports SCD Type 2 versioning.']
  feasibility_study_ref varchar [note: 'Document reference number or title of the feasibility study, pre-feasibility study (PFS), or definitive feasibility study (DFS) from which this recovery target is derived. Provides audit traceability to the authorised technical study.']
  head_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target head grade value (e.g., pct for percentage, g_per_t for grams per tonne, ppm for parts per million). Ensures correct interpretation of the grade figure across different commodities.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the current active version of the recovery target for the given circuit, commodity, ore type, and period. Supports efficient querying of the latest approved targets without filtering on version history.']
  lom_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the LOM plan from which this target is derived (e.g., LOM-2024-v3.1). Enables tracking of target changes across successive LOM plan revisions and supports variance analysis against the correct plan baseline.']
  metal_units_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target contained metal units (e.g., t for metric tonnes, oz for troy ounces, lb for pounds). Ensures correct interpretation across different commodity reporting conventions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording planning assumptions, metallurgical test work caveats, ore type blending assumptions, or other contextual information relevant to the interpretation of this recovery target.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ore type or metallurgical domain for which this target applies (e.g., oxide, sulphide, transitional, mixed, fresh). Ore type significantly influences achievable recovery rates and processing parameters.']
  processing_route varchar [note: 'The primary mineral processing method or beneficiation route for which this target applies (e.g., flotation, Dense Media Separation (DMS), leaching, Solvent Extraction Electrowinning (SX-EW), High Pressure Grinding Rolls (HPGR), Semi-Autogenous Grinding (SAG)). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flotation|leaching|dms|sxew|gravity|magnetic_separation|hpgr|sag|cil|cip|other — promote to reference product]']
  target_concentrate_grade decimal(10,5) [note: 'Planned grade of the final concentrate or product output from the processing circuit during the target period, expressed in the unit defined by concentrate_grade_unit. Represents the quality specification for the saleable product.']
  target_concentrate_kt decimal(12,3) [note: 'Planned total dry metric tonnes of concentrate (in kilotonnes) to be produced during the target period. Supports sales planning, logistics scheduling, and LOM revenue forecasting.']
  target_energy_consumption_kwh decimal(14,3) [note: 'Planned total electrical energy consumption in kilowatt-hours for the processing circuit during the target period. Used for energy cost budgeting, carbon footprint reporting, and OPEX modelling.']
  target_feed_moisture_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned moisture content of the Run of Mine (ROM) feed entering the processing circuit, expressed as a percentage. Affects dry tonne calculations, throughput, and reagent dosing. Derived from ore characterisation in the feasibility study.']
  target_head_grade decimal(10,5) [note: 'Planned feed grade (head grade) of the ore entering the processing circuit during the target period, expressed in the unit defined by head_grade_unit. Derived from the mine plan and geological resource model.']
  target_mass_pull_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned mass pull percentage — the proportion of feed mass reporting to the concentrate stream. A key flotation circuit design parameter that balances recovery against concentrate grade. Derived from metallurgical test work in the feasibility study.']
  target_metal_units decimal(14,3) [note: 'Planned quantity of contained metal units to be recovered during the target period, expressed in the unit defined by metal_units_unit (e.g., tonnes of copper, ounces of gold). Core metric for LOM production target and revenue planning.']
  target_period_end date [note: 'End date of the planning period to which this recovery target applies. Defines the upper boundary of the target window for performance measurement and LOM plan variance reporting.']
  target_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the planning period to which this recovery target applies. Combined with target_period_end to define the exact window for variance analysis against actual metallurgical balance results.']
  target_period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the planning period for which this recovery target is defined: monthly, quarterly, annual, or full Life of Mine (LOM). Determines the temporal resolution of variance analysis.']
  target_plant_availability_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned plant availability percentage for the processing circuit during the target period, representing the proportion of scheduled time the plant is available for production. Used in conjunction with throughput targets to derive planned production volumes.']
  target_reagent_consumption decimal(10,4) [note: 'Planned total reagent consumption rate in kilograms per dry metric tonne of ore processed. Aggregated across all reagents for the circuit. Used for OPEX budgeting and reagent procurement planning.']
  target_recovery_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned metallurgical recovery rate expressed as a percentage of valuable mineral recovered from feed to final product (concentrate or cathode). Derived from LOM plan or feasibility study metallurgical test work. Core KPI for processing performance variance analysis.']
  target_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this recovery target record, used in planning documents, feasibility studies, and variance reporting (e.g., RT-CU-FL-202401).']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this recovery target record: draft (under preparation), approved (authorised for use in variance reporting), superseded (replaced by a newer version), or archived (no longer active).']
  target_throughput_kt decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total planned dry metric tonnes (in kilotonnes) to be processed through the circuit during the target period. Complements the rate-based tph target for period-level production planning and LOM schedule adherence.']
  target_throughput_tph decimal(10,3) [note: 'Planned processing throughput rate in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph) for the circuit during the target period. Used to assess whether actual mill throughput meets LOM plan expectations.']
  target_utilisation_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planned plant utilisation percentage — the proportion of available time the plant is actually operating and processing ore. Distinct from availability; utilisation reflects operational efficiency within available time.']
  target_water_consumption_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Planned total water consumption in cubic metres for the processing circuit during the target period. Supports water balance management, environmental licence compliance, and sustainability reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recovery target record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'LOM plan-derived recovery rate targets per processing circuit, commodity, and ore type. Captures target period (month/quarter/year), circuit ID, ore type, target recovery (%), target throughput (tph), target head grade, target concentrate grade, and basis (LOM plan version, feasibility study reference). Enables variance analysis between actual and planned recovery performance.'
}

Table processing.operational_exception {
  operational_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for operational_exception']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary piece of equipment directly involved in or causing the operational exception. Links to the equipment asset register.']
  blast_execution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.blast_execution. Business justification: Processing upsets (crusher blockages, mill overloads, poor fragmentation) are frequently caused by blast quality issues. Linking operational exceptions to the originating blast enables blast-to-plant ']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific processing circuit affected by the exception (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit, SX-EW circuit, DMS circuit). Links to the circuit master.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Operational exceptions (unplanned downtime, process upsets) cause production losses that must be costed and reported against cost centres for variance analysis, insurance claims, and OEE cost impact r']
  downtime_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.downtime_event. Business justification: Processing operational exceptions (circuit trips, process upsets) are directly caused by or cause equipment downtime events. Linking them supports cross-domain root cause analysis, OEE loss categorisa']
  failure_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.failure_report. Business justification: Processing operational exceptions (mill trips, pump failures, process upsets) directly generate maintenance failure reports for the same event. Mining operations use this cross-domain link for integra']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Operational exceptions (downtime, process upsets) occur at specific functional locations. Links exceptions to maintenance functional location hierarchy for root cause analysis, maintenance planning, a']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Processing plant downtime events that trigger HSE incidents must reference the formal incident record for compliance reporting and root cause analysis. The current hse_incident_reference (string) shou']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which production loss from this exception is measured. Enables variance reporting between actual downtime impact and LOM plan targets.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mineral processing plant where the operational exception occurred. Links to the plant master record.']
  regulatory_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Process upsets and environmental exceedances at a plant may constitute breaches of tenement regulatory conditions, triggering mandatory regulatory reporting. Regulators require that each notifiable op']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Operational exceptions (process upsets, control deviations) are reviewed against the governing risk assessment to determine if critical controls were breached. Mining process safety management require']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Operational exceptions occur during a production shift run. One exception belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many exceptions. This links the exception to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shift report during which this operational exception was recorded. Links to the mine or plant shift report.']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Processing plant operational exceptions are frequently caused by TSF constraints — tailings line blockages, TSF freeboard limits forcing throughput curtailment, or TSF pump failures. Linking exception']
  unit_operation_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: Operational exceptions (unplanned downtime, process upsets, alarm events) occur during specific unit operation events. Linking operational_exception to unit_operation_event provides direct traceabilit']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work order raised in response to this operational exception. Enables linkage to the maintenance domain for corrective or emergency work orders.']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The measured actual value of the process parameter at the time of the deviation or alarm event. Compared against setpoint_value to determine deviation magnitude and against alarm limits for threshold breach analysis.']
  alarm_limit_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'The configured high alarm threshold for the process parameter at the time of the event. Used to contextualise whether the actual_value breached the high alarm boundary and to support alarm rationalisation analysis.']
  alarm_limit_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'The configured low alarm threshold for the process parameter at the time of the event. Used to contextualise whether the actual_value breached the low alarm boundary.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action taken by the operator or maintenance team to resolve the operational exception and restore normal plant operations. Feeds maintenance knowledge base and SOP review processes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operational exception record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and record provenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the operational exception was first detected: scada_alarm (automated SCADA/PLC alarm), operator_observation (manual observation by plant operator), dcs_alert (Distributed Control System alert), lab_result (assay or process sample result), inspection (physical inspection finding), predictive_alert (predictive maintenance or process analytics alert).']
  deviation_magnitude decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute difference between actual_value and setpoint_value at the time of the exception. Stored as a business field to support process control quality analysis and trend reporting without requiring arithmetic on raw values.']
  duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed duration of the operational exception in minutes, calculated from event_start_timestamp to event_end_timestamp. Stored as a business field to support OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) availability loss reporting without requiring real-time timestamp arithmetic.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'Operational category of the exception cause: mechanical (equipment failure), electrical (power or instrumentation fault), process (reagent, slurry, or chemistry issue), planned_maintenance (scheduled shutdown), feed_shortage (insufficient ROM feed), or parameter_exceedance (process variable breach). Drives OEE loss categorisation and maintenance domain routing.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operational exception event was resolved and normal operations resumed. Null if the event is still active. Used with event_start_timestamp to calculate event duration.']
  event_reference varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the operational exception event, used for cross-referencing with maintenance work orders, shift reports, and process control logs. Format: OE-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operational exception event commenced, as recorded by the SCADA/PLC system or operator. This is the principal real-world event time used for duration calculation and OEE loss attribution.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the operational exception record: open (event active, investigation pending), in_progress (corrective action underway), resolved (root cause identified and corrective action completed), closed (verified and signed off), cancelled (duplicate or incorrectly raised).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operational exception by its fundamental nature: downtime (equipment or circuit stoppage), parameter_deviation (process variable outside set-point tolerance), alarm (SCADA/PLC alarm triggered), near_miss (safety or process near-miss), or process_upset (uncontrolled process condition). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: downtime|parameter_deviation|alarm|near_miss|process_upset — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']
  hse_incident_raised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operational exception also triggered a formal HSE incident report in the IsoMetrix system (True) or not (False). Enables cross-domain linkage between process exceptions and HSE incident management.']
  immediate_action varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate containment action taken at the time of the exception to prevent further production loss, equipment damage, or safety risk, prior to full corrective action. Distinct from corrective_action which addresses root cause.']
  is_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime or exception event was planned (True) or unplanned (False). Planned events include scheduled maintenance shutdowns and planned feed stoppages. Used to segregate planned vs unplanned downtime in OEE calculations.']
  oee_loss_category varchar [note: 'OEE loss category to which this exception is attributed for OEE calculation purposes: availability (equipment or circuit not running), performance (running below rated throughput), or quality (product not meeting grade specification). Aligns with ISO 22400 OEE decomposition.']
  parameter_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the process parameter that deviated, providing context beyond the SCADA tag code. Examples: SAG Mill Feed Rate (tph), Flotation Cell Level (%), Leach Tank pH.']
  parameter_tag varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI System or SCADA tag identifier for the process parameter that deviated from its set-point. Applicable only for event_type = parameter_deviation or alarm. Examples: SAG_MILL_FEED_TPH, FLOTATION_CELL_LEVEL_PCT, LEACH_TANK_PH.']
  parameter_unit varchar [note: 'Engineering unit of measure for the process parameter set-point and actual values. Examples: tph (tonnes per hour), %, pH, m3/h, kPa, degC, g/L. Ensures correct interpretation of setpoint_value and actual_value.']
  production_loss_tonnes decimal(18,3) [note: 'Estimated ore or concentrate production lost in dry metric tonnes as a direct consequence of this operational exception. Used for OEE production loss attribution, LOM plan variance reporting, and OPEX impact quantification.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code classifying the root cause of the exception per the plant\'s failure taxonomy (e.g., ISO 14224 cause codes). Enables structured root cause analysis and trend reporting across events.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the identified root cause of the operational exception, as determined through investigation. Supports root cause analysis (RCA), recurring failure identification, and process improvement initiatives.']
  setpoint_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The target or set-point value for the process parameter at the time of the deviation event. Used in conjunction with actual_value to quantify the magnitude of the deviation. Applicable for event_type = parameter_deviation.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the operational exception indicating its impact on plant throughput, safety, and production targets. Critical: full circuit or plant stoppage; High: significant throughput reduction; Medium: partial degradation; Low: minor deviation with negligible production impact.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the shift during which the operational exception commenced. Used for daily and shift-level production reporting and exception trend analysis.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Type of shift during which the operational exception occurred: day, night, or afternoon. Supports shift-level performance analysis and identification of shift-specific exception patterns.']
  throughput_loss_tph decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated reduction in processing throughput rate in tonnes per hour (tph) attributable to this exception event. Supports OEE rate loss analysis and process bottleneck identification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operational exception record was most recently modified, such as when root cause, corrective action, or resolution details were updated. Supports audit trail and data currency tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of operational exceptions within the processing plant including unplanned/planned downtime events and process parameter deviations from set-point targets. Captures event type (downtime, parameter_deviation, alarm), start/end timestamps, duration, affected circuit, category (mechanical, electrical, process, planned maintenance, feed shortage, parameter exceedance), severity, set-point vs actual values for deviations, root cause description, corrective action taken, responsible operator, and production loss (tonnes). Feeds OEE calculation, process control quality analysis, root cause investigation, and maintenance domain work order linkage.'
}

Table processing.concentrate_stockpile {
  concentrate_stockpile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each concentrate stockpile inventory record in the processing domain. Primary key for this entity.']

Business justification: Concentrate stockpile inventory management requires certified assay results for grade tracking, blending decisions, and shipment nomination. The last_assay_date plain attribute signals this dependency']
  blend_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.blend. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles are built to achieve a specific blend specification before shipment. The blend product defines target grade and component ratios for the stockpile. blend_target_grade is a denor']
  cargo_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.cargo_shipment. Business justification: Cargo shipments draw concentrate from stockpiles for vessel loading. Inventory reconciliation, demurrage management, and loading rate optimization require tracking which stockpile feeds each shipment.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.circuit. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles receive product from specific processing circuits (e.g., flotation circuit, SX-EW circuit). Linking concentrate_stockpile to circuit enables circuit-level inventory tracking and']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Stockpiles hold specific commodities (iron ore concentrate, copper concentrate). Fundamental for inventory management, blending decisions, and production reconciliation. Replaces denormalized commodit']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Stockpile inventory is valued and tracked against cost centres for balance sheet reporting (IAS 2 inventory), NRV testing, and working capital management. Links physical inventory to financial asset a']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan against which this stockpile inventory is tracked for production target reconciliation.']
  offtake_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.offtake_schedule. Business justification: Stockpile readiness management is a named mining logistics process: concentrate stockpiles are built and managed against specific offtake schedule liftings. Port and logistics teams need to know which']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the processing plant or facility that produced or holds this concentrate stockpile. Links to the plant master record.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles track inventory against scheduled production for shipment planning, metal accounting, and working capital management. Critical for sales contract fulfillment and financial repor']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles represent finished goods inventory on the balance sheet. Profit centre assignment is required for period-end inventory valuation, IFRS inventory reporting, and segment asset dis']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Stockpiles contain specific saleable products awaiting dispatch. Essential for sales order fulfillment, inventory valuation, and linking physical inventory to sales contracts. Standard in mining logis']
  sales_shipment_nomination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.shipment_nomination. Business justification: Shipment nominations allocate specific stockpile inventory to customer orders and vessel schedules. Logistics planning, inventory management, and customer delivery commitments depend on linking nomina']
  source_tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Royalty accrual and regulatory reporting require concentrate stockpile inventory to be attributed to the source tenement. Royalty obligations are calculated per tenement; stockpile records without ten']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles are managed to meet a specific product specification before shipment nomination. Blending and inventory decisions are driven by specification limits. min_grade_spec and max_mois']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles are physical storage locations requiring warehouse management for inventory reconciliation, site access control, environmental compliance, and security. Links processing output ']
  additions_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Cumulative dry metric tonnes of concentrate added to this stockpile from production since the last inventory reconciliation period. Represents inflows from the processing circuit (e.g., flotation, leaching, SX-EW). Used in mass balance and inventory reconciliation.']
  concentrate_type varchar [note: 'Mineralogical classification of the concentrate product in this stockpile. Determines applicable processing routes, smelter penalty elements, and sales contract terms. E.g., sulphide concentrate from flotation vs oxide concentrate from leaching/SX-EW.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this concentrate stockpile record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage.']
  dust_suppression_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dust suppression measures (e.g., water sprays, chemical binders, covers) are currently applied to this stockpile. Relevant for environmental compliance, HSE reporting, and community impact management.']
  grade_element varchar [note: 'Chemical element or compound symbol for which the product_grade is reported (e.g., \'Cu\' for copper, \'Fe\' for iron, \'Li2O\' for lithium oxide, \'Ni\' for nickel). Ensures unambiguous interpretation of the grade value across commodities.']
  inventory_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Current physical inventory of concentrate in the stockpile expressed as dry metric tonnes (DMT), adjusted for moisture content. This is the primary inventory quantity used for sales nominations, shipping calculations, and inventory reconciliation. Updated after each survey event.']
  inventory_wmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Current physical inventory of concentrate in the stockpile expressed as wet metric tonnes (WMT), inclusive of moisture. Used for physical handling, transport weight calculations, and logistics planning. Distinct from DMT which is the commercial quantity.']
  is_shipment_nominated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stockpile inventory has been nominated against an active shipment or vessel loading schedule. True = nominated for an upcoming shipment; False = available for future nomination. Supports logistics scheduling and sales operations.']
  is_under_environmental_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stockpile is subject to an environmental hold that restricts movement or shipment of material (e.g., pending EIS review, acid rock drainage concern, or regulatory compliance investigation). True = hold in place; False = no environmental restriction.']
  last_assay_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent laboratory assay used to determine the current product grade and moisture content of the stockpile. Used to assess grade data currency for sales nominations and blend optimisation.']
  last_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical survey or measurement used to determine the current stockpile inventory volume and tonnage. Critical for assessing inventory data currency and triggering re-survey workflows when data is stale.']
  lom_inventory_target_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Target concentrate inventory level in dry metric tonnes as specified in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan for the current period. Used to assess inventory performance against plan and trigger production rate adjustments.']
  max_capacity_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Engineered maximum storage capacity of the stockpile expressed in dry metric tonnes (DMT). Used for capacity planning, logistics scheduling, and triggering production rate adjustments when inventory approaches capacity limits.']
  moisture_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Current moisture content of the concentrate stockpile expressed as a percentage of wet weight. Used to convert between WMT and DMT, assess handleability, and ensure compliance with shipping moisture limits (e.g., transportable moisture limit for bulk cargo under IMSBC Code).']
  nominated_shipment_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Quantity of concentrate in dry metric tonnes nominated against an active shipment or vessel loading schedule from this stockpile. Enables available-to-nominate calculation (inventory_dmt minus nominated_shipment_dmt) for logistics planning.']
  opening_inventory_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonne inventory at the start of the current reconciliation period. Together with additions_dmt and removals_dmt, enables closing inventory calculation and mass balance closure verification.']
  penalty_element_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of penalty elements detected in the concentrate above contractual threshold levels (e.g., \'As,Pb,Bi\'). Penalty elements attract price deductions in smelter contracts and may trigger quarantine or blending requirements. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results.']
  product_grade decimal(8,4) [note: 'Current assay-derived grade of the valuable mineral in the concentrate stockpile, expressed as a percentage (%). Represents the weighted average grade of all material currently in the stockpile based on the most recent survey or assay. Critical for sales nomination and blend optimisation.']
  reconciliation_period_end date [note: 'End date of the current inventory reconciliation period for which additions_dmt and removals_dmt are accumulated. Nullable if the period is still open.']
  reconciliation_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the current inventory reconciliation period for which additions_dmt and removals_dmt are accumulated. Aligns with metallurgical balance accounting periods for mass balance closure.']
  removals_dmt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Cumulative dry metric tonnes of concentrate removed from this stockpile for shipment, blending, or transfer since the last inventory reconciliation period. Represents outflows to logistics, port, or downstream processing. Used in mass balance and inventory reconciliation.']
  stockpile_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this stockpile location, used in logistics scheduling, sales nominations, and inventory reconciliation reports. Sourced from SAP MM material document or plant layout register.']
  stockpile_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the concentrate stockpile (e.g., \'Plant Pad A — Copper Concentrate\', \'Port Shed 3 — Iron Ore Fines\'). Used in operational dashboards and logistics communications.']
  stockpile_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by the metallurgist or inventory controller regarding this stockpile, such as quality observations, blending instructions, survey anomalies, or logistics constraints. Supports operational decision-making and audit trail.']
  stockpile_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the stockpile. \'active\' = receiving or dispatching material; \'inactive\' = temporarily not in use; \'full\' = at maximum capacity; \'quarantine\' = material held pending quality resolution; \'closed\' = permanently decommissioned.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'Method used to measure the current stockpile volume and derive inventory tonnage. Determines accuracy and frequency of inventory updates. Values: drone_lidar (aerial LiDAR scan), drone_photogrammetry (aerial photogrammetric survey), manual_survey (ground-based manual measurement), belt_scale (continuous belt weigher), weightometer (in-line weightometer), laser_scan (fixed laser scanner).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this concentrate stockpile record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Inventory record of concentrate stockpiles at processing plant pad and port storage facilities — the processing-domain view of physical inventory. Captures stockpile ID, location (plant pad, port shed, rail siding), commodity type, current inventory (dmt), grade (%), moisture (%), date of last survey (drone/LIDAR/manual), maximum capacity (dmt), and stockpile movement history (additions from production, removals for shipment). Supports logistics scheduling, sales nomination, blend optimisation, and inventory reconciliation. Note: this product owns the physical/operational stockpile state; commercial inventory positions, title transfer, and financial valuation are managed in the product and sales domains.'
}

Business justification: Mine-to-mill reconciliation (F1/F2/F3 factors) requires tracing process samples back to the originating blast event. This is a standard mining industry practice — metallurgists and mine planners use b']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit) within the plant from which the sample was collected.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Process samples assay specific commodities for grade control and metallurgical accounting. Core to quality control, mass balance reconciliation, and production reporting. Domain experts expect commodi']

Business justification: Process samples are collected from concentrate batches for quality assurance and grade verification. Metallurgical reconciliation reports a']
  feed_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Process samples are collected at defined sampling points within the processing plant, including feed stream sampling points (ROM feed, crushed feed, recirculating loads). Linking process_sample to fee']
  geological_domain_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Process samples are collected and characterised by ore_type for metallurgical accounting. Reconciling process sample head_grade against geological domain grade predictions is a core metallurgical acco']
  grade_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: Each process sample is analyzed for a specific grade parameter (Cu%, Fe%, SiO2%). Linking enables LIMS integration and ensures samples reference the correct parameter definition, unit of measure, and ']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Sampling activities involve exposure risks (dust inhalation, chemical contact, confined space entry). Incidents during sampling (exposure events, equipment failures, sample spills) need direct linkage']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan against which this sample\'s grade and recovery data is reconciled, supporting JORC-compliant resource and reserve reporting.']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Process samples collected during maintenance activities (oil analysis, wear liner sampling, post-repair metallurgical checks) must be linked to the triggering work order for traceability and cost allo']

Essential for F1/F2/F3 factor calculation and continuous improvement o']
