  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Process samples are collected during a production shift run. One sample belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many samples. This links the sample to the processing domain\'s shift entity.']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which the sample was collected, enabling shift-level metallurgical accounting and reconciliation.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Process samples are collected and analyzed against a target product specification to determine conformance for dispatch approval. QAQC and quality hold decisions depend on whether the sample meets the']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Tailings samples (stream_type=\'tailings\') collected from specific TSF for quality control and environmental monitoring. QA/QC requirement for TSF geochemical characterization, ARD assessment, and regu']
  unit_operation_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: Process samples are collected at defined sampling points during specific unit operations (e.g., flotation feed sample, leach discharge sample). Linking process_sample to unit_operation_event provides ']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the process sample was physically collected at the sampling point. This is the principal real-world event time used for time-series analysis and metallurgical accounting period assignment.']
  composite_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'End time of the compositing period for composite samples, defining the close of the time window over which increments were collected. Null for grab or incremental samples.']
  composite_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Start time of the compositing period for composite samples, defining the beginning of the time window over which increments were collected. Null for grab or incremental samples.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this process sample record was first created in the system, providing the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and compliance.']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current dispatch status of the prepared sample to the analytical laboratory, tracking the chain of custody from plant to laboratory.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the prepared sample was physically dispatched to the analytical laboratory. Null if not yet dispatched.']
  head_grade decimal(12,6) [note: 'Assayed grade of the primary element in the sample, expressed in the unit defined by head_grade_unit. For feed samples this is the head grade; for concentrate or tailings samples this is the stream grade. Populated after laboratory analysis.']
  head_grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the head_grade value: pct = percentage (%), g_per_t = grams per tonne, ppm = parts per million, ppb = parts per billion, kg_per_t = kilograms per tonne.']
  increment_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual increments combined to form a composite sample. Used to assess sampling representativeness per Theory of Sampling (TOS). Null for grab samples.']
  is_representative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample has been assessed as representative of the process stream for metallurgical accounting purposes. False samples are excluded from mass balance calculations.']
  mass_dry_g decimal(12,3) [note: 'Dry mass of the sample in grams after moisture removal during preparation. Used for dry-basis grade calculations and metallurgical accounting.']
  mass_wet_g decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total wet mass of the collected sample in grams as weighed at the sampling point prior to preparation. Used for mass balance calculations and sample representativeness assessment.']
  moisture_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Moisture content of the sample expressed as a percentage of wet mass, determined during sample preparation. Critical for converting wet-basis to dry-basis metallurgical accounting figures.']
  ore_type varchar [note: 'Mineralogical classification of the ore represented by the sample, used for metallurgical performance benchmarking and LOM plan reconciliation. Oxide, sulphide, mixed, transitional, and fresh rock types exhibit different processing behaviour.']
  particle_size_p80_um decimal(10,2) [note: '80th percentile passing particle size of the sample in micrometres (P80), characterising the grind size at the sampling point. Key comminution performance indicator for SAG and ball mill circuit control.']
  preparation_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory preparation method applied to the sample prior to assay, defining the comminution and splitting protocol. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crush_split|riffle_split|rotary_split|pulverise|filter_dry|evaporate|none — promote to reference product if additional methods required]']
  preparation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which sample preparation was completed in the sample preparation facility, marking readiness for dispatch to the laboratory.']
  pulp_density_pct_solids decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage solids by mass of the slurry at the sampling point, measured at time of collection. Relevant for slurry and pulp samples; null for dry ore samples.']
  qaqc_flag varchar [note: 'Quality Assurance and Quality Control (QAQC) classification of the sample: routine = standard production sample; blank = contamination check; standard = certified reference material for accuracy check; duplicate = laboratory duplicate for precision check; field_duplicate = field duplicate for sampling precision.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason a sample was rejected or flagged as non-representative, such as contamination, insufficient mass, sampler malfunction, or chain of custody breach. Null if sample is valid.']
  sample_medium varchar [note: 'Physical state or medium of the sample at the point of collection, distinguishing between slurry (wet ore/water mixture), dry ore, concentrate, leach solution (PLS), or pulp.']
  sample_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations recorded by the sampler or metallurgist at the time of collection or preparation, such as unusual process conditions, equipment anomalies, or visual observations.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique sample identifier assigned at collection, used for tracking through preparation, dispatch, and laboratory assay. Corresponds to the sample barcode or label number in LabWare LIMS.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the process sample tracking its workflow from collection through laboratory analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: collected|prepared|dispatched|received|analysed|rejected — promote to reference product if additional statuses required]']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the sample was collected: Composite = time or mass-proportional blend of multiple increments; Grab = single point-in-time collection; Incremental = single cut from a cross-stream sampler; Automatic = collected by automated sampler; Manual = collected by operator.']
  sampler_type varchar [note: 'Type of sampling device or method used to collect the sample: manual (operator-collected), automatic (automated cross-stream sampler), cross_belt, vezin (rotary cutter), or pipe_cross_cut. Affects representativeness assessment per TOS.']
  sampling_point_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code identifying the defined sampling point within the processing plant where the sample was collected (e.g., SAG_FEED, FLOAT_CONC, FLOAT_TAIL, CIL_FEED, LEACH_PLS). Corresponds to OSIsoft PI tag location.']
  sampling_point_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the sampling point location within the processing circuit (e.g., \'SAG Mill Discharge Screen Oversize\', \'Rougher Flotation Concentrate Launder\').']
  stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the process stream from which the sample was collected, indicating its position in the beneficiation flowsheet. Feed = ROM or circuit input; Concentrate = upgraded product stream; Tailings = waste stream; Intermediate = mid-circuit stream; Product = final saleable product; Recycle = recirculating load.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this process sample record was last modified, supporting data lineage, audit trail, and change tracking requirements.']

  Note: 'Record of process samples collected at defined sampling points within the processing plant for metallurgical accounting, quality control, and grade reconciliation. Captures sample ID, sampling point (feed, concentrate, tailings, intermediate stream), collection timestamp, sample type (composite, grab, incremental), mass (g), preparation method, and dispatch status to laboratory. Links to laboratory domain for assay results via sample ID. Supports JORC-compliant metallurgical accounting and process control.'
}

Table processing.plant_utility_consumption {
  plant_utility_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for plant_utility_consumption']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill, flotation, leaching, SX-EW) for which utility consumption is recorded. Enables circuit-level OPEX benchmarking.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Electricity, water, and gas consumption are major opex line items in processing. Cost centre assignment on each utility consumption record is required for GL posting, AISC energy cost component report']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: Water and energy consumption records must be reconciled against environmental permit licence limits for regulatory compliance reporting. The denormalized water_licence_limit_m3 and water_licence_compl']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Utility costs (power, water, gas) are posted to specific GL accounts for financial reporting. Direct GL account reference on utility consumption records enables automated journal posting, cost element']
  lom_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which utility consumption targets and budgets are benchmarked. Supports variance analysis against LOM plan targets.']
  metallurgical_balance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.metallurgical_balance. Business justification: Plant utility consumption (electricity, water, compressed air) is recorded per circuit per shift and feeds into the metallurgical balance for energy and water intensity calculations. Linking plant_uti']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mineral processing plant where utility consumption was recorded. Links to the plant master record.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Utility consumption must be tracked against production schedules for cost allocation, energy intensity benchmarking, and carbon accounting. Essential for AISC calculation and Scope 1/2 emissions repor']
  regulatory_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Water licence limits and environmental conditions on tenements directly govern plant utility consumption. plant_utility_consumption already tracks water_licence_compliance_flag and water_licence_limit']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Utility consumption is recorded per shift run. One consumption record belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many consumption records. This links the utility consumption to the processing domain\'']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production shift during which this utility consumption was recorded. Links to the shift production run record for operational context.']
  shutdown_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.shutdown_plan. Business justification: Plant utility consumption (electricity, water, compressed air) during planned shutdowns must be attributed to the shutdown plan for AISC cost reporting and energy benchmarking. Mining finance and oper']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Plant utility consumption records track water drawn from and returned to the TSF. Water licence compliance requires reconciling plant water use (fresh_water_intake_m3, recycled_water_m3) against the T']
  water_balance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.water_balance. Business justification: Plant water consumption records must be reconciled against the TSF water balance for water licence compliance reporting. A direct FK enables automated reconciliation of plant-side water use (process_w']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the utility consumption record was approved by the authorised employee. Marks the record as locked for OPEX cost posting and environmental compliance reporting.']
  carbon_budget_target_co2e_t decimal(14,4) [note: 'Approved carbon budget allocation for this circuit and period in tonnes of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e), derived from the LOM plan carbon budget. Used to calculate emissions variance and compliance status.']
  compressed_air_consumption_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of compressed air consumed by the circuit during the period in standard cubic metres (Sm³). Used for flotation cell aeration, instrumentation, and pneumatic equipment. Contributes to energy OPEX.']
  consumption_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number for this utility consumption record, used for cross-referencing with SAP S/4HANA CO cost postings and OPEX reporting. Format: UC-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this utility consumption record was first created in the system, sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian ingestion or manual entry. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary cost fields in this record (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Ensures consistent currency context for OPEX reporting and financial consolidation.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin system or method by which utility consumption data was captured. PI System and SCADA sources are automated and high-confidence; manual_entry and estimated records require additional validation before use in regulatory reporting.']
  electricity_consumption_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total electrical energy consumed by the circuit during the period in kilowatt-hours (kWh), sourced from OSIsoft PI System metering data. Primary input for energy OPEX calculation and carbon footprint reporting.']
  electricity_cost_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost of electricity consumed during the period in the reporting currency, calculated as electricity_consumption_kwh multiplied by electricity_unit_cost plus applicable demand charges. Posted to SAP S/4HANA CO cost centre.']
  electricity_unit_cost decimal(14,4) [note: 'Contracted or metered unit cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour ($/kWh) applicable to this period. Confidential commercial rate used for OPEX cost allocation and energy cost benchmarking.']
  energy_target_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned electrical energy consumption target for this circuit and period in kilowatt-hours (kWh), derived from the LOM plan energy budget. Used to calculate energy consumption variance for OPEX performance reporting.']
  evaporation_loss_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Estimated volume of water lost to evaporation from open process vessels, ponds, and the TSF during the period in cubic metres (m³). Required for water balance closure and environmental reporting.']
  fresh_water_intake_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of fresh water drawn from licensed water sources (rivers, bores, municipal supply) into the processing plant during the period in cubic metres (m³). Reported against water licence limits for regulatory compliance.']
  meter_reading_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the physical or virtual meter reading(s) underpinning this consumption record, as recorded in OSIsoft PI System or the utility provider invoice. Supports audit trail and data lineage for regulatory submissions.']
  natural_gas_consumption_gj decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of natural gas consumed by the circuit during the period in gigajoules (GJ), used for drying, heating, or on-site power generation. Input for Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording operational context, anomalies, meter faults, estimation methodology, or adjustment reasons relevant to this utility consumption record. Supports audit review and regulatory submissions.']
  peak_demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum instantaneous electrical power demand recorded during the period in kilowatts (kW). Used for utility tariff management, demand charge optimisation, and grid capacity planning.']
  period_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world end date and time of the utility consumption measurement period. Used with period_start_timestamp to calculate duration and validate completeness of the consumption record.']
  period_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world start date and time of the utility consumption measurement period. For shift records this aligns with shift start; for monthly records this is the first instant of the accounting month.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the consumption accounting period. Shift-level records are sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian; monthly records align with SAP S/4HANA CO accounting periods for OPEX reporting.']
  power_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Average power factor (ratio of real power to apparent power, dimensionless 0–1) for the circuit during the period. Low power factor incurs utility penalty charges and indicates reactive power inefficiency.']
  process_water_consumption_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total process water consumed within the circuit during the period (fresh intake plus recycled, minus discharge) in cubic metres (m³). Net water balance input for environmental reporting and water licence compliance.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle status of the utility consumption record. Draft records are pending submission; approved records are locked for OPEX and environmental reporting.']
  recycled_water_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of process water recycled from the Tailings Storage Facility (TSF) and other internal sources back into the processing circuit during the period in cubic metres (m³). Supports water stewardship reporting and licence compliance.']
  scope1_emissions_co2e_t decimal(14,4) [note: 'Direct greenhouse gas emissions from on-site combustion of natural gas or other fuels during the period in tonnes of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e). Supports carbon budget compliance and NGER/GRI reporting.']
  scope2_emissions_co2e_t decimal(14,4) [note: 'Indirect greenhouse gas emissions from purchased electricity consumption during the period in tonnes of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e), calculated using applicable grid emission factors. Supports carbon budget compliance and GRI/NGER reporting.']
  specific_energy_kwh_per_t decimal(12,4) [note: 'Electrical energy consumed per dry metric tonne of ore processed (kWh/t), calculated as electricity_consumption_kwh divided by tonnes_processed. Key comminution efficiency KPI benchmarked against LOM plan and AISC targets.']
  specific_water_m3_per_t decimal(12,4) [note: 'Process water consumed per dry metric tonne of ore processed (m³/t). Key water efficiency metric benchmarked against LOM plan targets and water licence conditions.']
  tonnes_processed decimal(18,3) [note: 'Dry metric tonnes of ore processed through the circuit during the period. Used as the denominator for calculating specific consumption rates (e.g., kWh/t, m³/t) for benchmarking and LOM plan variance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this utility consumption record was last modified. Tracks corrections, adjustments, and re-submissions for data lineage and audit compliance.']
  water_cost_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost of fresh water intake during the period in the reporting currency, including licence fees and supply charges. Posted to SAP S/4HANA CO cost centre for OPEX tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of utility resource consumption per processing circuit per shift or accounting period. Covers electrical energy (kWh, kW peak demand, power factor, specific energy kWh/t), water balance (fresh water intake m³, recycled water m³, process water consumption m³/t, evaporation losses), compressed air, and other plant utilities. Captures circuit ID, shift date, consumption volumes, unit costs, and compliance metrics against water licence limits and carbon budgets. Supports OPEX tracking, environmental reporting (water stewardship, carbon footprint), energy efficiency benchmarking, and regulatory compliance against LOM plan targets.'
}

Table processing.measurement_point {
  measurement_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for measurement_point']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment or asset where the measurement instrument is installed (e.g., SAG mill, flotation cell, thickener).']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.circuit. Business justification: Measurement points (PI/SCADA sensors, flow meters, density gauges) are physically installed within specific processing circuits. Linking measurement_point to circuit enables circuit-level sensor inven']
  grade_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: Measurement points in a processing plant measure specific grade parameters (e.g., a Cu grade analyzer measures the Cu grade parameter). Linking enables instrument-to-parameter traceability for LIMS in']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.plant. Business justification: Measurement points are installed within a specific processing plant facility. A direct plant_id FK on measurement_point enables plant-level sensor inventory queries without requiring a join through ci']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.pm_schedule. Business justification: Process measurement points (flow meters, density gauges, pH probes) have PM schedules governing calibration and inspection. Mining instrument technicians and reliability engineers manage calibration c']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Critical control measurement points (is_critical_control_point=true) are defined through bow-tie risk assessments in mining process safety. Linking measurement_point to the risk_assessment that identi']
  upstream_measurement_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on measurement_point (upstream_measurement_point_id)']
  accuracy decimal(8,4) [note: 'Specified accuracy or tolerance of the measurement instrument, typically expressed as a percentage of full scale or absolute value.']
  alarm_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper warning alarm threshold indicating the measurement is approaching critical limits.']
  alarm_high_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'Critical upper alarm threshold that triggers immediate operator intervention when exceeded.']
  alarm_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower warning alarm threshold indicating the measurement is approaching critical limits.']
  alarm_low_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'Critical lower alarm threshold that triggers immediate operator intervention when breached.']
  calibration_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Required interval in days between instrument calibration activities to maintain measurement accuracy and compliance.']
  control_system_tag varchar [note: 'Tag identifier used in the distributed control system or SCADA system to reference this measurement point for real-time monitoring and control.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement point record was first created in the master data system.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the current data quality status from the control system, reflecting instrument health and signal validity.']
  historian_tag varchar [note: 'Tag identifier used in the process historian system (e.g., OSIsoft PI, Aspen IP.21) for time-series data storage and retrieval.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement instrument was physically installed and commissioned at this location.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the measurement instrument or sensor technology used (e.g., magnetic flow meter, pressure transmitter, thermocouple, pH probe, laser particle size analyzer).']
  is_critical_control_point boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point is designated as a critical control point for process safety, environmental compliance, or production quality.']
  is_kpi_input boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point provides input data for calculating key performance indicators such as recovery rate, throughput, or product grade.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was last calibrated by maintenance or instrumentation technicians.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement point record was most recently updated or modified.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the physical location of the measurement point within the plant, including building, floor, and proximity references.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or vendor of the measurement instrument.']
  measurement_point_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the measurement point indicating its location and purpose within the processing circuit.']
  measurement_range_max decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the instruments calibrated measurement range.']
  measurement_range_min decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the instruments calibrated measurement range.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of physical or chemical property being measured at this point (e.g., flow, pressure, temperature, pH, density, particle size).']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model or part number of the measurement instrument.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration activity based on the calibration frequency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special considerations, or operational notes related to this measurement point.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the measurement point indicating whether it is actively collecting data, under maintenance, or out of service.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional group responsible for maintaining and monitoring this measurement point (e.g., Process Control, Instrumentation, Metallurgy).']
  sampling_frequency_seconds varchar [note: 'Interval in seconds at which the measurement point captures and records data values.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to the specific instrument unit.']
  tag_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric tag identifier assigned to the measurement instrument following plant instrumentation standards (e.g., FT-SAG-001, PT-FLOT-205).']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Desired or optimal measurement value as defined by the process control strategy or Life of Mine (LOM) plan.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the measurement value is expressed (e.g., t/h, kPa, °C, %, g/L, µm, pH units).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for measurement_point. Referenced by measurement_point_id.'
}

  sample_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.sample_program. Business justification: Every sample is collected under a sampling program or campaign.   waste_rock_dump_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.waste_rock_dump. Business justification: Waste rock dump geochemical and ARD monitoring requires samples to reference the specific dump they were collected from. ARD management plans and environmental compliance reporting depend on tracing s']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Machine-readable barcode assigned by LIMS for automated sample tracking through preparation and analysis workflows. Ensures chain-of-custody integrity.']
  chain_of_custody_status varchar [note: 'Status of the chain-of-custody documentation for this sample. Intact indicates complete documentation; broken indicates missing or inconsistent records; under investigation indicates active review.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sample was physically collected in the field or plant. Critical for chain-of-custody and time-series analysis of grade trends.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes about the sample, including field observations, preparation issues, or analytical anomalies.']
  core_diameter varchar [note: 'Standard drill core diameter designation for diamond drill samples. HQ (63.5mm), NQ (47.6mm), PQ (85mm), BQ (36.4mm), AQ (27mm). Applicable only to drill core samples.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sample record was first created in the LIMS system. Part of audit trail for data governance.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date when the sample or its pulp was disposed of or destroyed after the retention period expired.']
  from_depth_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Starting depth in meters for the sample interval within the drill hole or channel. Used for spatial correlation with geological models and resource estimation.']
  location_easting decimal(12,2) [note: 'Easting coordinate (X) of the sample collection point in the mine coordinate system. Used for spatial analysis and integration with geological models.']
  location_elevation decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation (Z) of the sample collection point in meters above sea level. Completes the 3D spatial coordinates for geological modeling.']
  location_northing decimal(12,2) [note: 'Northing coordinate (Y) of the sample collection point in the mine coordinate system. Used for spatial analysis and integration with geological models.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sample record was last modified in the LIMS system. Part of audit trail for data governance.']
  preparation_method varchar [note: 'Sample preparation technique applied before analysis. Crush and split for coarse reduction; pulverize for fine grinding; dry for moisture removal; wet screen for size separation; no prep for direct analysis.']
  process_stream_name varchar [note: 'Name of the processing plant stream from which the sample was collected (e.g., ROM feed, crusher discharge, flotation concentrate, tailings). Applicable for process stream samples.']
  pulp_weight_g decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight in grams of the pulverized sample (pulp) prepared for assay analysis. Typically 200-500g depending on analytical method and commodity.']
  qaqc_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sample for quality control purposes. Field duplicate tests sampling precision; lab duplicate tests analytical precision; standard tests accuracy; blank tests contamination; unknown is a regular production sample.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sample was received and registered in the laboratory LIMS. Used to track turnaround time and chain-of-custody.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the sample or its pulp must be retained before disposal, as per regulatory or company policy. Typically 90-365 days depending on sample type and jurisdiction.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the sample, typically a barcode or sequential number assigned by LIMS at sample registration. Used for chain-of-custody tracking and laboratory workflows.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sample in the laboratory workflow. Tracks progression from registration through preparation, analysis, completion, and archival or rejection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: registered|prepared|submitted|in_analysis|completed|archived|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sample based on its origin and purpose. Drill core samples (HQ, NQ, PQ) are from diamond drilling; RC (Reverse Circulation) chips are from percussion drilling; channel samples are from underground faces; process stream samples are from plant operations; metallurgical test charges are for beneficiation studies; quality control samples are standards and blanks.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the sample or its pulp/reject is stored after analysis. Used for sample retrieval and archival management.']
  submitted_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or team who submitted the sample to the laboratory. Part of chain-of-custody documentation.']
  to_depth_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ending depth in meters for the sample interval within the drill hole or channel. Defines the sample interval length together with from_depth_m.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Original weight of the sample in kilograms as received by the laboratory. Critical for calculating recovery rates and ensuring representative sub-sampling.']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical sample submitted to the laboratory, covering drill core (HQ, NQ, PQ), RC chips, channel samples, process stream samples, and metallurgical test charges. Captures sample identity, origin (drill hole ID, bench, pit, process stream), sample type, weight, preparation method, chain-of-custody status, LIMS barcode, and spatial coordinates (from/to depth or location). This is the SSOT for all sample identity data in LabWare LIMS and underpins resource estimation, grade control, and process optimisation workflows.'
}

  sample_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sample batch. Primary key for the sample batch entity.']

  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sample batch. Tracks progression through laboratory workflow from creation to final release of results.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the batch based on the analytical or preparation process. Determines the workflow and equipment requirements.']
  batch_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight in kilograms of all sample material in the batch. Used for material tracking and laboratory capacity planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the batch record was first created in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS). Audit trail field for data governance.']

  external_laboratory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch was processed by an external third-party laboratory. True if external, False if processed in-house.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the batch record was last updated. Tracks changes throughout the batch lifecycle for audit and compliance purposes.']
  mine_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the mine site or operational location from which the samples in this batch originated. Enables site-level reporting and resource estimation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the batch. Determines processing sequence and resource allocation in the laboratory queue.']
  qaqc_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch meets the required Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) insertion rate and protocol compliance. True if compliant, False if non-compliant.']
  qaqc_insertion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) samples relative to total samples in the batch. Used to verify compliance with laboratory quality standards.']
  qaqc_sample_count varchar [note: 'Number of Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) samples (blanks, standards, duplicates, certified reference materials) inserted into the batch for quality validation.']
  reanalysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is a reanalysis or repeat of a previous batch due to Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) failure or data quality concerns. True if reanalysis, False if original analysis.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the batch results were formally released and made available to downstream systems and users after Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) approval.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when batch results were released. Critical for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  requesting_department varchar [note: 'Business unit or department that requested the batch analysis (e.g., Exploration, Mine Operations, Mineral Processing). Used for cost allocation and reporting.']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual samples included in this batch. Used for capacity planning, workload balancing, and batch size optimization.']
  sample_origin_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source or origin of samples in the batch. Determines the analytical requirements and data usage downstream.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch was completed within the agreed Service Level Agreement (SLA) turnaround time. True if compliant, False if breached.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the sample batch was formally submitted to the laboratory for processing. Marks the start of the Service Level Agreement (SLA) turnaround time calculation.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the batch was submitted to the laboratory. Used for detailed workflow tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  turnaround_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual elapsed time in hours from batch submission to completion. Key performance indicator for laboratory efficiency and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']

  Note: 'Groups individual samples into a preparation or analytical batch for laboratory processing. Tracks batch number, batch type (preparation, fire assay, XRF, ICP, leach, metallurgical), submission date, assigned laboratory, priority level, batch status (created, submitted, in-progress, completed, released), expected turnaround date, actual completion date, sample count, and QA/QC insertion compliance flag. Enables throughput management, QA/QC batch-level review, turnaround time monitoring against SLA, and workload balancing across internal and external laboratories.'
}

Regulators require individual result-leve']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Assay results certify that a saleable product meets grade requirements before shipment. Product quality certification and shipment release workflows require traceability from assay result to the speci']
  sample_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.sample_batch. Business justification: Assay results are produced as part of a batch submission. Currently assay_result has batch_number (STRING) but no FK to sample_batch. Adding sample_batch_id FK allows joining to batch-level metadata (']
  sample_preparation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.sample_preparation. Business justification: An assay result is performed on a prepared sample (pulp or split). Linking assay_result to the specific sample_preparation record that produced the analytical portion enables full chain-of-custody tra']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Assay results are evaluated against product specifications to determine grade compliance pass/fail for product release. This is the core QAQC and product certification workflow in mining — every assay']
  accuracy_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Calculated accuracy of the assay result compared to the expected grade for standard reference materials, expressed as a percentage. Measures systematic bias in the analytical method.']
  analysis_date date [note: 'The date on which the assay analysis was performed. Critical for tracking sample turnaround time and ensuring results are used within appropriate timeframes for resource estimation and grade control.']
  analysis_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the assay analysis was completed. Provides granular traceability for quality control and audit purposes.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assay result was formally approved by a qualified person. Marks the transition from preliminary to certified status.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the qualified person or laboratory supervisor who reviewed and approved this assay result for release. Required for certified results used in resource estimation.']
  below_detection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay result was below the detection limit of the analytical method. True if below detection, False otherwise. Results flagged as below detection are typically assigned half the detection limit value for statistical purposes.']
  certified_result_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assay result has been certified and approved for use in JORC/NI 43-101 resource estimation and public reporting. True if certified, False if preliminary or for internal use only.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for analyst or reviewer comments regarding the assay result, including notes on sample condition, analytical issues, QA/QC observations, or special handling requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assay result record was first created in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  detection_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum concentration of the analyte that can be reliably detected by the analytical method. Results below this threshold are typically reported as below detection limit (BDL) or less than (<) values.']
  dilution_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'The factor by which the sample was diluted prior to analysis, typically applied when initial results exceed the upper detection limit. A value of 1.0 indicates no dilution; values greater than 1.0 indicate the sample was diluted (e.g., 10.0 means 10x dilution).']
  expected_grade decimal(18,6) [note: 'The certified or expected grade value for a standard reference material. Used to calculate accuracy and bias in QA/QC analysis. Null for non-standard samples.']
  grade_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the reported grade. Common units include ppm (parts per million), percent (%), g/t (grams per tonne), oz/t (ounces per ton), mg/kg (milligrams per kilogram), ppb (parts per billion).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assay result record was last modified in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS). Tracks changes for audit and quality control purposes.']
  over_limit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the initial assay result exceeded the upper detection limit of the analytical method, requiring dilution and re-analysis. True if over limit, False otherwise.']
  precision_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Calculated precision of the assay result based on duplicate sample analysis, expressed as a percentage. Measures random error and reproducibility of the analytical method.']
  qaqc_sample_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the type of QA/QC sample if this result is part of the quality control program. Original indicates a regular production sample; other values indicate QA/QC control samples inserted into the analytical stream.']
  qaqc_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality assurance and quality control checks applied to this assay result. Indicates whether the result passed all QA/QC protocols including blank, duplicate, and standard reference material checks.']
  reported_date date [note: 'The date on which the assay result was officially reported and released from the laboratory to the requesting party. May differ from analysis date due to quality control review and approval workflows.']
  reported_grade decimal(18,6) [note: 'The final assay grade value reported for the analyte in the sample. This is the primary geochemical measurement used for resource estimation, grade control, and metallurgical balance calculations. May be expressed in various units depending on analyte (ppm, percent, g/t).']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assay result. Preliminary results are for internal use only; final results are certified for resource estimation; cancelled results have been invalidated; superseded results have been replaced by re-analysis.']
  upper_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum concentration of the analyte that can be accurately measured by the analytical method without dilution. Results exceeding this threshold require sample dilution and re-analysis.']

  Note: 'Stores the primary geochemical assay result for each sample-analyte combination, including fire assay, XRF, ICP-MS, ICP-OES, and AAS methods. Records analyte code, reported grade, detection limit, over-limit flag, dilution factor, analytical method, instrument ID, run date, and certified result status. This is the SSOT for all geochemical grade data used in JORC/NI 43-101 resource estimation and grade control.'
}

  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Metallurgical tests evaluate processing characteristics of specific commodities. Enables recovery rate analysis by commodity, process optimization, and product specification validation. Replaces denor']

Linking metallurgical_test to shutdown_pl']
