Linking each inspection to its governing contract enables cost tracking against the contract, vendor performance eva']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.corrective_action. Business justification: Inspection findings requiring remedial actions must link to corrective action tracking system. Core compliance workflow: inspection identifies deficiency → corrective action assigned → closure verifie']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Inspection costs (contractor fees, EOR time, instrumentation calibration) are charged to the TSF\'s cost centre for AISC calculation. Required for opex tracking and regulatory compliance cost reporting']
  inspection_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_vendor. Business justification: Dam safety inspections are conducted by specialist engineering firms engaged as procurement vendors. Mining operations track which vendor firm performed each inspection for vendor performance manageme']
  regulatory_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Dam safety inspections are mandated by specific regulatory conditions on the tenement (dam safety licence conditions). Inspection frequency, reporting requirements, and regulatory submission obligatio']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Dam safety inspection reports are formally submitted to regulators (regulatory_submission_required, regulatory_submission_date fields exist on this product). Linking to the regulatory_submission recor']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Dam safety inspections assess factor_of_safety and slope_stability against risk criteria defined in a formal risk assessment. Linking inspection records to the governing risk assessment is required fo']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tailings storage facility or waste rock embankment being inspected.']
  tsf_raise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf_raise. Business justification: Dam safety inspections are frequently conducted in direct association with raise events — specifically post-raise geotechnical sign-off inspections required before a raise is commissioned. Adding tsf_']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Dam safety inspections are regulatory compliance activities whose costs are tracked as sustaining CAPEX under WBS elements. Mining finance teams allocate inspection costs to WBS nodes for AISC reporti']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Dam safety inspections generate remedial maintenance work orders for identified defects. Regulatory frameworks (MAC, ANCOLD) require traceability from inspection findings to corrective work orders. da']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dam safety inspection record was first created in the system.']
  eor_signoff_date date [note: 'Date on which the Engineer of Record formally signed off on the inspection report and stability analysis results.']
  factor_of_safety decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated factor of safety from the stability analysis, representing the ratio of resisting forces to driving forces. Minimum FoS thresholds are defined by ANCOLD, CDA, and ICMM GISTM based on loading condition and consequence classification.']
  failure_mode_assessed varchar [note: 'Description of the failure mode evaluated in the stability analysis (e.g., circular slip, wedge failure, liquefaction, foundation failure, overtopping).']
  fos_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the calculated factor of safety meets or exceeds the minimum design criteria and regulatory requirements.']
  freeboard_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the measured freeboard meets the design criteria and regulatory requirements.']
  freeboard_measurement_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vertical distance in meters between the current water level in the TSF and the crest of the dam, a critical safety parameter to prevent overtopping.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date on which the dam safety inspection was conducted.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the inspection, typically a sequential or coded reference used in reports and compliance documentation.']
  inspection_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or file path to the formal inspection report, including photographs, drawings, and detailed findings.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection: scheduled (planned but not started), in progress (field work underway), completed (field work done), report pending (awaiting documentation), approved (reviewed and accepted), closed (all actions resolved).']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection: routine (regular operational checks), annual (comprehensive yearly review), independent review (third-party assessment), ITRB (Independent Tailings Review Board), post-event (following seismic or extreme weather), emergency (triggered by incident), construction phase, or commissioning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: routine|annual|independent_review|itrb|post_event|emergency|construction_phase|commissioning — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  instrumentation_compliance varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the instrumentation array meets the design and regulatory requirements for monitoring.']
  instrumentation_status varchar [note: 'Summary of the operational status of geotechnical instrumentation (piezometers, inclinometers, settlement monuments, flow meters), including any malfunctioning or missing instruments.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dam safety inspection record was last updated.']
  material_strength_parameters varchar [note: 'Summary of the geotechnical material strength parameters used in the analysis, including cohesion, friction angle, and unit weight for tailings, foundation, and embankment materials.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next dam safety inspection, based on regulatory requirements and the consequence classification of the TSF.']
  overall_safety_rating varchar [note: 'Summary assessment of the dam safety condition: satisfactory (no concerns), fair (minor issues), marginal (requires monitoring), unsatisfactory (remedial action needed), urgent (immediate intervention required).']
  phreatic_surface_assumption varchar [note: 'Description of the phreatic surface (water table) assumptions used in the stability analysis, including piezometric data sources and interpolation methods.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the inspection report was submitted to the regulatory authority, if applicable.']
  regulatory_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection report must be submitted to a regulatory authority or governing body.']
  remedial_action_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the remedial actions: none (no action needed), low (routine maintenance), medium (planned intervention), high (near-term action), urgent (immediate response).']
  remedial_actions_required varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any corrective or remedial actions identified during the inspection, including repairs, monitoring enhancements, or operational changes.']
  seepage_observation varchar [note: 'Qualitative description of seepage conditions observed during the inspection, including location, flow rate, clarity, and any signs of internal erosion or piping.']
  seepage_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of seepage severity: none (no seepage detected), minor (expected and controlled), moderate (requires monitoring), significant (remedial action needed), critical (immediate intervention required).']
  slope_condition varchar [note: 'Description of the physical condition of the upstream and downstream slopes, including observations of cracking, slumping, erosion, vegetation, or other surface anomalies.']
  slope_stability_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of slope stability based on visual inspection: stable (no concerns), minor concern (cosmetic issues), moderate concern (requires monitoring), unstable (remedial action needed).']
  stability_analysis_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal geotechnical stability analysis was performed as part of this inspection cycle.']

  Note: 'Records formal dam safety inspections and geotechnical stability analyses conducted on TSFs and waste rock embankments. For inspections: captures inspection type (routine, annual, independent review, ITRB), inspector identity and credentials, inspection date, overall safety rating, freeboard measurement, seepage observations, slope condition, instrumentation status, and required remedial actions. For stability analyses: captures analysis type (static, pseudo-static, dynamic), software used, failure mode assessed, factor of safety (FoS) calculated, phreatic surface assumptions, material strength parameters, and engineer of record sign-off. Supports compliance with ANCOLD, CDA, MAC, USACE, and ICMM GISTM Requirements 5 (EoR), 10 (design basis), and 11 (dam safety review) including minimum FoS obligations.'
}

Table tailings.geotechnical_instrument {
  geotechnical_instrument_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the geotechnical monitoring instrument. Primary key for the geotechnical instrument register.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: Geotechnical instruments are covered by maintenance and calibration service contracts. Mining operations track which contract governs each instrument\'s maintenance schedule and calibration services fo']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Ongoing calibration, maintenance, and data acquisition costs for geotechnical instruments are charged to a cost centre for OPEX reporting. Instrument-level cost centre assignment enables granular TSF ']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Instruments (piezometers, inclinometers, survey prisms) are capitalized fixed assets with depreciation schedules. Required for asset register reconciliation, insurance valuation, and capex vs opex cla']
  geological_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.geological_unit. Business justification: Piezometers, inclinometers, and settlement monitors are installed within specific geological units of the TSF foundation or embankment. Instrument readings are interpreted against the geological unit\'']
  supply_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: Geotechnical instruments are procured assets with manufacturer part numbers, warranty terms, and spare parts requirements. Linking to material master enables calibration parts ordering, warranty claim']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tailings storage facility where this instrument is installed. Links the instrument to the specific TSF being monitored.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Instrument manufacturers/suppliers provide calibration services, technical support, and spare parts. Linking to vendor enables vendor performance tracking for calibration turnaround times, parts avail']
  alert_threshold_level_1 decimal(12,3) [note: 'First level alert threshold value for the instrument reading. When exceeded, triggers initial investigation and increased monitoring frequency per the TSF management plan.']
  alert_threshold_level_2 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Second level alert threshold value for the instrument reading. When exceeded, triggers escalated response including engineering review and potential operational restrictions.']
  alert_threshold_level_3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Third level critical alert threshold value for the instrument reading. When exceeded, triggers emergency response protocols and may require immediate operational shutdown.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration or verification of the instrument against known standards. Critical for ensuring data quality and regulatory compliance.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next calibration or verification must be performed to maintain instrument certification and data validity. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was commissioned and began producing valid monitoring data after installation and baseline stabilization. May differ from installation date due to settling period requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this instrument record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  data_acquisition_method varchar [note: 'Method by which readings are collected from the instrument. Automated and SCADA-integrated instruments provide real-time data; manual instruments require field visits.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was taken out of service, either due to failure, replacement, or completion of monitoring requirements. Null for active instruments.']
  elevation_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the instrument installation point above mean sea level in meters. Critical for understanding vertical position relative to dam structure and phreatic surface.']
  facility_zone varchar [note: 'Specific zone or section within the TSF or waste rock facility where the instrument is located, such as upstream face, downstream toe, crest, or foundation. Used for spatial analysis and reporting.']
  installation_contractor varchar [note: 'Name of the contractor or company that performed the instrument installation. Used for warranty claims and installation quality tracking.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was physically installed at the monitoring location. Marks the start of the instruments operational lifecycle and baseline establishment period.']
  installation_depth_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Depth below ground surface at which the instrument sensor is installed, measured in meters. Applicable to subsurface instruments such as piezometers and inclinometers. Null for surface instruments.']
  installation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing important details about the installation process, site conditions, deviations from design, or special considerations that may affect instrument performance or data interpretation.']
  instrument_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or label for the instrument, typically including location context and instrument type for easy identification in reports and dashboards.']
  instrument_subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification or model specification of the instrument type, such as standpipe piezometer, pneumatic piezometer, or in-place inclinometer. Provides additional technical detail beyond the primary type.']
  instrument_tag varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric tag or serial number assigned to the instrument for field identification and tracking. Used by field technicians and in monitoring reports.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the geotechnical monitoring instrument based on its measurement function. Determines the type of data collected and analysis methods applied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: piezometer|inclinometer|settlement_plate|survey_prism|vibrating_wire_sensor|seepage_weir|extensometer|tiltmeter — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_regulatory_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this instrument is required by regulatory permits, Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) conditions, or dam safety orders. True if mandated by external authority; false if installed for internal monitoring purposes.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the instrument installation location in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and spatial analysis of monitoring network coverage.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the instrument installation location in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and spatial analysis of monitoring network coverage.']
  measurement_range_max decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum value of the instruments design measurement range. Defines the upper bound of valid readings the instrument can produce.']
  measurement_range_min decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum value of the instruments design measurement range. Defines the lower bound of valid readings the instrument can produce.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the readings produced by this instrument, such as meters, millimeters, kPa, degrees, or liters per second. Must align with the instrument type and measurement range.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model or part number for the instrument. Critical for identifying compatible replacement parts and understanding instrument specifications.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this instrument record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which readings are taken from this instrument under normal operating conditions. May be increased during alert conditions or construction activities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: real_time|hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|event_based — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the instrument. Active instruments are producing valid data; failed instruments require repair or replacement; inactive instruments are temporarily not monitored.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Primary purpose or objective for which this instrument was installed, such as phreatic surface monitoring, settlement tracking, slope stability assessment, or seepage quantification. Guides data interpretation and reporting.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific instrument unit. Used for warranty claims, calibration tracking, and asset management.']

  Note: 'Master register of all geotechnical monitoring instruments installed at TSFs and waste rock facilities, including piezometers, inclinometers, settlement plates, survey prisms, vibrating wire sensors, and seepage weirs. Captures instrument type, installation date, location coordinates, depth, design range, alert thresholds, and operational status. SSOT for instrument identity used by all monitoring readings.'
}

Table tailings.geotechnical_reading {
  geotechnical_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the geotechnical instrument reading record.']
  geotechnical_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.geotechnical_instrument. Business justification: Geotechnical readings are captured from geotechnical instruments installed at tailings facilities. The existing instrument_id FK points to laboratory.instrument (cross-domain, generic instrument regis']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HSE incident record if this reading triggered a dam safety incident or TARP activation.']
  instrument_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geotechnical monitoring instrument that captured this reading (piezometer, inclinometer, settlement plate, survey prism).']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tailings storage facility or waste rock facility where the instrument is installed.']
  action_threshold decimal(18,6) [note: 'The action threshold value defined in the Trigger Action Response Plan (TARP) that triggers immediate intervention and response protocols.']
  alert_threshold decimal(18,6) [note: 'The alert threshold value defined in the Trigger Action Response Plan (TARP) that indicates elevated monitoring attention is required.']
  baseline_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The initial or reference baseline value established for this instrument location, used for calculating deviation and trend analysis.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'The date when the instrument was last calibrated, ensuring measurement accuracy and reliability.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assessment flag indicating the reliability and validity of the reading for dam safety surveillance purposes.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The source system or platform from which the reading was ingested (SCADA, manual field entry, remote telemetry, third-party monitoring service).']
  deviation_from_baseline decimal(18,6) [note: 'The calculated difference between the current reading value and the established baseline value.']
  incident_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this reading triggered an incident report or TARP response action.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'The category of geotechnical monitoring instrument that produced this reading.']
  rate_of_change decimal(18,6) [note: 'The calculated rate of change in the measured parameter over time, critical for identifying accelerating deformation or pore pressure trends.']
  rate_of_change_unit varchar [note: 'The unit expressing the rate of change calculation (e.g., millimeters per day, kilopascals per day).']
  reading_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the reading was captured: manual field measurement, automated SCADA system, or remote telemetry.']
  reading_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or engineer comments regarding anomalies, field conditions, or contextual information relevant to the reading.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the geotechnical reading was captured in the field, representing the actual measurement event time.']
  reading_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The measured numeric value captured by the geotechnical instrument at the reading timestamp.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reading record was first created in the data system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reading record was last modified or updated.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature at the time of reading, which may affect instrument calibration and reading interpretation.']
  threshold_breach_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the reading value has breached alert or action thresholds defined in the TARP.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the reading value is expressed (millimeters for displacement, kilopascals for pore pressure, degrees for inclination, percent for strain).']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Reference to the geotechnical engineer or competent person who validated the reading.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'The validation status of the reading by a qualified geotechnical engineer or dam safety specialist.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reading was validated by a qualified person.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'The weather condition at the time of reading capture, relevant for interpreting pore pressure and seepage readings.']

  Note: 'Time-series records of geotechnical instrument readings captured from piezometers, inclinometers, settlement plates, and survey prisms at TSFs and waste rock facilities. Stores reading timestamp, measured value, unit of measure, reading method (manual, automated SCADA), operator, data quality flag, and comparison against alert and action thresholds. Feeds dam safety surveillance and trigger action response plans (TARPs).'
}

Table tailings.seepage_monitoring {
  seepage_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the seepage monitoring record.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Seepage monitoring costs (field sampling, lab analysis, data management) are allocated to the TSF cost centre for environmental compliance cost tracking and AISC reporting.']
  environmental_monitoring_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_monitoring. Business justification: Seepage monitoring results are formally reported through the environmental monitoring program for regulatory compliance. Linking seepage_monitoring to the corresponding environmental_monitoring record']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: Seepage monitoring is conducted to verify compliance with environmental permit discharge limits. The environmental_licence_limit and licence_exceedance_flag columns are permit-driven; the environmenta']
  geological_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.geological_unit. Business justification: Seepage monitoring points are installed in specific geological units whose permeability and mineralogy control seepage chemistry and flow rates. Hydrogeologists reference the geological unit when inte']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the laboratory information management system (LIMS) for tracking the sample through analysis.']
  regulatory_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Seepage monitoring is performed to comply with specific environmental licence conditions on the tenement. The licence_exceedance_flag, environmental_licence_limit, and regulatory_reporting_flag fields']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Seepage exceedances trigger mandatory regulatory notifications (regulatory_reporting_flag on seepage_monitoring). Linking to the regulatory_submission record enables tracking of notification status, s']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the tailings storage facility where seepage is monitored.']
  water_balance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.water_balance. Business justification: Seepage monitoring records measure water outflow from the TSF through seepage collection systems. These measurements are inputs to water balance calculations as outflow components. Each seepage monito']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Seepage monitoring programs are environmental compliance activities tracked as WBS-level projects for regulatory cost reporting and environmental licence compliance cost disclosure in statutory accoun']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Seepage monitoring exceedances trigger maintenance work orders for investigation, pump installation, or drainage remediation. Environmental licence compliance requires traceability from seepage exceed']
  alert_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement triggered an automated alert or notification due to threshold exceedance (True = alert triggered, False = no alert).']
  arsenic_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Arsenic concentration in seepage water in milligrams per liter (mg/L).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for technician observations, anomalies, or contextual notes related to the seepage measurement.']
  conductivity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for electrical conductivity.']
  copper_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Copper metal concentration in seepage water in milligrams per liter (mg/L).']
  design_exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the measured flow rate exceeds the design seepage rate (True = exceeded, False = within design).']
  design_seepage_rate decimal(12,3) [note: 'Expected design seepage flow rate for this monitoring location in liters per second (L/s), used for comparison against actual measurements.']
  electrical_conductivity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Electrical conductivity of seepage water in microsiemens per centimeter (µS/cm), indicating dissolved ion concentration.']
  flow_rate decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured seepage flow rate in liters per second (L/s).']
  flow_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the seepage flow rate.']
  internal_erosion_risk_indicator varchar [note: 'Risk assessment indicator for potential internal erosion based on turbidity, flow rate trends, and other seepage characteristics.']
  iron_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Iron metal concentration in seepage water in milligrams per liter (mg/L).']
  laboratory_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the sample was sent for laboratory analysis (True) or measured in-field (False).']
  lead_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lead metal concentration in seepage water in milligrams per liter (mg/L).']
  licence_exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the measurement exceeds the environmental licence limit (True = exceeded, False = compliant).']
  licence_limit_parameter varchar [note: 'The specific parameter to which the environmental licence limit applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flow_rate|turbidity|ph|copper|iron|manganese|zinc|arsenic|lead|sulfate|tds — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Type of monitoring location where seepage is measured.']
  manganese_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Manganese metal concentration in seepage water in milligrams per liter (mg/L).']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Quality status of the measurement indicating whether the data is valid for reporting and analysis.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the seepage measurement was recorded in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  monitoring_point_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific monitoring point or instrument (e.g., TD-01, SCP-03, UD-05).']
  ph_level decimal(4,2) [note: 'pH measurement of seepage water on a scale of 0-14, indicating acidity or alkalinity.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seepage monitoring record was first created in the system in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seepage monitoring record was last updated in the system in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement must be included in regulatory environmental reporting (True = required, False = internal monitoring only).']
  sample_collection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to collect the seepage water sample for analysis.']
  sulfate_concentration decimal(10,2) [note: 'Sulfate ion concentration in seepage water in milligrams per liter (mg/L).']
  total_dissolved_solids decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total dissolved solids concentration in milligrams per liter (mg/L).']
  turbidity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Turbidity measurement of seepage water in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU), indicating suspended solids and potential internal erosion.']
  turbidity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for turbidity reading.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time of measurement, which may influence seepage rates.']
  zinc_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Zinc metal concentration in seepage water in milligrams per liter (mg/L).']

  Note: 'Records seepage flow measurements and water quality observations at TSF toe drains, seepage collection ponds, and underdrains. Captures measurement date, flow rate, turbidity, pH, conductivity, metal concentrations, and comparison against design seepage rates and environmental licence limits. Supports early detection of internal erosion and regulatory environmental reporting.'
}

Table tailings.tarp_trigger {
  tarp_trigger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the TARP trigger event record. Primary key for the tarp_trigger product.']
  condition_monitoring_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.condition_monitoring. Business justification: TARP (Trigger Action Response Plan) events at TSFs are activated by condition monitoring alerts — vibration threshold breaches, visual defect observations, or instrument anomalies detected during CM i']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.corrective_action. Business justification: TARP triggers require corrective actions to address root causes and prevent recurrence. Links monitoring threshold exceedances to formal action tracking. Core safety management workflow: trigger activ']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: TARP response actions (investigations, remediation, additional monitoring) incur costs charged to the facility cost centre. Required for incident cost tracking and management reporting of unplanned ex']
  geotechnical_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific monitoring instrument or sensor that recorded the parameter breach (e.g., piezometer ID, survey monument ID, flow meter ID).']
  hse_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: TARP (Trigger Action Response Plan) Level 2/3 exceedances escalate to formal HSE incidents requiring investigation per mining safety management systems. Links operational monitoring thresholds to inci']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.plant. Business justification: TARP (Trigger Action Response Plan) activations at a TSF require immediate notification and potential curtailment of the processing plant feeding that TSF. Dam safety management protocols mandate dire']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.regulatory_submission. Business justification: TARP activations at Level 3/4 require mandatory regulatory notification (regulatory_notification_required_flag, regulatory_authority, regulatory_notification_date on tarp_trigger). Linking to the regu']
  response_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Emergency response equipment (pumps, excavators, generators) pre-assigned to TARP trigger levels for rapid deployment. Critical for emergency preparedness planning, TARP drill exercises, equipment rea']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: TARP trigger levels and threshold values are defined based on formal risk assessments. Linking tarp_trigger to the governing risk assessment enables traceability between trigger thresholds and the ris']
  seepage_monitoring_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.seepage_monitoring. Business justification: TARP triggers can be activated by seepage exceedances detected at toe drains or seepage collection points. tarp_trigger already references geotechnical_instrument_id for instrument-based triggers, but']
  triggering_geotechnical_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.geotechnical_reading. Business justification: A TARP trigger is typically activated when a specific geotechnical reading breaches a threshold level. tarp_trigger already has geotechnical_instrument_id (linking to the instrument) and measured_valu']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tailings storage facility where the TARP trigger event occurred.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Emergency response and remediation costs from TARP activations are tracked against WBS elements for project cost control, insurance claims, and regulatory incident cost reporting. Direct link enables ']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the TARP trigger was activated due to the parameter threshold breach. This is the principal business event timestamp for the trigger record.']
  closure_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authority who approved the closure of the TARP trigger event, confirming that all required actions have been satisfactorily completed.']
  closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the TARP trigger event was formally closed after all response actions, investigations, and corrective measures were completed and verified.']
  corrective_actions varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions implemented to address the root cause and prevent recurrence of similar TARP trigger events.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this TARP trigger event data originated (e.g., IsoMetrix HSE Incident Management, OSIsoft PI System, custom TSF monitoring system).']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the parameter breach was detected, indicating whether it was identified through automated monitoring systems, manual inspection, or other means.']
  environmental_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Summary of any environmental impacts resulting from the TARP trigger event, including potential or actual effects on water quality, soil, air, or surrounding ecosystems.']
  exceedance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage by which the measured value exceeded the threshold value, calculated as ((measured_value - threshold_value) / threshold_value) * 100.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'The date when the formal investigation into the TARP trigger event was completed and findings were documented.']
  investigation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a formal investigation is required for this TARP trigger event based on its severity and potential consequences.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Documentation of lessons learned from the TARP trigger event and response, used to improve future monitoring, response protocols, and risk management practices.']
  measured_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual measured value of the parameter at the time of trigger activation, recorded in the unit of measure specified for that parameter.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when key personnel and authorities were notified of the TARP trigger activation.']
  notified_personnel varchar [note: 'List of personnel, roles, or positions that were notified of the TARP trigger activation as per the emergency response protocol (e.g., TSF Manager, Chief Geotechnical Engineer, Site Manager, Regulatory Authority).']
  parameter_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the triggering parameter into broad monitoring categories for analysis and reporting purposes.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this TARP trigger record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this TARP trigger record was last modified or updated.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or governing body that was notified of the TARP trigger event (e.g., state mining department, environmental protection agency).']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'The date when regulatory authorities were formally notified of the TARP trigger event, if required.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the TARP trigger event severity requires formal notification to regulatory authorities or governing bodies.']
  response_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the immediate response actions taken following the TARP trigger activation, including operational adjustments, additional monitoring, engineering interventions, or emergency procedures initiated.']
  response_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the formal response actions were initiated following the TARP trigger activation.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Summary of the root cause analysis findings that identified the underlying factors contributing to the parameter threshold breach.']
  safety_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Summary of any health and safety impacts or risks to personnel, communities, or infrastructure resulting from the TARP trigger event.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The defined threshold limit for the parameter that was breached, triggering the TARP response. This value corresponds to the trigger level (green, yellow, orange, or red) in the TARP framework.']
  trigger_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the TARP trigger event. Green indicates normal operations, yellow indicates alert threshold breach, orange indicates action threshold breach requiring immediate response, and red indicates emergency threshold breach requiring critical intervention.']
  trigger_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the TARP trigger event, used for external reporting and communication with regulatory authorities and management.']
  trigger_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the TARP trigger event, tracking progression from initial activation through investigation and resolution.']
  triggering_parameter varchar [note: 'The specific geotechnical or operational parameter that breached its threshold and activated the TARP trigger (e.g., piezometric level, settlement rate, seepage flow, freeboard, beach length, dam crest displacement).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the measured value and threshold value are expressed (e.g., meters, millimeters, liters per second, kilopascals, degrees).']

  Note: 'Records Trigger Action Response Plan (TARP) trigger events activated at a TSF when geotechnical or operational parameters breach defined alert, action, or emergency thresholds. Captures trigger level (green, yellow, orange, red), triggering parameter, measured value, threshold value, activation timestamp, notified personnel, and response actions taken. Critical for dam safety governance and emergency preparedness.'
}

Table tailings.deposition {
  deposition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for deposition']
  area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mine_area. Business justification: Deposition volumes are spatially associated with mine areas for area-level water balance and tailings management reporting. Mine area FK supports operational planning for slurry pipeline routing and a']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.circuit. Business justification: Tailings deposition events are circuit-specific — different processing circuits (e.g. flotation vs. leach) produce tailings with different properties routed to different TSF cells. Circuit-level depos']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Deposition operations (pumping, spigot management, surveys) are core TSF operating costs allocated to the facility cost centre for unit cost calculation and AISC reporting.']
  opex_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.opex_budget. Business justification: Tailings deposition operations are a major OPEX line item budgeted annually. Linking deposition events to the opex_budget enables actual vs. budget variance analysis for tailings management costs in A']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.processing_plant. Business justification: Tailings deposition events originate from specific processing plant. Source tracking for TSF management, mass balance reconciliation between plant output and TSF input, and operational planning. Links']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Daily tailings deposition volumes must reconcile with planned mill throughput from mining production schedules. Variance analysis between scheduled ore tonnes and actual tailings generation is a stand']
  shift_production_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Tailings deposition tied to specific production shift for operational reconciliation. Enables matching processing tonnes_processed with TSF solids_tonnage_t, shift-level mass balance, and production r']
  shift_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.shift_report. Business justification: Tailings deposition events occur within mine shifts. Linking deposition to shift_report enables shift-level reconciliation of tailings solids tonnage against mine ore production — a standard daily pro']
  survey_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Survey equipment (drones, GPS rovers, total stations) used for tailings beach elevation surveys and capacity assessments must be tracked for calibration status, accuracy classification, and regulatory']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the tailings storage facility where deposition occurred.']
  tsf_raise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf_raise. Business justification: Deposition events are operationally constrained by the current raise level — deposition can only proceed within the approved crest elevation of the current raise. The deposition table has a raise_trig']
  water_balance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.water_balance. Business justification: Deposition events deliver tailings slurry (solids + water) to the TSF, representing water inflow. Each deposition event occurs within a specific water balance period. The water component of the slurry']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the survey record or deposition event in the operational workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the record was approved.']
  beach_elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average elevation of the tailings beach surface at time of survey, measured in meters above sea level. Null for deposition event records.']
  beach_length_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of the tailings beach from the deposition point to the pond edge, measured in meters.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the deposition event or survey against the approved deposition plan and regulatory requirements.']
  current_storage_volume_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of tailings currently stored in the TSF at time of survey, measured in cubic meters. Null for deposition event records.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system or device that captured the deposition or survey data (e.g., SCADA, survey equipment model).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the deposition event occurred or the capacity survey was conducted.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of event recorded: deposition event or periodic capacity survey.']
  freeboard_m decimal(8,3) [note: 'Vertical distance between the pond water level and the crest of the dam at time of event, measured in meters.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional operational context, anomalies, or observations related to the deposition event or survey.']
  point_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the deposition point or spigot location. Null for capacity survey records.']
  point_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the deposition point or spigot location. Null for capacity survey records.']
  pond_level_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Elevation of the pond water surface at time of event, measured in meters above sea level.']
  pond_surface_area_m2 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Surface area of the supernatant pond at time of survey, measured in square meters. Null for deposition event records.']
  raise_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the survey results trigger the need for a dam raise or expansion. Null for deposition event records.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this record was last modified in the system.']
  remaining_airspace_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Available storage volume remaining in the TSF at time of survey, measured in cubic meters. Null for deposition event records.']
  remaining_life_years decimal(8,2) [note: 'Calculated remaining operational life of the TSF based on current storage volume and deposition rate, measured in years. Null for deposition event records.']
  slurry_density_t_per_m3 decimal(8,3) [note: 'Density of the tailings slurry at time of deposition, measured in tonnes per cubic meter. Null for capacity survey records.']
  solids_tonnage_t decimal(18,3) [note: 'Dry tonnage of solid tailings material deposited, measured in tonnes. Null for capacity survey records.']
  spigot_configuration varchar [note: 'Configuration of spigots used during deposition: single, multiple, rotating, or fixed. Null for capacity survey records.']
  survey_accuracy_class varchar [note: 'Classification of survey accuracy based on method and conditions: high, medium, or low. Null for deposition event records.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'Method used to conduct the capacity survey: drone photogrammetry, LiDAR, GPS, total station, or other. Null for deposition event records.']
  total_storage_capacity_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total design storage capacity of the TSF at time of survey, measured in cubic meters. Null for deposition event records.']
  variance_from_plan_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Variance between actual deposition volume and planned volume, measured in cubic meters. Positive indicates over-plan, negative indicates under-plan.']
  volume_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of tailings deposited during the event, measured in cubic meters. Null for capacity survey records.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at time of event or survey, relevant for operational context and data quality assessment.']

  Note: 'Records tailings deposition events and periodic volumetric capacity surveys for TSFs. For deposition: captures volume deposited, slurry density, solids tonnage, deposition point location, spigot configuration, beach length, pond level, and freeboard at time of deposition. For capacity surveys: captures survey date, survey method (drone photogrammetry, LiDAR, GPS), total storage capacity, remaining airspace, current storage volume, beach elevation, and pond surface area. SSOT for TSF filling status and remaining life calculations. Tracks operational filling against the approved deposition plan and capacity model, informing raise scheduling and operational planning. Supports GISTM Requirement 12 (management and operations).'
}

  Note: 'Master register of all waste rock dumps, overburden stockpiles, and low-grade ore stockpiles, including full placement event history. For placement events: captures placement date, material type, volume placed, lift number, compaction method, geochemical classification (PAF/NAF), source pit or mining area, and placement equipment.   Note: 'Records waste rock and overburden placement events at waste rock dumps, capturing placement date, material type, volume placed, lift number, compaction method, geochemical classification (PAF/NAF), source pit or mining area, and placement equipment. Tracks the incremental build-up of waste rock dumps against approved designs and waste management plans.'
}

Table tailings.water_balance {
  water_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the water balance record. Primary key for the water balance data product.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Water management costs (pumping, treatment, monitoring) are allocated to the TSF cost centre for environmental compliance cost tracking and water licence fee allocation.']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: Water balance reporting is typically a permit condition for TSF operations. Links operational water management data to regulatory compliance obligations. Required for demonstrating permit compliance a']
  mine_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.mine_site. Business justification: Water balance reports are submitted at mine site level to regulators, aggregating across multiple TSFs. Direct mine_site FK supports site-level water licence compliance reporting and regulatory submis']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to processing.processing_plant. Business justification: TSF water balance must reconcile decant_return_outflow_m3 with source processing plant water usage. Integrated water management requirement for site-wide water balance, licence compliance, and water c']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Process water availability from TSF decant return directly constrains mining production rates in water-scarce operations. Mine planners schedule production based on water balance forecasts; decant vol']
  regulatory_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Water balance calculations are performed to demonstrate compliance with water licence conditions, which are regulatory conditions on the tenement. The water_licence_compliance_status field directly re']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Water balance reports are submitted to regulators as part of water licence compliance obligations (water_licence_compliance_status on water_balance). Linking to regulatory_submission tracks the formal']
  tsf_capacity_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf_capacity_survey. Business justification: Water balance calculations rely on accurate storage capacity data from the most recent volumetric survey. The water_balance table tracks opening/closing storage volumes and freeboard, which are direct']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tailings storage facility for which this water balance is calculated.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Water management programs (decant, treatment, discharge infrastructure) are tracked as WBS-level projects for environmental compliance cost reporting and water licence compliance cost disclosure in re']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Water balance deviations at TSFs trigger maintenance work orders for pump deployment, decant system maintenance, or emergency water management actions. Linking water_balance to work_order provides tra']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the water balance for operational use or regulatory submission. Typically a senior technical authority or site manager.']
  balance_calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the water balance calculation was performed. Supports audit trail and version control for reconciliation purposes.']
  balance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording observations, assumptions, data quality issues, or operational events that affected the water balance during the period. Supports interpretation and future reference.']
  balance_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the water balance calculation period. Defines the reporting interval for inflows, outflows, and storage changes.']
  balance_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the water balance calculation period. Typically daily, weekly, or monthly depending on operational requirements and regulatory reporting frequency.']
  balance_reconciliation_variance_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Difference between calculated storage change and measured storage change, measured in cubic meters. Represents the water balance closure error and indicates measurement accuracy and unaccounted losses or gains.']
  balance_reconciliation_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Balance reconciliation variance expressed as a percentage of total inflow. Provides a normalized measure of water balance accuracy. Acceptable variance thresholds are typically defined in operational procedures.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the water balance record in the approval workflow. Draft indicates initial calculation, preliminary for review, final for operational use, revised for corrections, and approved for regulatory submission.']
  closing_pond_level_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Elevation of the TSF pond water surface at the end of the balance period, measured in meters above a defined datum. Used to calculate closing storage volume and freeboard.']
  closing_storage_volume_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of water stored in the TSF pond at the end of the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Derived from pond level survey and storage-elevation relationship.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the water balance record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  crest_elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Current elevation of the TSF embankment crest at the time of the balance period, measured in meters above a defined datum. Used to calculate freeboard and assess storage capacity.']
  decant_return_outflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of water returned from the TSF to the process plant or water storage via decant structures during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Primary mechanism for water recovery and freeboard management.']
  design_freeboard_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Minimum required freeboard specified in the TSF design for the current operational stage, measured in meters. Used to assess compliance with design criteria and trigger operational responses.']
  evaporation_outflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of water lost from the TSF pond through evaporation during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Calculated from evaporation rate and pond surface area.']
  freeboard_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Vertical distance between the pond water level and the embankment crest at the end of the balance period, measured in meters. Critical safety parameter for TSF operational management and regulatory compliance.']
  groundwater_inflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of groundwater ingress into the TSF pond during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Typically estimated from piezometric monitoring and seepage models.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the water balance record was last updated. Supports version control and change tracking for water management data.']
  opening_pond_level_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Elevation of the TSF pond water surface at the start of the balance period, measured in meters above a defined datum. Used to calculate opening storage volume.']
  opening_storage_volume_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of water stored in the TSF pond at the start of the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Derived from pond level survey and storage-elevation relationship.']
  process_water_inflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of process water discharged to the TSF from mineral processing operations during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Includes tailings slurry water and any additional process water streams.']
  rainfall_inflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of direct rainfall onto the TSF pond surface during the balance period, calculated from rainfall depth and pond area, measured in cubic meters.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who reviewed and validated the water balance calculation. Ensures quality control and technical oversight of water management data.']
  runoff_inflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of surface water runoff from the TSF catchment area entering the pond during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Includes runoff from embankment slopes and upstream catchment if not diverted.']
  seepage_outflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of water lost through seepage from the TSF embankment and foundation during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Estimated from piezometric data, seepage collection systems, and geotechnical models.']
  spillway_discharge_outflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of water discharged through the TSF spillway during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Typically occurs during extreme rainfall events when pond capacity is exceeded.']
  storage_change_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Net change in water storage volume during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Calculated as closing storage minus opening storage. Should reconcile with total inflow minus total outflow.']
  total_inflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Sum of all water inflows to the TSF during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Calculated as process water + rainfall + runoff + groundwater + other inflows.']
  total_outflow_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Sum of all water outflows from the TSF during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Calculated as evaporation + seepage + decant return + spillway discharge + other outflows.']
  water_licence_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the water balance period operations complied with water licence conditions including discharge limits, return water quality, and reporting requirements.']

  Note: 'Periodic water balance calculations and decant operation records for TSFs and associated water management infrastructure. For water balance: captures all inflows (process water, rainfall, runoff, groundwater ingress), outflows (evaporation, seepage, decant return, spillway discharge), storage volumes, pond levels, freeboard, and reconciliation against design assumptions. For decant operations: captures decant event date, decant structure used (penstock, decant tower, pump), volume decanted, water quality at point of decant, destination (process water return, evaporation pond, treatment plant), and compliance with water licence conditions. SSOT for TSF water management including return water quality. Supports water licence compliance, TSF operational planning, and GISTM Requirement 12 (management of water).'
}

  planned_closure_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for commencement of final closure activities for the facility, aligned with Life of Mine (LOM) planning.']
  post_closure_monitoring_period_years varchar [note: 'Duration in years for which post-closure environmental and geotechnical monitoring will be conducted before final relinquishment.']

  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number or identifier assigned by the regulatory authority upon approval of the closure plan.']
  rehabilitation_objectives varchar [note: 'Documented rehabilitation goals including ecosystem restoration targets, water quality outcomes, geotechnical stability criteria, and community land use expectations.']
  review_frequency_years varchar [note: 'Regulatory or corporate mandated frequency in years for periodic review and update of the closure plan.']
  stakeholder_consultation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether community and stakeholder consultation was conducted as part of closure plan development, as required by ICMM GISTM.']
  variance_to_approved_estimate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between the current estimated closure cost and the last regulatory-approved estimate, triggering re-approval if threshold exceeded.']

  Note: 'Master records for mine closure and rehabilitation plans associated with TSFs and waste rock facilities, including closure strategy, final landform design, rehabilitation objectives, progressive rehabilitation milestones, and regulatory approval status. Supports ICMM GISTM Requirements 6 (closure planning) and 9 (financial assurance), IFRS/IAS 37 provisioning, and regulatory financial assurance compliance.'
}

Table tailings.ard_assessment {
  ard_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the acid rock drainage assessment record.']

  drill_hole_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.drill_hole.   anc_kg_caco3_per_tonne decimal(10,2) [note: 'Acid neutralization capacity expressed as kilograms of calcium carbonate equivalent per tonne of material.']

  lead_leachate_mg_per_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Lead concentration in leachate solution expressed in milligrams per liter.']
  management_strategy varchar [note: 'Recommended waste management strategy based on ARD and ML assessment results (e.g., encapsulation, co-disposal with NAF material, underwater storage, lime treatment).']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material tested: waste rock, tailings, overburden, ore reject, or mixed material.']
  maximum_potential_acidity_kg_h2so4_per_tonne decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum potential acidity calculated from total sulfur content, representing worst-case acid generation potential.']
  metal_leaching_risk varchar [note: 'Assessment of metal leaching risk based on leachate test results and metal concentrations.']
  nag_ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'Final pH value from Net Acid Generation test, indicating the acid-generating potential after oxidation.']
  nag_value_kg_h2so4_per_tonne decimal(10,2) [note: 'Net acid generation potential expressed as kilograms of sulfuric acid equivalent per tonne of material.']
  net_neutralization_potential_ratio decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ratio of acid neutralization capacity to maximum potential acidity (ANC/MPA), used to classify ARD risk.']
  paste_ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'pH value measured from paste pH test, indicating acidity or alkalinity of the sample when mixed with water.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the assessment was reviewed and approved by the senior reviewer.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the senior geochemist or competent person who reviewed and approved the assessment.']
  sample_collection_date date [note: 'Date when the physical sample was collected from the field location.']
  sample_depth_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Depth in meters below surface at which the sample was collected, relevant for drill core or pit samples.']
  sample_location_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the specific location within the TSF, waste rock dump, or mine site where the sample was collected.']
  sulfate_sulfur_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Sulfate sulfur content expressed as weight percentage, representing pre-oxidized sulfur that does not contribute to further acid generation.']
  sulfide_sulfur_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Sulfide sulfur content expressed as weight percentage, representing the acid-generating sulfur fraction.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Geochemical test method applied: NAG (Net Acid Generation), ANC (Acid Neutralization Capacity), paste pH, SPLP (Synthetic Precipitation Leaching Procedure), kinetic cell, humidity cell, column leach, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAG|ANC|paste_pH|SPLP|kinetic_cell|humidity_cell|column_leach|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  test_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or file path to the detailed laboratory test report.']
  total_sulfur_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Total sulfur content of the sample expressed as weight percentage, primary indicator of acid generation potential.']
  zinc_leachate_mg_per_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Zinc concentration in leachate solution expressed in milligrams per liter.']

  Note: 'Records Acid Rock Drainage (ARD) and Metal Leaching (ML) geochemical assessments conducted on waste rock and tailings materials, including sample identity, test type (NAG, ANC, paste pH, SPLP leach), net acid generation potential, acid neutralisation capacity, net neutralisation potential ratio (NPR), ARD risk classification (PAF/NAF/UC), and recommended management strategy. Critical for waste characterisation and closure planning.'
}

Table tailings.tsf_capacity_survey {
  tsf_capacity_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the TSF capacity survey record. Primary key.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: TSF capacity surveys are performed under standing surveying services contracts. Linking each survey to its governing contract enables cost tracking against the contract and vendor performance evaluati']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Survey costs (contractor fees, drone surveys, data processing) are charged to the TSF cost centre for operational cost tracking and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: TSF capacity surveys verify compliance with permit-specified storage limits and freeboard requirements. Linking to the governing environmental permit enables direct compliance assessment of survey res']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.regulatory_submission. Business justification: TSF capacity surveys are submitted to regulators as part of dam safety reporting requirements (regulatory_submission_flag, regulatory_submission_date on tsf_capacity_survey). Linking to regulatory_sub']
  tsf_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the tailings storage facility that was surveyed.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_vendor. Business justification: TSF capacity surveys are conducted by licensed surveying firms engaged as procurement vendors. Mining operations track which vendor performed each survey for vendor performance management and regulato']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: TSF capacity surveys are discrete sustaining CAPEX activities tracked under WBS elements for project cost control. Survey costs (surveyor fees, equipment) are allocated to WBS nodes for AISC sustainin']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: TSF capacity surveys are executed under maintenance work orders for survey crew mobilisation, equipment deployment, and cost tracking. Linking tsf_capacity_survey to work_order enables cost capture ag']
  beach_elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average elevation of the tailings beach surface in meters above sea level or site datum, measured during the survey. The beach is the sloped surface of deposited tailings.']
  capacity_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total storage capacity currently utilized, calculated as (current storage volume / total storage capacity) × 100. Key indicator for TSF filling rate monitoring.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Geodetic coordinate reference system used for the survey, including datum and projection (e.g., WGS84 UTM Zone 50S, GDA2020 MGA Zone 51).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  crest_elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Current elevation of the TSF embankment crest in meters above sea level or site datum at the time of survey.']
  current_storage_volume_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current volume of tailings material stored in the TSF in cubic meters at the time of the survey.']
  design_capacity_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original design storage capacity of the TSF in cubic meters at the current stage or raise level, as specified in engineering design documents.']
  estimated_filling_rate_m3_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated average daily rate at which the TSF is being filled with tailings material in cubic meters per day, calculated from historical deposition data.']
  freeboard_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measured freeboard meets or exceeds the minimum required freeboard. True indicates compliance, False indicates non-compliance requiring immediate action.']
  freeboard_m decimal(8,3) [note: 'Vertical distance in meters between the pond water surface elevation and the crest elevation. Critical safety parameter monitored against design requirements and regulatory limits.']
  horizontal_accuracy_m decimal(6,3) [note: 'Horizontal positional accuracy of the survey measurements in meters, representing the precision of X-Y coordinate data.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey record was last modified in the system. Audit trail field.']
  minimum_required_freeboard_m decimal(8,3) [note: 'Minimum freeboard distance in meters required by design specifications and regulatory requirements. Used to assess compliance and trigger operational responses.']
  pond_elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Elevation of the supernatant pond water surface in meters above sea level or site datum, measured during the survey.']
  pond_surface_area_m2 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Surface area of the supernatant pond (water body) on the TSF in square meters at the time of survey. Critical for freeboard and dam safety monitoring.']
  projected_capacity_exhaustion_date date [note: 'Forecasted date when the TSF will reach full capacity based on remaining airspace and estimated filling rate. Critical for raise scheduling and operational planning.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the survey results were submitted to regulatory authorities for compliance reporting.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this survey was submitted to regulatory authorities as part of compliance reporting requirements. True indicates submission was made.']
  remaining_airspace_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Available remaining storage capacity in cubic meters, calculated as the difference between total storage capacity and current storage volume. Critical metric for operational planning and raise scheduling.']
  survey_accuracy_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey accuracy level based on surveying standards and methodology. High accuracy surveys use advanced methods like LiDAR or drone photogrammetry with ground control.']
  survey_date date [note: 'The date on which the volumetric capacity survey was conducted. Principal business event timestamp for this survey record.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'The surveying technology or method used to capture volumetric data for the TSF capacity assessment. Includes drone photogrammetry, LiDAR, GPS survey, total station, bathymetric survey, and terrestrial laser scanning.']
  survey_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, conditions, limitations, or contextual information relevant to the capacity survey.']
  survey_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number or code assigned to this capacity survey for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  survey_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or identifier for the detailed survey report containing methodology, data, calculations, and certifications.']
  survey_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity survey record. Tracks progression from draft through validation and approval stages.']
  surveyor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the licensed surveyor or lead surveyor responsible for conducting and certifying the capacity survey.']
  surveyor_registration_number varchar [note: 'Professional registration or license number of the surveyor who conducted the capacity survey, as required by regulatory authorities.']
  total_storage_capacity_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total volumetric storage capacity of the TSF in cubic meters as determined by the survey, measured from the base to the current crest elevation.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the survey results were validated and approved by the responsible engineer.']
  variance_from_design_m3 decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference in cubic meters between the surveyed total storage capacity and the design capacity. Positive values indicate capacity exceeding design; negative values indicate capacity below design.']
  vertical_accuracy_m decimal(6,3) [note: 'Vertical positional accuracy of the survey measurements in meters, representing the precision of elevation (Z) data. Critical for volume calculations.']

  Note: 'Records periodic volumetric surveys of TSF storage capacity, including survey date, survey method (drone photogrammetry, LiDAR, GPS), total storage capacity, remaining airspace, current storage volume, beach elevation, pond surface area, and freeboard. Tracks TSF filling rate against design capacity and informs raise scheduling and operational planning.'
}

  classification_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, assumptions, limitations, and key considerations documented during the consequence classification assessment process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consequence classification record was first created in the system.']

  environmental_impact_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of potential environmental consequences including water quality, aquatic ecosystems, terrestrial habitat, and protected areas.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility being classified for consequence assessment.']

  inundation_area_km2 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated area of downstream inundation in square kilometers based on dam break analysis for the credible failure scenario.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consequence classification record was last updated in the system.']
  loss_of_life_potential varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of potential loss of life in the event of facility failure, based on population at risk and warning time availability.']
  next_reassessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next consequence classification reassessment, typically triggered by material changes to the facility or downstream conditions.']
  peak_discharge_m3_per_sec decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated peak discharge rate in cubic meters per second at the breach point for the credible failure scenario.']
  population_at_risk varchar [note: 'Estimated number of people potentially affected in the downstream impact zone in the event of facility failure. Critical factor in determining consequence category.']

  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned by the regulatory authority for the consequence classification submission and approval.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the consequence classification assessment was submitted to the regulatory authority for review.']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed consequence classification report and supporting technical documentation including dam break studies and inundation mapping.']
  warning_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated time available for downstream warning and evacuation from detection of failure precursors to breach occurrence.']

  Note: 'Records the consequence classification assessments for TSFs and waste rock facilities, capturing classification category (Extreme, Very High, High, Significant, Low), population at risk, downstream infrastructure, failure mode scenario, assessment date, assessor credentials, and regulatory acceptance status. Drives dam safety review frequency and design standards per ANCOLD and ICMM GISTM.'
}
