  configuration_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for this pricing configuration template, used in offtake agreements and commercial contracts.']
  configuration_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the pricing configuration template (e.g., \'IODEX 62% Fe CFR China Standard\', \'LME Copper Grade A Premium\').']
  configuration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing configuration template.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pricing configuration record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this pricing configuration becomes active and applicable to new contracts or shipments.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this pricing configuration remains active. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) defining the delivery basis and responsibility transfer point (e.g., FOB - Free on Board, CIF - Cost Insurance and Freight, CFR - Cost and Freight). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pricing configuration record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special conditions, exceptions, or clarifications for this pricing configuration (e.g., \'Applies only to Pilbara Blend Fines\', \'Moisture penalty waived for Q1 2024\').']
  penalty_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total penalty amount that can be applied for this parameter, preventing excessive price reductions.']
  penalty_rate_per_unit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Price adjustment rate applied per unit below the base reference grade (e.g., -0.50 USD/dmt per 0.1% Fe below 62.0%).']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for pricing (e.g., USD/dmt, USD/t, USD/lb, USD/kg). Defines the denominator for price calculations.']
  pricing_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the pricing is denominated (e.g., USD, AUD, CNY).']
  provisional_pricing_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the estimated final price paid provisionally at shipment (typically 80-90%). Remainder settled after final assay and quotational period completion.']
  quotational_period_definition varchar [note: 'Definition of the quotational period used for pricing (e.g., \'Month of Bill of Lading\', \'Month Prior to Shipment\', \'Average of M+1 and M+2\'). Determines which market prices apply to a shipment.']
  rejection_limit_lower decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the quality parameter. Material below this threshold may be rejected or subject to renegotiation.']
  rejection_limit_upper decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the quality parameter (for deleterious elements). Material above this threshold may be rejected.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this pricing configuration, incremented when the template is revised or superseded.']

  Note: 'Unified commercial pricing configuration template for each saleable product, combining pricing basis and penalty/bonus adjustment schedules. Captures: benchmark price index reference (Platts IODEX 62% Fe CFR China, LME Copper Grade A, Newcastle Coal Index, Fastmarkets Lithium Hydroxide CIF Asia, LME Nickel), pricing currency (USD, AUD, CNY), price unit (USD/dmt, USD/t, USD/lb), quotational period definition, provisional pricing percentage, Incoterms (FOB, CIF, CFR), AND per-parameter penalty/bonus rates (base reference grade, penalty rate per unit below base, bonus rate per unit above base, penalty/bonus caps, rejection limits). This is a product-level pricing TEMPLATE — actual transactional pricing and invoice calculations belong to the sales domain. Directly referenced by offtake agreements to determine how quality deviations translate to price adjustments.'
}

  delivery_destination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_destination. Business justification: Export certifications (phytosanitary, radiation, IMSBC compliance) are often destination-specific (e.g., China AQSIQ for iron ore, Japan radiation certificates). Required for logistics planning, custo']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Certification cost financial posting: certification fees (ISO audits, environmental certification body fees) post to specific GL accounts. Mining finance teams require GL account assignment on certifi']
  regulatory_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Product certifications (environmental, quality, export) are often obtained specifically to satisfy regulatory conditions attached to a tenement licence. Linking enables compliance teams to demonstrate']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the saleable mineral product (iron ore fines, lump, copper concentrate, thermal coal, lithium hydroxide, nickel matte) that this certification applies to.']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Export certifications and compliance certificates reference source tenement for provenance verification, conflict minerals compliance, and responsible sourcing standards. Required for ESG reporting an']
  applicable_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The specific legal or regulatory jurisdiction under which this certification is governed. May include sub-national regions, trade zones, or regulatory frameworks (e.g., EU REACH, China AQSIQ, Korea MOE).']
  applicable_market varchar [note: 'The geographic market, country, or jurisdiction where this certification is required and valid. Examples: European Union, China, South Korea, Japan, United States. Use 3-letter ISO country codes where applicable (e.g., CHN, KOR, JPN, USA) or regional identifiers (e.g., EU).']
  audit_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification audit or inspection was conducted by the issuing authority or third-party auditor. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual auditor or audit firm that conducted the certification assessment.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate or registration number issued by the certifying authority. This is the externally-known identifier for the certification.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'A description of the scope of the certification, including what aspects of the product, process, or operation are covered. Examples: product composition, manufacturing process, environmental impact, quality management system, occupational health and safety.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the certification. Active: currently valid and in force. Expired: validity period has ended. Suspended: temporarily not valid. Pending: application submitted, awaiting approval. Revoked: cancelled by authority. Renewed: re-issued after expiry.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'The category or type of certification. Examples include REACH registration (EU chemical regulation), China GB (Guobiao) standard approval, Korean KS certification, Japanese JIS (Japanese Industrial Standards) approval, hazardous goods classification certificate (IMDG, ADR, IATA), ISO 9001 quality management, ISO 14001 environmental management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reach_registration|china_gb_standard|korean_ks_certification|japanese_jis_approval|hazardous_goods_classification|iso_9001|iso_14001 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'The specific industry standard, regulatory code, or technical specification that the product must comply with under this certification. Examples: GB/T 20123 (China), KS M ISO 9001 (Korea), JIS M 8100 (Japan), REACH Annex XVII, ISO 14001:2015.']
  cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost incurred to obtain or renew this certification, including application fees, audit fees, testing fees, and administrative costs. Expressed in the reporting currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the certification cost. Examples: USD, AUD, EUR, CNY.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the certification becomes valid and the product can be legally sold in the applicable market. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification expires and is no longer valid. Nullable for certifications with indefinite validity. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'The hazardous goods classification code assigned to the product for transport and handling. Examples: UN number (UN 3264 for corrosive liquids), IMDG class (Class 8 Corrosive), ADR class, IATA dangerous goods code. Critical for lithium products and concentrates.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification was officially issued by the authority. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'The name of the regulatory body, government agency, or certification organization that issued the certificate. Examples: European Chemicals Agency (ECHA), Standardization Administration of China (SAC), Korean Agency for Technology and Standards (KATS), Japanese Standards Association (JSA), International Maritime Organization (IMO).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this certification record was last modified or updated in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'The date by which the certification must be renewed to maintain validity. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Null if renewal is not required.']
  product_grade_covered varchar [note: 'The specific product grade, specification, or variant that this certification covers. For example, battery-grade lithium hydroxide monohydrate (>56.5% LiOH), copper concentrate (>25% Cu), iron ore fines (Fe 62% min). Critical for products with multiple grades.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the certification. Examples: conditional approval notes, restrictions, special handling requirements, or audit findings.']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'The frequency in months at which the certification must be renewed. Examples: 12 (annual), 24 (biennial), 36 (triennial). Null if renewal is not required.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification requires periodic renewal. True if renewal is required, False if the certification is indefinite or one-time.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The name of the internal business unit, department, or individual responsible for managing and maintaining this certification. Examples: Product Compliance Team, HSE Department, Quality Assurance Manager.']
  supporting_documentation_reference varchar [note: 'A reference or file path to the supporting documentation, certificate files, audit reports, or compliance records associated with this certification. May be a document management system identifier or URL.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'The four-digit UN number assigned to hazardous materials for international transport. Format: UN followed by 4 digits (e.g., UN3264). Applicable to hazardous mineral products.']

  Note: 'Tracks all product quality certifications, compliance approvals, and regulatory registrations required for each saleable product to be sold in specific markets. Includes certification type (REACH registration, China GB standard approval, Korean KS certification, Japanese JIS approval, hazardous goods classification certificate), issuing authority, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, applicable market/jurisdiction, and certification status. Critical for lithium battery-grade products and hazardous concentrate exports.'
}

Table product.blend {
  blend_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product blend recipe. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Blending operations occur at specific processing facilities tracked as cost centres. Required for production cost allocation, AISC calculation, and tracking blending costs against budget. Reusing exis']
  customer_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.counterparty. Business justification: Custom blends are created to meet individual customer specifications (e.g., blending ore sources to achieve contractual Fe/SiO2 ratios). Supports blend approval workflows, customer acceptance testing,']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Blending cost GL posting: blending operations incur processing costs (labour, equipment, stockpile handling) that post to specific GL accounts. Mining finance requires GL account on blend activities f']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific facility or location where this blend is executed (e.g., ROM Pad 3, Port Stockpile A, Train Loadout 2).']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Blend production revenue and cost attribution: blending operations produce saleable product generating revenue attributed to a profit centre for segment reporting. Mining operations require profit cen']
  saleable_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the saleable product that this blend produces (e.g., iron ore fines 62% Fe, copper concentrate, thermal coal).']
  source_orebody_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Product blends target material from specific orebodies to achieve grade specifications. Blend recipes reference source orebodies for quality prediction, optimization algorithms, and production plannin']
  source_tenement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Blended products must be traceable to their source tenement for royalty apportionment, tenement compliance reporting, and regulatory returns. A mining operations expert expects blend-to-tenement trace']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: A blend recipe targets a specific product specification — the blend is designed to meet the quality parameters defined in a specification. blend.target_specification_code is a STRING that denormalizes']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Role or position authorized to approve this blend recipe (e.g., Chief Metallurgist, Mine Planning Manager, Technical Services Superintendent).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this blend recipe was formally approved for production use.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this blend recipe for production use.']
  blend_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code identifying this blend recipe, used across mine planning, processing, and logistics systems.']
  blend_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the blend recipe, typically reflecting target grade or customer specification (e.g., \'62% Fe Lump Blend\', \'Premium Thermal Coal Blend\').']
  blend_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the blend recipe: draft (under development), approved (ready for use), active (currently in production), suspended (temporarily inactive), retired (no longer used).']
  blend_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the blend by commodity type: ore_blend (iron ore, copper ore), coal_blend (thermal, metallurgical), concentrate_blend (processed mineral concentrate), custom_blend (customer-specific recipe).']
  blending_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility where blending occurs: rom_pad (Run of Mine stockpile pad), stockpile (intermediate stockpile), train_loadout (rail loading facility), port_stockpile (port storage yard), ship_loader (vessel loading point).']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Primary mineral commodity that this blend produces: iron_ore, copper, coal (thermal/metallurgical), lithium, nickel, gold, zinc, lead. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron_ore|copper|coal|lithium|nickel|gold|zinc|lead — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  complexity_rating varchar [note: 'Operational complexity classification: simple (2-3 components, wide tolerance), moderate (3-4 components, standard tolerance), complex (5+ components, tight tolerance), advanced (requires real-time optimization and quality prediction).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this blend recipe record was first created in the system.']
  customer_specification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code to the customer contract or specification document that defines the quality requirements for this blend. Links blend to commercial agreements.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this blend recipe becomes valid and can be used in production operations.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this blend recipe remains valid. Nullable for open-ended blends. Used for seasonal blends or customer-specific campaigns.']
  environmental_classification varchar [note: 'Environmental handling requirements for this blend: standard (normal conditions), dust_sensitive (requires dust suppression), moisture_sensitive (requires weather protection), temperature_controlled (requires climate control).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this blend recipe record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  maximum_component_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of source products allowed in this blend (typically 3-8). Limits operational complexity and maintains traceability.']
  minimum_component_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of source products required to create this blend (typically 2-5). Ensures blend complexity and quality consistency.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this blend recipe record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, historical performance observations, or customer-specific requirements.']
  optimization_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective for this blend: maximize_grade (highest quality), minimize_cost (lowest production cost), meet_specification (exact customer requirement), maximize_recovery (best yield), balance_inventory (stockpile management).']
  quality_assurance_protocol varchar [note: 'Level of quality testing and validation required: standard (routine sampling), enhanced (increased sampling frequency), premium (continuous monitoring), customer_specific (per contract requirements).']
  quality_prediction_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code to the predictive analytics model used to forecast blend quality outcomes based on source material properties. Supports real-time blend optimization.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for quality sampling and assay testing: per_batch (every blend batch), per_shift (8-12 hour intervals), per_day (daily composite), continuous (real-time monitoring), per_shipment (before dispatch).']
  target_alumina_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target alumina content percentage for iron ore and mineral blends. Impacts smelting efficiency (typical range 1-4%).']
  target_ash_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target ash content percentage for coal blends. Lower ash content increases energy value and reduces waste (typical range 8-25%).']
  target_energy_content_kcal_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target calorific value for coal blends measured in kilocalories per kilogram. Critical for thermal coal pricing and power generation efficiency (typical range 4500-7000 kcal/kg).']
  target_fe_grade_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target iron content percentage for iron ore blends, typically 58-67% Fe. Critical for meeting customer specifications and pricing.']
  target_moisture_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target moisture content percentage for the blended product. Critical for shipping weight calculations and product quality (typical range 2-10%).']
  target_phosphorus_percent decimal(5,3) [note: 'Target phosphorus content percentage for iron ore blends. Low phosphorus is critical for high-grade steel production (typical range 0.02-0.10%).']
  target_silica_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target silica content percentage for iron ore and mineral blends. Lower silica is preferred for steel-making (typical range 2-8%).']
  tolerance_range_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowable deviation from target specification expressed as percentage (e.g., ±2% for Fe grade). Defines acceptable quality variance for the blend.']

  Note: 'Approved blending recipe that combines two or more source products to produce a target blended product meeting a defined specification. Captures blend identifier, target saleable product, target specification reference, blending location (ROM pad, train load-out, port stockpile, ship-loader feed), blend tolerance range, quality prediction model reference, and approval status. Critical for iron ore operations where multiple ore sources are blended to meet 62% Fe specification, and for coal operations blending energy and ash content. Supports mine-to-port blend optimisation and real-time blend tracking.'
}

Table product.commodity_grade_scope {
  commodity_grade_scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the commodity_grade_scope association']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the commodity whose quality specification this record contributes to.']
  grade_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the grade parameter that applies to this commodity.']
  applicable_commodity_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of mineral commodities to which this parameter applies (e.g., iron_ore, copper_concentrate, thermal_coal, metallurgical_coal, lithium_hydroxide, lithium_carbonate, nickel_matte, nickel_concentrate, gold_dore). Defines the product catalog scope where this parameter is relevant for quality specification. [Moved from grade_parameter: This comma-separated text field on grade_parameter is a denormalized encoding of the M:N relationship. It must be removed and replaced by proper FK records in commodity_grade_scope, which provides referential integrity, queryability, and the ability to store commodity-specific attributes (ranges, contractual flags, penalty/bonus flags) per combination.]']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this grade parameter became applicable to this commodity. Supports versioning of the commodity quality specification over time.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this grade parameter ceased to apply to this commodity. Null indicates currently active applicability.']
  is_bonus_element boolean [note: 'Indicates whether exceeding specified thresholds for this parameter results in commercial price bonuses for this specific commodity. Bonus applicability is commodity-specific.']
  is_contractual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grade parameter is included in sales contracts and commercial specifications for this specific commodity. A parameter may be contractual for coal but informational-only for lithium.']
  is_penalty_element boolean [note: 'Indicates whether exceeding specified limits for this parameter results in commercial price penalties for this specific commodity. Penalty applicability is commodity-specific.']
  scope_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this commodity-parameter applicability record. Allows parameters to be retired from a commodity\'s scope without deleting the historical record.']
  typical_range_maximum decimal [note: 'Upper bound of the typical value range for this grade parameter as observed specifically for this commodity. Overrides the generic typical_range_maximum on grade_parameter with a commodity-specific value.']
  typical_range_minimum decimal [note: 'Lower bound of the typical value range for this grade parameter as observed specifically for this commodity. Overrides the generic typical_range_minimum on grade_parameter with a commodity-specific value.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formally managed applicability contract between a commodity and a grade parameter. It captures which quality parameters govern each commodity type, along with the commodity-specific specification boundaries, contractual inclusion status, and commercial penalty/bonus flags that exist only in the context of a specific commodity-parameter combination. Each record defines one quality dimension of one commodity\'s specification envelope, actively maintained by metallurgists and commercial teams.. Existence Justification: In mining operations, each commodity type (iron ore, copper, coal) is governed by a formally managed set of quality parameters — metallurgists and commercial teams explicitly define which grade parameters apply to each commodity, along with commodity-specific ranges, contractual status, and penalty/bonus flags. This is not derivable from transactional data; it is a controlled reference catalog actively maintained by technical and commercial teams. A single grade parameter (e.g., moisture H2O) applies to multiple commodities, and a single commodity (e.g., iron ore) is governed by many grade parameters — making this a genuine operational M:N.'
}

Table tenement.tenement {
  tenement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the tenement record. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Tenements are primary cost objects in mining operations. Each tenement is assigned to a cost centre for operational cost tracking, AISC calculation, and unit cost reporting. Essential for monthly mana']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Tenements are held by specific legal entities — this is a fundamental regulatory requirement (mining licences are granted to legal entities). Finance teams need legal entity linkage for consolidation ']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Tenements are evaluated as profit-generating assets. Assignment to profit centres enables segment P&L reporting, asset-level profitability analysis, and investment decision support. Required for IFRS ']
  rehabilitation_provision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rehabilitation_provision. Business justification: Rehabilitation provisions under IFRS/AASB 137 are calculated per tenement area under mining legislation. Finance teams must link each tenement to its rehabilitation provision for closure cost estimati']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Tenements are represented as WBS elements in mining ERP systems for IFRS 6 exploration expenditure capitalisation. Finance teams link tenements to WBS for exploration cost tracking, impairment testing']
  annual_expenditure_commitment decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum annual expenditure amount (in local currency) that must be spent on exploration or development activities to maintain the tenement in good standing. Failure to meet this commitment may result in forfeiture.']
  application_date date [note: 'The date on which the tenement application was submitted to the regulatory authority. Establishes priority in jurisdictions with first-in-time rules.']
  area_hectares decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total surface area of the tenement measured in hectares. Defines the spatial extent of the mineral rights.']
  centroid_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The latitude coordinate of the tenement centroid in decimal degrees. Used for approximate location reference and spatial queries.']
  centroid_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The longitude coordinate of the tenement centroid in decimal degrees. Used for approximate location reference and spatial queries.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'The coordinate reference system (CRS) or projection used for the tenement boundary (e.g., \'GDA2020 MGA Zone 50\', \'NAD83 UTM Zone 11N\'). Critical for accurate spatial representation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the tenement is located (e.g., AUS, CAN, USA, ZAF).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tenement record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  environmental_conditions varchar [note: 'Summary of key environmental conditions or restrictions imposed on the tenement by the regulatory authority (e.g., \'No clearing within 100m of watercourses\', \'Seasonal access restrictions during breeding season\'). Free-text field for recording compliance obligations.']
  expenditure_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the annual expenditure commitment (e.g., AUD, CAD, USD, ZAR).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the tenement licence term ends unless renewed. Critical for tracking renewal obligations and avoiding forfeiture.']
  gis_boundary_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the GIS spatial dataset defining the tenement boundary coordinates. Enables integration with GIS systems for spatial analysis and mapping.']
  grant_date date [note: 'The date on which the tenement was officially granted by the regulatory authority, marking the commencement of the licence term.']
  granted_commodities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of minerals or commodities for which the tenement grants exploration or extraction rights (e.g., \'Iron Ore, Gold, Copper\', \'Lithium, Nickel\'). Defines the scope of permitted mineral activities.']
  heritage_clearance_status varchar [note: 'The status of cultural heritage clearance for the tenement. Not Required: no heritage survey needed; Cleared: heritage survey completed with no restrictions; Survey Required: heritage assessment not yet conducted; Restricted Areas Identified: heritage sites present requiring protection; Pending: clearance process underway.']
  holder_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage ownership interest held by the registered holder in this tenement. Relevant for joint ventures or partial interests. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  joint_venture_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenement is held under a joint venture arrangement with one or more partners. True if joint venture; False if sole ownership.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The state, province, or territory in which the tenement is registered and governed (e.g., \'Western Australia\', \'Queensland\', \'British Columbia\'). Determines the applicable mining legislation and regulatory authority.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent physical or regulatory inspection of the tenement. Used to track compliance monitoring and site visit schedules.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tenement record was last updated in the system. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  licence_type varchar [note: 'The category of mineral tenement defining the permitted activities and rights. Exploration Licence (EL) allows exploration activities; Mining Lease (ML) permits extraction; Prospecting Licence (PL) for small-scale exploration; Retention Licence (RL) to retain discovered resources not yet economically viable; Miscellaneous Licence for ancillary purposes.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the tenement in its regulatory lifecycle. Application: submitted but not yet granted; Granted: approved and active; Active: in good standing with all obligations met; Suspended: temporarily inactive due to regulatory or operational reasons; Surrendered: voluntarily relinquished by holder; Expired: lapsed due to term completion; Forfeited: revoked by authority for non-compliance; Renewal Pending: application for extension submitted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: application|granted|active|suspended|surrendered|expired|forfeited|renewal_pending — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  native_title_status varchar [note: 'The status of native title considerations for the tenement. Not Applicable: jurisdiction does not recognize native title; Determined: native title has been legally determined; Registered Claim: a native title claim is registered over the area; Agreement in Place: native title agreement (e.g., ILUA) executed; Negotiation Required: consultation or agreement process underway.']
  next_reporting_date date [note: 'The date by which the next statutory report (e.g., annual expenditure report, exploration report) must be submitted to the regulatory authority. Critical for compliance tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording additional information, special conditions, historical context, or operational notes relevant to the tenement management.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The name of the government department or agency responsible for administering the tenement (e.g., \'Department of Mines, Industry Regulation and Safety\', \'Ministry of Energy and Mines\').']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date by which a renewal application must be submitted to extend the tenement term. Nullable for tenements not yet requiring renewal.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage royalty rate payable on production from the tenement, either to the government or to a third-party royalty holder. Nullable if no royalty applies.']
  royalty_type varchar [note: 'The type of royalty obligation applicable to the tenement. Government: statutory royalty to state; Private: royalty to third party; Net Smelter Return (NSR): based on net proceeds after smelting; Gross Revenue: based on gross sales; Profit Based: based on net profit.']
  statutory_identifier varchar [note: 'The official government-issued unique identifier for the tenement as registered with the mining authority (e.g., EL12345, ML67890, PL54321). This is the externally-known business identifier used in all regulatory correspondence and public registers.']
  strategic_priority varchar [note: 'Internal classification of the tenement\'s strategic importance to the company\'s portfolio. Tier 1: highest priority, active development or production; Tier 2: medium priority, advanced exploration; Tier 3: early-stage or prospective; Non-Core: retained but not strategic; Divestment Candidate: targeted for sale or surrender.']
  surface_rights_status varchar [note: 'The status of surface land access rights for the tenement. Owned: company owns the surface land; Leased: surface land leased from owner; Access Agreement: formal access agreement in place; Negotiation Required: access rights being negotiated; Restricted: surface access limited or denied.']
  tenement_name varchar [note: 'The common or project name assigned to the tenement for internal reference and communication (e.g., \'Iron Ridge North\', \'Copper Creek Prospect\').']

  Note: 'Master register of all mineral tenements held or applied for by the company, including exploration licences, mining leases, prospecting licences, and retention licences. SSOT for tenement identity, licence type, status, jurisdiction, granted commodities, area (hectares), grant/expiry dates, and lifecycle status (application, granted, suspended, surrendered, expired, forfeited). Each record represents one tenement with its unique statutory identifier.'
}

Table tenement.boundary {
  boundary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tenement boundary record. Primary key for the tenement boundary data product.']
  heritage_clearance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.heritage_clearance. Business justification: boundary.heritage_area_overlap_flag indicates that a boundary polygon overlaps with a heritage area requiring clearance. Adding heritage_clearance_id links the specific boundary version to the heritag']
  native_title_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.native_title_agreement. Business justification: boundary.native_title_overlap_flag indicates that a boundary polygon overlaps with a native title claim or determination area. Adding native_title_agreement_id links the specific boundary version to t']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent tenement for which this boundary is defined. Links to the tenement master record.']
  accuracy_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the boundary accuracy. Survey accurate for professionally surveyed boundaries, cadastral for official land registry boundaries, digitized for boundaries traced from maps, approximate for estimated boundaries.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Business or regulatory reason for the boundary change. Examples include partial relinquishment, area reduction, survey correction, native title agreement, or regulatory directive.']
  area_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the tenement area. Planar for flat-earth approximation, geodesic for spherical earth, ellipsoidal for precise WGS84 calculations, or surveyed for official cadastral measurements.']
  area_hectares decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total area of the tenement boundary in hectares. Calculated from the polygon geometry. Used for expenditure commitment calculations and regulatory reporting.']
  area_square_kilometers decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total area of the tenement boundary in square kilometers. Alternative area measurement for reporting and analysis.']
  boundary_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the boundary definition. Indicates whether the boundary is in draft, under regulatory review, officially approved, or has been superseded by a newer version.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, clarifications, or context about the boundary definition. May include survey notes, regulatory correspondence references, or operational considerations.']
  coordinate_reference_system varchar [note: 'The spatial reference system used for the boundary coordinates. Typically an EPSG code (e.g., EPSG:4326 for WGS84, EPSG:28350 for GDA94 MGA Zone 50). Critical for accurate spatial alignment and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this boundary record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or document from which the boundary geometry was obtained. Examples include regulatory portal, survey plan, GPS field capture, or digitized from historical maps.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this boundary definition becomes legally effective. Marks the start of the boundary version validity period.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this boundary definition remains valid. Null for the current active boundary. Populated when a new boundary version supersedes this one.']
  environmental_overlay_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the tenement boundary intersects with environmental protection zones, national parks, or conservation areas. Impacts permitting and operational constraints.']
  geometry_wkb varchar [note: 'Spatial polygon geometry of the tenement boundary in Well-Known Binary format. Binary representation for efficient storage and processing in GIS systems.']
  geometry_wkt varchar [note: 'Spatial polygon geometry of the tenement boundary in Well-Known Text format. Represents the precise geographic coordinates defining the tenement perimeter. Used for GIS integration and spatial analysis.']
  gis_layer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the GIS layer or feature class where this boundary is stored in the corporate GIS system. Supports integration with spatial data platforms.']
  heritage_area_overlap_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the tenement boundary intersects with registered heritage sites or protected cultural areas. Triggers heritage clearance requirements.']
  is_current_boundary boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the current active boundary for the tenement. True for the latest approved version, false for historical versions.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this boundary record. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this boundary record was last updated in the system. Tracks the most recent change for audit and synchronization purposes.']
  map_sheet_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the topographic map sheet or cadastral map on which the tenement boundary is depicted. Used for regulatory submissions and field operations.']
  native_title_overlap_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the tenement boundary intersects with registered native title claims or determinations. Used for heritage and consultation planning.']
  perimeter_meters decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total perimeter length of the tenement boundary in meters. Calculated from the polygon geometry. Used for boundary management and overlap analysis.']
  positional_accuracy_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated positional accuracy of the boundary coordinates in meters. Represents the maximum expected deviation from true ground position. Critical for spatial analysis and overlap detection.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the boundary definition was officially approved by the mining regulator or land authority. Establishes the legal effective date of the boundary.']
  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number or document identifier for the regulatory approval of this boundary. Used for compliance tracking and audit purposes.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the boundary definition was submitted to the regulatory authority for approval. Used for tracking regulatory processing timelines.']
  survey_date date [note: 'Date when the boundary survey was conducted. Critical for establishing the official boundary definition and regulatory compliance.']
  survey_datum varchar [note: 'The geodetic datum used for the boundary survey. Common values include GDA94 (Geocentric Datum of Australia 1994), GDA2020, WGS84. Ensures consistency with regulatory mapping standards.']
  survey_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number or identifier for the survey plan document lodged with the regulatory authority. Links to the formal cadastral record.']
  surveyor_licence_number varchar [note: 'Professional licence number of the surveyor who certified the boundary. Required for regulatory compliance and validation of survey accuracy.']
  surveyor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the licensed surveyor or surveying firm that conducted the boundary survey. Required for regulatory submissions and audit trail.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this boundary definition. Increments with each boundary amendment or survey update. Supports tracking of boundary changes over the tenement lifecycle.']
  vertex_count varchar [note: 'Number of vertices (corner points) defining the boundary polygon. Indicates the complexity of the boundary shape.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who created this boundary record in the system. Supports audit trail and data governance.']

  Note: 'GIS spatial boundary definition for each tenement, storing polygon geometry (WKT/WKB), coordinate reference system (CRS), survey datum, area calculation method, and version history of boundary amendments. Supports GIS integration for overlap analysis, native title intersection, and regulatory mapping submissions. One tenement may have multiple boundary versions over its lifecycle.'
}

Table tenement.expenditure_commitment {
  expenditure_commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the expenditure commitment record. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Statutory expenditure commitments must be budgeted and tracked against cost centres for budget vs actual variance reporting. Essential for regulatory compliance reporting and ensuring commitments are ']
  general_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Expenditure commitments require GL account coding for financial reporting and commitment management in mining ERP. Finance teams post commitment accruals to specific GL accounts (ifrs6_exploration_fla']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Statutory expenditure commitments on mining tenements are fulfilled through actual procurement spend. Mining companies must demonstrate regulatory compliance by linking committed amounts to actual POs']
  regulatory_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Expenditure commitments in mining are frequently mandated by specific regulatory conditions attached to a tenement (e.g., minimum work program requirements, environmental rehabilitation bonds). Linkin']
  tenement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenement (exploration licence or mining lease) to which this expenditure commitment applies.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Tenement expenditure commitments (minimum spend conditions) are tracked against WBS elements in mining ERP for IFRS 6 exploration capitalisation. Finance teams require WBS coding on expenditure commit']
  actual_expenditure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual expenditure incurred to date against this commitment during the reporting period. Updated as expenditure is recorded and approved.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the mining authority approved or accepted the expenditure report and confirmed compliance. Null if not yet approved or if under review.']
  carry_forward_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of surplus expenditure from this reporting period that is eligible to be carried forward to offset future period commitments, where permitted by regulation.']
  carry_forward_expiry_date date [note: 'The date by which any carry-forward expenditure credit must be utilized, after which it expires and can no longer be applied to future commitments.']
  commitment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned by the mining authority or internal system to uniquely identify this expenditure commitment obligation.']
  commitment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the expenditure commitment based on its origin and nature. Statutory commitments are mandated by regulation, voluntary commitments are self-imposed, negotiated commitments result from agreements with authorities.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The statutory minimum expenditure amount that must be spent on exploration or mining activities within the reporting period to maintain the tenement in good standing. Expressed in the currency of the jurisdiction.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the expenditure commitment. Indicates whether the commitment has been met, is at risk of non-compliance, or has been granted an exemption or carry-forward arrangement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this expenditure commitment record was first created in the system. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the commitment and expenditure amounts are denominated (e.g., AUD, USD, CAD, ZAR).']
  exemption_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an exemption from the expenditure commitment has been granted by the mining authority for this reporting period. True if exemption granted, False otherwise.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'The reason or justification provided for the exemption from the expenditure commitment, if an exemption was granted. May reference force majeure, regulatory delays, or other circumstances.']
  expenditure_category varchar [note: 'The category of expenditure that qualifies toward meeting the commitment. Different tenement types and jurisdictions may have specific rules about what expenditure categories are eligible.']
  forfeiture_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenement is at risk of forfeiture due to non-compliance with this expenditure commitment. True if forfeiture risk exists, False otherwise.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this expenditure commitment record was last modified. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context regarding this expenditure commitment, including explanations of variances, special circumstances, or correspondence with authorities.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial penalty imposed by the mining authority for non-compliance with the expenditure commitment, if applicable. Null if no penalty assessed.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the reporting period for which this expenditure commitment applies. Defines the deadline by which committed expenditure must be incurred and reported.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the reporting period for which this expenditure commitment applies. Typically aligned with the tenement grant anniversary or regulatory year.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the internal business unit, cost centre, or individual responsible for ensuring this expenditure commitment is met and reported.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the expenditure report was submitted to the mining authority. Null if not yet submitted.']
  submission_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the expenditure report must be submitted to the mining authority to demonstrate compliance with the commitment. Typically falls after the reporting period end date.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between committed amount and actual expenditure amount. Positive values indicate surplus (over-expenditure), negative values indicate shortfall (under-expenditure).']

  Note: 'Tracks the statutory minimum expenditure obligations attached to each tenement for each reporting period. Records the committed amount, actual expenditure to date, shortfall or surplus, reporting period start/end dates, and compliance status. Supports regulatory reporting to mining authorities and internal CAPEX/OPEX planning. Failure to meet commitments can result in tenement forfeiture.'
}

Tenement managers must link the renewal obligation to ']
